markdown:
Beware the haunting sobs of the attic whisperer, for they carry the pained wrath of an abandoned child who perished due to the neglect or absence of their caretakers. Animated by loneliness, the embittered spirit binds itself to the material world in a body made of bits and oddments of a lost childhood. To give themselves the semblance of a head, they top their patchwork bodies with a small animal's skull. Attic whisperers most frequently lurk in old infirmaries, orphanages, and other such institutions where children were forgotten, and they lay dormant for decades in hopes they might one day find a playmate to ease their eternal loneliness. When they sense the living, attic whisperers attempt to lure them into their clutches by calling out to them using the voice of a small child. Though attic whisperers intend only to play with those they encounter, they drain the breath and voice from living creatures as their worst impulses take over. **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Attic Whisperer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4090) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 47 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=20) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +11, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +0 **Dex** +5 **Con** +0 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +8 **Ref** +13 **Will** +12 **HP** 60 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Aura of Sobs** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 10 feet. An attic whisperer enshrouds itself in a tapestry of stolen voices. Each living creature that enters or starts their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 19 Will save or the unnerving, bitter sobs render them distraught and they become [stupefied 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) for as long as they remain within the aura. A creature that succeeds is temporarily immune for 1 hour. The attic whisperer can activate or deactivate the aura with a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait.
**Whispered Despair** **Trigger** A creature with an active emotion effect enters an attic whisperer's aura of sobs; **Effect** The attic whisperer attempts to counteract the emotion effect, with a counteract modifier of +13. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** jaws +14 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602)), **Damage** 2d8 piercing plus steal breath **Melee** bony hand ([agile +12](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d10 void plus steal voice **Steal Breath** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The attic whisperer siphons the breath from living creatures, sapping their strength. A living creature hit by a jaws Strike must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is enfeebled 1 for 24 hours and [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73).
**Critical Failure** The target is enfeebled 1 for 24 hours, fatigued, and falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95). **Steal Voice** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When an attic whisperer hits a living creature with a bony hand Strike, it tries to pull the victim's voice into its aura. The victim must attempt a DC 21 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target's voice is weak for 1 minute. Anytime it attempts to Cast a Spell or use an action that has the [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) trait, it must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the action is lost.
**Failure** The target loses the ability to speak for 1 hour, until the curse is removed, or until the attic whisperer is destroyed, whichever comes first. During this time, the attic whisperer can perfectly mimic the target's voice, and the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against that attic whisperer's aura of sobs.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effects last until the attic whisperer is destroyed or the curse is removed.
Varisian Nursery Rhyme _Is the attic whispering?
Are we safe below?
Do you think he's listening?
Is that his shadow? Do you hear him waking,
Up above the stairs?
Do you hear him weeping?
Is he really there? Can you say “I'm speaking?”
Are you saying naught?
Is it you who's weeping?
Is it you he's caught?_
name: Attic Whisperer

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Stalker Automaton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4091)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Stalker automatons house the minds of skilled hunters, scouts, and assassins in bodies patterned after the great hunters of the natural world. Stalkers generally have the shape of wolves, large cats, and other predatory creatures. The ancient artificers improved upon their natural abilities, adding in specialized systems of arcane camouflage and movement. Surviving stalkers still pride themselves as hunters without peer, even as they've adapted to their animalistic forms.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 25

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Automaton_Stalker.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Stalker Automaton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4091)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=683" />
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Automaton" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=398" />
<trait label="Construct" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=563" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 48

**Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59)

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); one other language the stalker knew in life (usually Jistkan); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +12, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +4

**Dex** +5

**Con** +3

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +1
</row>

**Adaptive Camouflage** The stalker's magically treated metal frame constantly shifts and changes to match their surroundings. The stalker does not need [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=459) or concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +10 

**Ref** +14 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 65

</row>

**Immunities**
[bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)

**Resistances**
physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2855))

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +15,
**Damage** 2d10+6 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d6+6 slashing

**Arcane Innate Spells** DC 20
- **4th**
[Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will)

**Astral Blink** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The stalker steps sideways into the astral realm, reappearing nearby. The stalker teleports to an unoccupied space within 15 feet that they can see.

**Astral Pounce** <actions string="Two Actions" />  **Requirements** The stalker hasn't used Astral Blink this round; **Effect** The stalker Astral Blinks or Strides and makes a Strike at the end of that movement. If the stalker began this action [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), it remains hidden until after this ability's Strike. The stalker then Astral Blinks or Strides again, whichever it did not already do.

**Sneak Attack**   The stalker deals an additional 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

</column>

markdown:
The artificers of Jistka crafted warrior automatons to fight on the front lines of their armies, and in this goal they succeeded admirably. Brutal, effective combatants, champion automatons contain the souls of the best of the Imperium's armies, its most dedicated soldiers and respected officers, all rendered deadlier than ever before in their bodies of metal and magic. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Champion Automaton](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4092) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 49 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1); one other language the champion knew in life (usually Jistkan); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 60 feet **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +5 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 170 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) effects, [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661) attacks, [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Resistances** physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2855)) --- **Speed** 25 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** pincer +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d12+12 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** energy beam +23 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+10 fire **Arcane Slam** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Requirements** The champion has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The champion channels supernatural energy through its pincers, then slams its foe against the ground. The grabbed creature takes 3d6 bludgeoning and 3d6 fire damage, is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and must attempt a DC 29 Fortitude save. On a failure the target is [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) (enfeebled 2 on a critical failure) from the force of the slam. At the end of the Arcane Slam, the grapple ends. **Spinning Toss** **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The champion has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77); **Effect** The champion spins on its axis, using the creature it's holding as an impromptu bludgeon before tossing it aside. The champion attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the grabbed creature's Fortitude DC.
**Critical Success** The champion flings its victim. The grapple ends. The grabbed creature is thrown into a space within 10 feet, takes 8d6 bludgeoning damage, and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). All creatures adjacent to the champion take the same amount of bludgeoning damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save).
**Success** As critical success, except the grabbed creatures is thrown into a space within 5 feet, and creatures take 4d6 bludgeoning damage.
**Failure** The champion tosses its victim aside. The grapple ends. The grabbed creature falls prone.
**Critical Failure** The champion fumbles its grasp on its victim and the grapple ends.
name: Champion Automaton

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Yamah (Spelldrinker Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4093)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Yamahs are devoted to protecting others from magic used for evil, particularly the kind that traps souls or controls a creature's free will. They relish taking a malevolent spellcaster's magic and turning it to virtuous purposes.

 Yamahs often sport stern countenances but still enjoy moments of joy and whimsy like other azatas. In particular, they revel in lighthearted jokes and cheery songs when appropriate. Once they enter combat, however, their bright dispositions change to one of focus and determination. While fighting enemies of freedom, yamahs tend to attack from a distance, using their flight and ranged skills to harry foes. If a spellcaster is involved, yamahs move to the front line, attempting to intercede between their allies and the spellcaster's magic.

 Lunar eclipses and other notable celestial events tend to draw yamahs to [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). These azatas appear more often during these events to aid the followers of deities with connections to such events, such as [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281) and the empyreal lord [Ashava](/Deities.aspx?ID=466). Tales among these faiths speak of yamahs who ally with worshippers in their endeavors against evil fiends. These stories inevitably end with the yamah departing after the task is complete, leaving the mortal worshipper without a word.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 20

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Azata_Yamah.webp" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 5">[Yamah](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4093)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Medium" />
<trait label="Azata" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=543" />
<trait label="Celestial" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=551" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 50

**Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485)

**Languages**
[Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +10, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +12, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +12, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +3

**Dex** +4

**Con** +2

**Int** +3

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +5
</row>

**Items**
[Starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=44), _forceful quartz bracelet_ with 3 gems

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 21 

**Fort** +9 

**Ref** +13 

**Will** +13 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 75

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 5, [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 5

**Free Mind** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** An ally of the yamah's attempts a saving throw against an effect that has the [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait; **Effect** The ally's gains a +4 status bonus to the saving throw. If the ally rolls a success, they get a critical success instead.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 70 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_starknife_ +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 piercing

**Ranged**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_starknife_ +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [deadly d6](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)),
**Damage** 2d4+8 piercing

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 21, attack +14
- **Cantrips (3rd)**
[Divine Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1498)
- **2nd**
[Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×2), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Sure Footing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1708)
- **3rd**
[Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493)
- **Constant (5th)**
[Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Crystallized Attack** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** The yamah has a charged gem on its _forceful quartz bracelet_; **Effect** The yamah channels the magic from an active gem, causing its starknife to glow with unnatural brightness. Their next starknife Strike before the end of their turn deals an extra 1d6 force damage and increases its thrown range to 60 feet. This drains one of their quartz gems.

**Sneak Attack**   The yamah's Strikes deal an extra 1d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.

**Starstrike**   Any non-magical [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399) becomes a _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) weapon_ while a yamah wields it.

**Steal Magic** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The yamah makes a melee spell attack against a creature under the effects of a spell; a yamah automatically succeeds with this attack against a willing creature. On a success, the yamah's divine touch attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell (counteract rank 3, counteract modifier +16). A successful counteract siphons the magical energy into one of the gems on its _forceful quartz bracelet_, recharging it.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Yamah Bracelets</title>

Each yamah wears a personalized bracelet, embedded with quartz gems that reflect the cosmos. These are empowered by unseen magic, recharging when the yamah has 8 hours of rest. These bracelets have a unique divine connection to their respective yamah, and the bracelets only function for that yamah. On occasion, a yamah will gift the bracelet to a mortal, more as a symbol of trust and a sign that the individual is protected than an attempt to share power.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-368" />
name: Yamah

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Raelis (Laureate Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4094)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Formed from the souls of storytellers, these boisterous azatas roam the planes, seeking out ever more impressive stories to collect. They attempt to spread joy by sharing these tales and righting wrongs they encounter. Their constant search for tales brings raelises to the mortal Universe more often than other azatas.

 A raelis is generally knowledgeable about trails and directions, as their journeys take them to a variety of different locales. Raelises prefer to avoid taking the same journey twice and will go out of their way to avoid doing so. After millennia of constant journeys, a raelis becomes a living atlas, something they take great pride in.

 Raelises adore brawling and wrestling, and often travel in mortal guises so as not to unduly influence the events they chronicle. Their ability to travel discreetly among settlements make raelises excellent spies and discreet agents for gods with similar interests.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 28

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Azata_Raelis.webp" />

If this spell has the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) trait, they Cast it as one spell rank higher. This expends the scroll as normal.

**Word Caller** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The raelis senses the presence of words around them within 60 feet, reading up to 100 pages of nonmagical writing or automatically succeeding at a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) to identify 1 magical scroll.

markdown:
Zhuraitas are dedicated to the freedom of academic and scientific inquiry, protecting the creativity and inventiveness of scholarly fields. Many good-hearted academics have breakthroughs due to a zhuraita's inspiration, notably with projects that have lasting positive impacts on others. Zhuraitas also travel to defend important centers of knowledge when they are in danger of destruction. Zhuraitas despise those who would use research as a tool of oppression, and there are even rumors of them sabotaging such projects, hindering their completion. The azatas are known to sometimes hide away knowledge they believe to be harmful or dangerous. They don't outright destroy such knowledge, however, and instead choose to seal it away in places they can defend. On rare occasions, a zhuraita will recognize that such knowledge might actually be of some use for a trusted ally and share the information, though only under the zhuraita's supervision. **[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Zhuraita](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4095) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 52 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=2), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Academia Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +30, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +28, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +25, [Library Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +30, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +28, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +26, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +28, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +28 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +23 **Ref** +26 **Will** +29 **HP** 280 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 15, [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 15 **Deconstruct** **Trigger** The zhuraita is targeted by an effect with the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) or [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) trait; **Effect** The zhuraita critiques their enemy's theoretical underpinnings, attempting a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check with a bonus of +28 (counteract rank 8). If the effect is counteracted, the triggering enemy becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) (frightened 2 if the zhuraita critically succeeded). --- **Speed** 40 feet; fly 80 feet **Melee** thesis +27 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+10 bludgeoning **Ranged** looseleaf +27 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d10+10 slashing **Divine Innate Spells** DC 36 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **1st** [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603) (at will) - **3rd** [Hypercognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563) - **4th** [Translate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1723) (at will) - **8th** [Clear Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) - **Constant (6th)** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) **Revealing Hypothesis** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** The zhuraita hits a creature with its thesis; **Effect** The zhuraita's thesis opens, with the pages fluttering at an unnatural speed. It then slams shut, with the cover now showing the image of the creature it hit. The zhuraita's Strikes against that creature deal an additional 2d6 precision damage. The zhuraita can only have one creature as the focus of its Revealing Hypothesis, which lasts until they target someone else. **Thesis Shield** A spinning circle of tomes surrounds the zhuraita, lasting until the start of their next turn. While Thesis Shield is active, the zhuraita gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and has the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition.
Prayer To The Zhuraita Many a scholar have found themselves burning the last nub of their candle late into the night, the halls of learning echoing their words:
_O holy Scholar, please recall to me this footnote's source;
Without its mislaid citation, my evidence will falter;
My professor will scorn my unfinished work;
Earning my thesis unjust failure._
name: Zhuraita

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Veranallia (Rebirth Azata)](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4096)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Veranallias represent the freedom of life to grow, change, and adapt. They rarely interact directly with non-azatas, as most other beings find their nature hard to comprehend, but those who secure their aid find them powerful allies. The lower half of a veranallia's body is made of vegetation that constantly repeats a dizzying cycle of sprouting, blooming, thriving, and wilting. The upper half of their body appears as that of a humanoid of any gender—it's rare for a veranallia to remain consistent in their gender for more than a few seasons at a time.

 Veranallias transform the landscape in their wake, bringing creation and destruction alike. The world around them overflows with an abundance of vegetation, with plenty of food for nearby animals, and when they bring destruction, they do so without cruelty, as it's sometimes necessary to make room for new life. They trigger forest fires in woodlands before dry brush piles up to dangerous levels and bring bitter winters to areas plagued with parasites that thrive in hot weather.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Celestial](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 40

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Azata_Veranallia.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 20">[Veranallia](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4096)</title>

**Skills**
[Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +38, [Elysium Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +36, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +34, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +38

**Dex** +6

**Con** +8

**Int** +6

</row>

_+3 [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [sickle](/Weapons.aspx?ID=364)_

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 45 

**Fort** +33 

**Ref** +30 

**Will** +36 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 475

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 20, [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 20

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) 20, [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 20

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, fly 40 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_sickle_ +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716)),
**Damage** 3d4+12 slashing plus 4d6 cold and 1d6 spirit

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
vine +36 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 4d12+16 bludgeoning plus 1d6 spirit and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66)

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 42, attack +34
- **6th**
[Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479) (at will), [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) (at will)
- **8th**
[Arctic Rift](/Spells.aspx?ID=1444) (at will), [Sunburst](/Spells.aspx?ID=1707) (at will)
- **10th**
[Cataclysm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1460), [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) (once per year), [Revival](/Spells.aspx?ID=1654), [Nature’s Enmity](/Spells.aspx?ID=2010), [Nature's Pathway](/Spells.aspx?ID=1613) (at will), [Regenerate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1648), [Wrathful Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1759)
- **Constant (9th)**
[Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517), [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680), [Speak with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1681), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)

**Rituals** DC 42

- **2nd**
[Consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=8)
- **3rd**
[Reincarnate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=35)
- **4th**
[Blight](/Rituals.aspx?ID=4), [Plant Growth](/Rituals.aspx?ID=18)
- **6th**
[Awaken Animal](/Rituals.aspx?ID=3), [Commune](/Rituals.aspx?ID=6) (nature only), [Primal Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=19)

**Alter Weather** <actions string="Three Actions" />  **Frequency** three times per day; **Effect** The veranallia dramatically alters weather patterns in the surrounding area, producing any of the results of a successful 9th-rank [_control weather_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) ritual.

**Rebirth**  ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The veranallia spends a minute to encase a creature that has been dead for no longer than a year and a day in a cocoon. After 24 hours, the creature is restored to life and the cocoon explodes in a shower of colorful blossoms. If the veranallia chooses, Rebirth can change the creature's ancestry or heritage, typically into a [nephilim](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=68).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Primordial Veranallia</title>

In the deepest reaches of [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), the eldest of veranallias roam. These beings concern themselves with transformations that take place over the course of millennia, as well as changes in the climates of entire worlds. Primordial veranallias have an array of unique powers that can affect the area for many miles around them, from turning deserts into glass to triggering earthquakes.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-368" />
name: Veranallia

markdown:
The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons. The innate magic that flows through these dragons causes them to draw particular metals to their bodies like magnets or, in some cases, naturally grow these skymetals on their bodies. Adamantine dragons begin their lives with tough scales that are naturally replaced with thicker and even tougher adamantine plating as they grow older. Adamantine dragons are typically steadfast and loyal. Once they commit to a certain purpose, changing their minds is nigh impossible. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Adamantine Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4097) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 42 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 120 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Mining Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +34, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +9 **Int** +5 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +40 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 400 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 20 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915)) **Abandon Armor** Once the adamantine dragon is reduced to fewer than half their Hit Points, their resistance is reduced by 10 and they gain a +10 circumstance bonus to their Speeds.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 41
**Resilient Form** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The archdragon's scales ward off deadly attacks. The dragon attempts a DC 14 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333). On a success, the triggering attack becomes a normal hit. --- **Speed** 50 feet, burrow 60 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=66) **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning **Ranged** rock +37 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range increment 150 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+18 bludgeoning plus adamantine weight **Adamantine Body** The dragon's unarmed melee Strikes are [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915). **Adamantine Weight** ([Earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon shapes rocks even after it throws them, capturing its prey. The creature struck must succeed at a DC 44 Reflex save or be [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81). Creatures remain immobilized until they [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) and immediately fall if they're flying. The weight can be destroyed as an object with 40 Hit Points and 20 Hardness. **Avalanche Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon belches a mass of boulders that deals 17d8 bludgeoning damage in a 50-foot cone (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Avalanche Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Burrowing Pounce** **Requirements** The dragon is burrowed; **Effect** The dragon [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310), then [Leaps](/Actions.aspx?ID=2298) out of the ground, landing at a point within 40 feet. The dragon makes a melee Strike against a creature within reach when they land. If the Strike is a critical hit, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). **Fast Swallow** **Trigger** The dragon [Grabs](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) a creature; **Effect** The dragon uses [Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77). **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Avalanche Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Rock Tunneler** The dragon can burrow through solid stone at a Speed of 40 feet. They can leave a tunnel behind if they desire. **Sky Below Stone** ([Metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) An eruption of skymetal blades churns the earth in a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) and creatures touching the ground take 8d8 slashing damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) DC 45 Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save are knocked prone and moved 20 feet in a direction of the dragon's choice. **[Swallow Whole](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=77)** Large, 4d12+12 bludgeoning, Rupture 40 **[Throw Rock](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=80)**

Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures. Cinder dragons’ appearance evokes their flame, often in scales with mixed patterns of red, orange, and yellow. Many cinder dragons dwell in active volcanoes and similarly fiery locales. Cinder dragons prefer treasures that can withstand the heat of their bodies and lairs, with gemstones, gold, and silver common among their hoards. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Cinder Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4098) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 46 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +35, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +43, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +42, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +40, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +42 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +8 **Int** +6 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +8 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair a cinder dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +34 **Will** +36 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 525 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** cold 20 **Dragon Heat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) 5 feet, 5d6 fire damage (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex)
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 42
**Boiling Blood** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon's superheated blood spills onto the attacker. The target takes 12d6 fire damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** Jaws only --- **Speed** 70 feet, fly 210 feet **Melee** jaws +41 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+16 piercing plus 2d6 persistent fire **Melee** horn +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+20 piercing **Melee** claw +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+16 slashing **Melee** tail +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+16 slashing **Melee** wing +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+16 slashing **All Becomes Flame** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon curses a creature within 60 feet to have its magic replaced with primordial flames. The creature must attempt a DC 43 Will save. Regardless of the result, the target becomes temporarily immune for 1 day.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is cursed for 1 round. While cursed, any damage the cursed creature would deal by any means becomes fire damage, regardless of the original damage type. The cursed creature can temporarily suppress the curse for 1 round as an action.
**Failure** As success, but the curse's duration is 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the curse's duration is 1 day. **Blazing Wind** **Requirements** The dragon is able to use their Pyre Breath; **Effect** The dragon Flies up to their Speed in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388). At the end of their movement, a superheated gale blows out ahead of them in a 200-foot line that's 20 feet wide. Structures and vehicles might collapse or catch fire in the whirling inferno, as earthquake. In addition, any creature in the area must attempt a DC 45 Fortitude save. This ability counts as a use of the dragon's Pyre Breath.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 8d6 fire damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 16d6 fire damage and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** As failure, plus metal items are superheated and wooden items catch fire. Creatures who critically fail their save catch on fire, taking 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The area then fills with black smoke for 1 minute. This has the effects of mist, except it fills the cone's area. The dragon can't use Pyre Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Stoke the Flames** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon intensifies nearby fires. Every foe within 60 feet taking [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) takes 6d6 fire damage.

Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion. Even by draconic standards, coral dragons stand out for their arrogance and vanity, boasting inordinate pride in the vibrant hues of their own coral-encrusted scales and the dazzling splendor of the reefs they protect. Coral dragons like to decorate their reefs, which they view as lairs, with objects of beauty, dotted with the occasional bit of valuables like coins. Those who seek the favor of a coral dragon often find the creature easily swayed by flattery, particularly when paired with shiny or brightly colored gifts that complement the scintillating beauty of the dragon and their underwater domain. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Coral Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4099) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 54 **Perception** +38; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +36, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +32, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +36, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +38 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +33 **Will** +34 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 400 **Immunities** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Kaleidoscopic Display** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 90 feet. The coral formations covering the dragon's body shimmer with vivid colors, overwhelming the senses. Any creature entering or beginning its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 42 Will save. Regardless of outcome, the creature is temporarily immune to the dragon's Kaleidoscopic Display for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is blinded for 1 round and dazzled for 1 minute.
**Reef Bond** Every coral dragon is mystically bound to a single living coral reef. If the dragon moves more than 3 miles from their reef, they become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 and unable to recover, with the sickened value increasing by 1 every 6 hours unless they succeed at a DC 46 Fortitude save. After 24 hours, the dragon becomes [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1; their drained value increases by 1 every 24 hours.
If the dragon's reef suffers significant physical damage, they immediately become aware of the location where the reef was harmed, but not the source or nature of the damage. Should the reef ever be completely destroyed, the dragon is immediately slain.
Biomineralize **Trigger** The dragon takes a critical hit from a melee weapon without reach or an unarmed attack that deals slashing or piercing damage; **Effect** A gout of blood spurts from the dragon's wound and instantaneously calcifies into a jagged branch of sharpened coral. The coral branch impales the triggering creature, dealing 10d6 piercing damage (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The triggering creature also takes 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86)on a critical failure. Regardless of the outcome, the coral then crumbles to dust. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 80 feet, swim 140 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning plus [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hydraulic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hydraulic Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon exhales a pressurized jet of water that deals 22d6 bludgeoning damage in a 140-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that critically fail their Reflex save against the Hydraulic Breath are pushed back 10 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The dragon can't use Hydraulic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Reef Armor** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon calls upon primal forces of the sea to encase themself in an impenetrable shell of protective coral, gaining 80 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and resistance 15 to piercing and slashing damage until the temporary Hit Points are depleted. The effect lasts for 1 minute, until destroyed, or until the dragon [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect. **Reef Meld** ([Extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=596), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The coral dragon is in physical contact with their bound reef; **Effect** The dragon physically merges with the reef and vanishes, along with up to 12 willing creatures who are touching the reef, into an extradimensional space where they can neither affect nor be affected by the outside world. The effect lasts indefinitely or until the dragon [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) it. Once merged, the dragon can spend 1 minute traveling to and emerging from any point on their reef, regardless of distance.
name: Coral Archdragon

markdown:
Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit. As with other occult dragons, despair dragons are driven by an innate compulsion, in their case, the desire to strike terror in others. Despair dragons tend to settle near settlements, so they have a source of fear to draw from. The most common items in despair dragon hoards are scrolls, tomes, and relics that serve as research material. Through these, despair dragons learn of local folklore, urban legends, and more to better haunt their targets. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Despair Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4100) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 58 **Perception** +38; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), fearsense (precise) 60 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 90 feet, [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +41, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +35, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +39 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +8 **Fearsense** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon senses all creatures with the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition at the listed range. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +36 **Will** +38 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 415 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 120 feet. DC 45
Consume Fear ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet loses the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition; **Effect** The dragon feasts upon the fear that leaves the triggering creature's body, gaining 9d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
Unbidden Thoughts ([fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The attacker's mind fills with visions of their worst fears that overwhelm their senses, and they must choose one of the following results: either the triggering attack becomes a normal Strike, or the critical hit is unaffected but the triggering creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+22 piercing **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+22 slashing **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 45 - **9th** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (at will), [Wave of Despair](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Shrieking Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Inspire Dread** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The despair dragon telepathically communicates with a creature within 90 feet, listing their deep-seated fears. The creature takes 16d6 mental damage and must attempt a DC 45 Will save. Regardless of the result, the creature becomes temporarily immune to Inspire Dread for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
Failure The creature takes full damage and becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and becomes frightened 3. **Look Behind You** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** The dragon successfully [Hides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) from a creature within 90 feet; **Effect** The dragon teleports to a space directly behind the target creature. The dragon immediately becomes detected unless their chosen position has [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2559) or another means to remain [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79). **Shrieking Breath** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The dragon lets out a cacophonous sound made of every scream the dragon has drawn from a terrified enemy, dealing 23d6 sonic damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail their Will save must spend the first action of their next turn doing nothing but screaming. The dragon can't use Shrieking Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Tongue Decoy** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The despair dragon inflates several bladders at the end of their tongue to create the basic form of a creature. This process takes 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the bladders deflate, and the dragon must start over. Once the process is complete, the dragon can maintain the inflated bladders indefinitely, and can Dismiss to deflate the bladders and retract their tongue instantly.
The inflated tongue takes the basic form of an animal or humanoid, and can be inflated to be either Small or Medium. The form resembles the general silhouette of a creature, though closer inspection and success at a DC 30 Perception check can determine the true nature of the tongue. While inflated, the dragon can send their voice through the decoy, though keeping their tongue inflated makes it difficult to speak, causing the dragon to take a –4 circumstance penalty to any checks related to speaking, such as [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389).
The dragon's tongue can extend up to 90 feet from the dragon's body, and they can fully extend their tongue as part of the process to inflate the bladders. The dragon can move the inflated part of their tongue up to 15 feet at a time with an action, which has the concentrate, manipulate, and move traits. While extended, the inflated end of the tongue occupies space as a creature of the appropriate size, but the rest of the tongue doesn't impede or block movement in any way. The dragon's scent functions through cilia at the end of the tongue, but otherwise the dragon has no means of knowing what's near their tongue.
Attacking the tongue is the same as attacking the dragon, except that the tongue is always [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the tongue takes any damage, it immediately deflates and remains extended. The dragon is off-guard as long as their tongue is out, but the dragon can retract their tongue with 2 consecutive Interact actions. If the tongue takes damage, the dragon can't use their Tongue Decoy again for 1 day.
name: Despair Archdragon

markdown:
[Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself. Diabolic dragons, these scholars argue, are just extensions of the plane, living creatures that break off from Hell to enact its will. Whether this is true or whether diabolical dragons are simply the reborn souls of dragons sent to Hell, the fact remains that these dragons are powerful, cunning, and tyrannical. Every diabolic dragon's goal is to further Hell's will, though how this happens can vary. Regardless of their goals, these dragons always approach newcomers with an unsettling calmness. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Diabolic Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4101) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 62 **Perception** +37; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +43, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Hell Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +38, [Legal Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +39, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +39, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +37, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +37 **Str** +12 **Dex** +6 **Con** +9 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +9 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair the dragon's vision they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +40 **Ref** +36 **Will** +36 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 480 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 20 [**Frightful Presence**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 44
Hell's Sting ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack) **Effect** The dragon channels the rancor of Hell back through the body of their foe, overwhelming it with an infernal assault on the mind. The triggering creature takes 12d6 mental damage with a DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. Holy creatures use an outcome one degree of success worse than they roll on their saving throw.
**[**Reactive Strike**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 80 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +42 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d12+22 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** claws +42 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d8+22 piercing plus 2d6 fire and [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) (page 229) **Melee** tail +40 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 4d8+22 bludgeoning plus 2d6 fire and [Improved Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=28) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 46, attack +38 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; self only) - **8th** [Summon Fiend](/Spells.aspx?ID=1701) ([phistophilus](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2909) only; at will) - **9th** [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496) (at will), [Falling Stars](/Spells.aspx?ID=1521) (fire only), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) (at will) - **10th** [Gate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1540) (to Hell only) **Diabolic Fire** Any fire damage that a diabolic dragon deals, including fire damage from spells, is imbued with the unholy power of Hell to scorch the spirit as well. A creature takes spirit damage instead of fire damage if that would be more detrimental to the creature (as determined by the GM). A diabolic dragon is immune to the diabolic fire of other diabolic dragons, the fire from divine immolation, and similar effects. **Diabolic Impalement** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) **Requirements** The dragon has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in its claw and doesn't already have a creature impaled; **Effect** The dragon attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check against the creature's Fortitude DC. On a success, the dragon drives the creature through the bony protrusion on their chest, dealing 7d10 piercing damage (doubled on a critical success). The creature is grabbed while on the protrusion and can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as normal. The dragon's claws become free to Grab other creatures, and the dragon doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the impaled creature grabbed. If the dragon moves, they bring the grabbed creature along with them.
An impaled creature takes 2d10 spirit damage at the end of each of its turns, as the bone drives itself deeper to pierce the creature's soul. If the creature dies from this spirit damage, its soul becomes condemned to [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42). A creature condemned to Hell in this way can't be resurrected except by a [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual, divine intervention, or reclaiming the soul from Hell. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hellfire Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hellfire Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)) The dragon unleashes a blast of infernal fire that deals 24d6 fire damage in a 70-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The dragon can't use Hellfire Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Diabolic Archdragon

markdown:
Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them. They constantly use these magical energies to empower their magical abilities and even their bodies, as the energy can heal wounds. A fortune dragon has a typical build for an arcane dragon, but their bodies sport a striking feature: their treasure. The dragon's nature of drawing upon magic causes coins, gems, and, most notably, magical items to cling to their body like iron drawn to magnets. A dragon constantly pulls magical energies from the items attached to their body and makes use of these energies to cast spells. The magical energies that flow through a fortune dragon constantly flow through the dragon's items as well, and in many cases, the items melt from the heat produced in this process. Fortune dragons are seekers of novel experiences. This desire for originality leads fortune dragons to approach visitors of other ancestries with curiosity, though this initial interest quickly wanes if a visitor lacks exciting qualities. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Fortune Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4102) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 70 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=157), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109) **Skills** [Accounting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +43, [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +42, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +43, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +37, [Mercantile Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +43, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +42 **Str** +9 **Dex** +9 **Con** +8 **Int** +11 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +39 **Will** +37 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 400 **Immunities** [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Aura of Disruption ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 120 feet. The dragon radiates disruptive energies that allow them to feed on magic. When a spell is [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) or [disrupted](/Rules.aspx?ID=2236) within the aura, the dragon regains one expended spontaneous spell slot and gains 45 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
Capture Spell ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon succeeds or critically succeeds at a saving throw against a spell; **Effect** The dragon attempts to capture a portion of the spell's magic to feed themself. They attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) the spell (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +42). If successful, the dragon is unaffected by the spell and regains one expended spontaneous spell slot; other subjects are affected by the spell normally. --- **Speed** 90 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+19 piercing plus 1d6 force **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+19 piercing plus 1d6 force **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+19 bludgeoning plus 1d6 force **Arcane Spontaneous Spells** DC 50, attack +42 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **10th** [Chain Lightning](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Implosion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1572), [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644), [Slither](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676), [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732), [Warp Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754) (3 slots) **Disruptive Breath** ([[arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [[force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)](/Traits.aspx?ID=666)) The dragon unleashes a spray of magic-disrupting energies that deals 22d6 force damage in an 80-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 50 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Disruptive Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon chooses to either recharge Disruptive Breath or regain one expended spontaneous spell slot. **Drain Hoard** **Requirements** The dragon is within 60 feet of their hoard; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon draws power out of the magic items in their hoard, regaining all their expended spontaneous spell slots. **Share the Wealth** **Requirements** The dragon's body is covered in riches (this is typically the case when the dragon is first encountered); **Effect** The dragon shakes their body aggressively, sending coins and other riches flying in every direction, dealing 22d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save to all creatures in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The dragon's body is then no longer covered in riches. **Treasure Dive** **Requirements** The dragon's body isn't covered in riches and the dragon is adjacent to their hoard; **Effect** The dragon [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through their hoard using their land Speed. They coat themself in coins, magic items, and other treasures. This contact with magical items revitalizes the dragon, causing them to regain one expended spontaneous spell slot. The dragon can move through other creatures while moving in this way. Creatures in the dragon's path, or above it if the dragon Burrows, must succeed at a DC 43 Reflex save or be pushed 10 feet (or pushed 20 feet and knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) on a critical failure). **Treasure Tsunami** **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Requirements** The dragon is within 120 feet of their hoard; **Effect** The dragon exerts their pull on their hoard, creating a wave of treasures 60 feet wide and 60 feet tall that [emanates](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) at a place of their choosing within their hoard and within 120 feet. The wave moves in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) toward them, ending where they stand and crashing over them, covering the dragon in riches. Creatures struck by the wave must succeed at a DC 43 Reflex save or take 15d10 bludgeoning damage and are [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=3123) 20 feet toward the dragon (on a success they take half the bludgeoning damage and are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) instead of pulled).
name: Fortune Archdragon

markdown:
The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon. Notably among these are the massive paired horns of the horned dragon. While their bulky frames, natural coloration, and prominent ridged scales are all remarkable in their own way, it's the horns that are most obvious and striking at first glance. Horned dragons use their horns to impale their prey in a quick and brutal display of their might. They are generally contemplative and have a fixation on knowledge and self-discipline, traits belied by their bestial appearance. As a result, horned dragons are generally more open to speaking with outsiders. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 47 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 45 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 [Horned Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4103) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 74 **Perception** +36; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Arboreal](/Languages.aspx?ID=26), [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +30, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +38, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +33, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +37, [Forest Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +37, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +34, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +41, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 **Camouflage** The dragon can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) in natural environments even if they don't have cover. --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +35 **Will** +38 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 400 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 43
Miasma ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) 20 feet. After the dragon uses their Poison Breath, a cloud of poison gas continues to [emanate](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from their body for 1 round. Any creature that ends its turn in the miasma takes 6d6 poison damage (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Any creature in the miasma is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) and treats other creatures as concealed. The dragon can see through this concealment.
**Twisting Tail** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using **Effect** The dragon makes a tail Strike at the creature with a –2 penalty. If the Strike hits, the dragon disrupts the creature's action. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 240 feet, swim 60 feet; forest passage, trackless journey **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus 3d6 poison **Melee** claw +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 slashing **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+18 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 44 - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) (at will) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) - **8th** [Earthquake](/Spells.aspx?ID=1507) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Poison Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Forest Passage** The horned dragon [ignores any difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) caused by plants, such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth. Even plants manipulated by magic don't impede their progress. **Impaling Charge** **Requirements** The dragon doesn't have a creature impaled on their horn; **Effect** The dragon attempts to gore a foe. They [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), then attempt a horn Strike. On a hit, the target becomes impaled on the dragon's horn. The creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) while on the horn (and can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) as normal). The dragon doesn't need to use additional actions to keep the impaled creature grabbed. If the dragon moves, they bring the grabbed creature along with them. **Poison Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The dragon breathes a toxic cloud that deals 22d6 poison damage in a 70-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). The Poison Breath also creates a miasma. The dragon can't use Poison Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Trackless Journey** The horned dragon always gains the benefits of [Cover Tracks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2407) in natural surroundings, even while moving at full speed. **Venomous Horn** **Requirements** The dragon can use its Poison Breath; **Effect** The dragon redirects its natural poisons into its horn. Until the end of its turn, the dragon's next horn Strike deals an additional 3d6 poison damage. If the dragon uses Impaling Charge while Miasma Horn is active to impale a target, the target must also succeed at a DC 41 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 3 (sickened 4 on a critical failure).
name: Horned Archdragon

markdown:
Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas. In addition to their magical prowess, mirage dragons possess a number of additional features to help them on hunts or mislead attackers, such as their camouflaging scales and a hallucinatory breath that can confound multiple foes at once. Mirage dragons are vain and egotistical figures. They ultimately care more about themselves than others. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Mirage Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4104) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 78 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), illusion sense, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +40, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +38, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +40, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +44, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +42, [Illusion Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +40, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +42, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +42, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +40 **Str** +9 **Dex** +9 **Con** +7 **Int** +7 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +10 Camouflage The dragon can [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) in natural environments even if they don't have [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372).
**Illusion Sense** When the dragon moves within 30 feet of an illusion that can be disbelieved, they automatically attempt a secret check to disbelieve, even if they didn't spend an action to [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259). --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +34 **Ref** +35 **Will** +39 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 450 **Immunities** [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Aura of Isolation ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) 60 feet. The mirage dragon warps the light around them, confounding their foes' vision. A creature who enters or begins its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 42 Will save or treat all its allies as [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) by sight for 1 round (though it can use its other senses to ascertain their whereabouts).
Scintillating Defense ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon flashes their iridescent scales at the triggering creature to throw off the attack. The dragon gains [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 70 feet, climb 50 feet, fly 210 feet **Melee** jaws +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+17 piercing **Melee** claws +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+17 slashing **Melee** tail +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+17 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 45, attack +40 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **4th** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) (at will) - **8th** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will), [Illusory Scene](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570) (at will), [Mirage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741) - **9th** [Illusory Creature](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [Phantasmagoria](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629) - **10th** [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) **Captivating Display** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon opens the fins on their head, creating a radiant display of enthralling colors. Each creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (or slowed 2 on a critical failure) for 1 round.
