The dragon can't use Hallucinatory Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Lunging Bite** The dragon lunges their head forward, making a jaws Strike with an extended reach of 25 feet. **Sneak Attack** The dragon's Strikes deal an additional 3d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) targets.

markdown:
Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet song that never stops playing in their minds. While an omen dragon can focus on or ignore the music of fate at any time, the song plays all the same. At a glance, omen dragons resemble other occult dragons in appearance, save for the mirror-like interior membrane of their wings. An omen dragon's wings offer glimpses into the future. These glimpses are cloudy and vague, but generally correct, if only technically. Omen dragons have a natural compulsion to share the futures they see. These dragons have no compunctions about what the visions show and share their knowledge equally with innocent villagers as they do with wicked tyrants. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Omen Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4105) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 82 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +36, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +35, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +39, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +39, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +37 **Str** +9 **Dex** +7 **Con** +8 **Int** +10 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +33 **Will** +35 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 360 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Untethered to Fate The dragon can choose to negate any [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effects that would affect them; other creatures remain affected normally.
Challenge Fate ([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted by an attack; **Effect** This fate is not set in stone. The attacker rolls the triggering attack twice and uses the worse result.
**Cheat Fate** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** An attack would reduce the dragon to 0 HP; **Effect** The dragon foresees the killing blow and negates the triggering attack, avoiding all damage and other effects. --- **Speed** 70 feet, fly 210 feet **Melee** jaws +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+17 piercing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** claw +36 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+17 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** tail +34 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+17 bludgeoning plus 1d8 mental **Melee** wing +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+17 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 44 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) - **7th** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566) (at will), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603) (at will), [True Target](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726) (×2) - **8th** [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) - **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537) - **10th** [Freeze Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=1538) **Destiny Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon breathes a translucent mist of potentialities that overwhelms creatures with visions of possible futures, dealing 19d6 mental damage in a 50-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 44 Will save). A creature that fails its save is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round (or slowed 2 on a critical failure) as it struggles with the visions. The dragon can't use Destiny Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum ** The dragon recharges their Destiny Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Impending Fate** The dragon's attacks bring their foes closer to their eventual fates. When the dragon critically hits with a Strike or a creature critically fails against the dragon's Destiny Breath, the creature becomes [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1, or increases its doomed value by 1 if it was already doomed. **Prophetic Wings** The dragon or any ally can glimpse into the future in their wings in a process that requires 10 minutes of concentration. This casts a 10th-rank [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) spell, except that the wings can predict results up to 1 day into the future and the dragon always speaks a few cryptic words related to the result of the prediction. The dragon can use their wings in this way only once per hour and a given creature can seek a future in the wings only once per week. In addition, a creature can choose to predict events up to 1 year into the future—the dragon can view a year ahead in their own wings only once per day. **Walk the Timelines** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The dragon splits themself into two versions with different fates. Each copy [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) from the dragon's current space, then takes a single action. If the actions are both attacks, they use the same multiple attack penalty and count as one attack toward the dragon's multiple attack penalty. After both actions, the dragon chooses one of the two locations as their actual destination and the other version of themself disappears.
name: Omen Archdragon

markdown:
No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic. Explorers and scholars, phase dragons move about at will, discovering new locales and the arcane secrets of teleportation. They frequently establish multiple lairs in far-flung places they repeatedly visit. Beyond the typical wealth found in lairs, phase dragons tend to keep items of sentimental value from their travels, such as a particularly rare flower from the region or a piece by a local artist. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Phase Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4106) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 86 **Perception** +38; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +41, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +42, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +44, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +40, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +37 **Str** +7 **Dex** +9 **Con** +5 **Int** +10 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 --- **AC** 47 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +38 **Will** +37 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 300 **Immunities** [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Unerring Location The dragon automatically attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) any teleportation effect that targets them (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +37). The dragon can choose to be affected normally instead. Other creatures targeted by the same effect remain affected normally.
**Redirect** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** A creature within 100 feet casts a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) spell or uses a teleportation effect to appear within 100 feet; **Effect** The dragon determines the destination of the spell or effect. It must be within 100 feet of the dragon and in the spell's range, on the ground, and in a space with no hazards.
**Shoo!** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Trigger** An enemy within 15 feet damages the dragon; **Effect** The dragon teleports the creature up to 45 feet away. The destination must be on the ground and in a space with no hazards. --- **Speed** 70 feet, fly 210 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+17 piercing **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+17 slashing **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+17 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 45 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **4th** [Flicker](/Spells.aspx?ID=1532), [Planar Tether](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (at will) - **5th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **6th** [Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), [Planar Seal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635) - **8th** [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) (×2) - **10th** [Gate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1540) - **Constant (7th)** [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) **Rituals** DC 45 - **7th** [Gathering Call](/Rituals.aspx?ID=148) (doesn't require secondary casters), [Teleportation Circle](/Rituals.aspx?ID=155) (doesn't require secondary casters) **Blinking Barrage** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The dragon channels all their teleportation prowess into a remarkable series of blows. The dragon teleports up to 70 feet to a space adjacent to a creature and makes a claw Strike against that creature. The dragon can do this any number of times, teleporting to a different creature each time. Each attack counts toward their multiple attack penalty, but the penalty does not increase until all attacks have been made. The dragon cannot take actions with the [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710) trait again until the end of their next turn. **Dislocating Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The dragon exhales a swirl of energy that pulls creatures apart, dealing 21d6 force damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The dragon can teleport any creature that fails its save, teleporting that creature up to 60 feet (or twice as far on a critical failure) in any direction. The destination must be on the ground and in a space with no hazards. The dragon can't use Dislocating Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon chooses to either recharge Dislocating Breath or regain an expended teleportation spell. **Phase Jump** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dragon teleports up to 120 feet. If they are airborne, they maintain their momentum, and do not fall at the end of their turn, even if they didn't use an action to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312). **Portal Strike** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) The dragon momentarily opens a small portal, and makes a claw Strike against a creature within 120 feet. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to the Strike.

markdown:
Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife. The safe journey of a soul is of utmost importance to a requiem dragon, and some will follow individual souls from their first entry into the river through to their judgment in the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) and eventually to their ultimate resting place. Most requiem dragons tie themselves to specific planes and shepherd any souls bound to that plane, leading to dragons linked to places like [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41) or [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=42), though these dragons never swear true allegiance to these planes. Requiem dragons fill their lairs along the River of Souls—patchworks of somber architecture rescued from other planes—with invaluable records and mementos of great accomplishments. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 53 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 51 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 48 [Requiem Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4107) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 90 **Perception** +42; lifesense 150 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, status sight **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Daemonic](/Languages.aspx?ID=31), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Requian](/Languages.aspx?ID=34) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +38, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +44, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +47, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +44, [River of Souls Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +40 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +12 **Cha** +7 Soul Journey ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595)) The dragon spends 1 hour to travel through planar channels to reach the River of Souls, and then reach any point along the river. This has the effects of [_interplanar teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576), except that the dragon can arrive precisely where they like on any major plane.
Status Sight The requiem dragon automatically knows the Hit Points of all creatures they can see. --- **AC** 50 **Fort** +38 **Ref** +36 **Will** +42 +2 status to all saves vs. divine **HP** 500 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Soul Anchor ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet would drop to 0 Hit Points; **Effect** The dragon anchors the triggering creature's soul to their body. The creature remains at 1 Hit Point, becomes [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 2, and gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) equal to the dragon's level for 1 minute. The creature becomes temporarily immune to further Soul Anchor attempts for 24 hours.
Withhold Death ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** The dragon resists the loosening of their own soul, preventing some of the damage. The dragon gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 25 to all damage against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 70 feet, fly 210 feet, swim 70 feet **Melee** horn +43 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 piercing plus 2d8 spirit **Melee** claw +43 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+20 slashing plus 2d8 spirit **Melee** tail +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 bludgeoning plus 2d8 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 48 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) - **9th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×4) - **10th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) (×3) **Dooming Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) The raw energy of [Creation's Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27) streams from the dragon's mouth, dealing 20d8 spirit damage in a 150-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures who fail the save must also succeed at a DC 48 Will save or become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. If the target is already doomed, the doomed value increases by 1 (to a maximum of doomed 4). The dragon can't use Dooming Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw strikes and one tail strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Dooming Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Redirect River** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The requiem dragon redirects a small portion of the River of Souls, using their body as a spiritual connection. The river swells around them, filling the area in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) for 1 round, becoming [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) to all other creatures. Additionally, creatures that begin their turn in the emanation or enter it for the first time each round must succeed a DC 48 basic Fortitude save or become [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 and [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1. If the target is already drained or doomed, the value increases by 1 (to a maximum of 4). The dragon can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) the effect. **Soul Shield** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The requiem dragon uses their life essence to create a shield of spiritual energy around a creature within 60 feet. The shield creates a link between the dragon and the creature with the effects of [_share life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) except that the effect doesn't end regardless of distance and remains for 1 hour. In addition, the creature gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to physical and spirit damage that applies to the half of damage it receives. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect. **Spare the Dying, Free the Tormented** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The requiem dragon flaps their mighty wings to produce a gust of energy from [Creation's Forge](/Planes.aspx?ID=27). Each [dying](/Conditions.aspx?ID=69) creature within a 200-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) immediately loses the dying condition, though it remains [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) at 0 Hit Points. Each creature with [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) in the same area takes 16d6 vitality damage (DC 48 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save); a creature who is reduced to 0 Hit Points by this damage is immediately destroyed and its soul returns to the River of Souls, even if that creature has an ability like [rejuvenation](/Feats.aspx?ID=3502). The dragon can choose to exclude as many creatures from this effect as they wish.
name: Requiem Archdragon

markdown:
Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death. They have a mastery of vital energies, allowing them to restore life to the dead, and a mastery of void energies, to bestow death on others. They make use of their abilities to play with the lives of mortals, calling on spirits to aid them or reviving creatures they find important or interesting. The lair of a resurrection dragon is generally a barren place. While they still hoard wealth like other dragons, they do little to decorate their lairs and treasures are generally kept in dark niches, as if the dragon has little care for their possessions. Resurrection dragons tend to take tokens from those they resurrect or plan to resurrect, however, and these are kept particularly safe. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Resurrection Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4108) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 94 **Perception** +40; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +36, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +43, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +43, [Necromancy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +46, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +43, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +38 **Str** +11 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +6 **Wis** +10 **Cha** +8 Arise! ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [exploration](/Traits.aspx?ID=595), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623)) The resurrection dragon uses their mastery over life energy to cast their own soul into the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=39) and pull a willing creature's soul back to their body in a process that takes 1 hour. This has the effects of [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645) except the maximum level of the target is 18 and the soul is tethered to the dragon's. Only one creature can be tethered to the dragon's soul at a time. If the creature and the dragon are no longer on the same plane or the dragon dies, the raised creature dies and can't be raised with Arise! again. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the connection at any time. Doing so doesn't prevent the dragon from raising the creature with Arise! again.
While raised in this way, the creature is still a valid target for _raise dead_, [_resurrection_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=123), and similar effects. Returning the creature to life in this way fully restores the creature, severing the connection to the dragon and allowing the dragon to establish a connection with a different creature. --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +35 **Will** +40 **HP** 470 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) 25 Risen Commander ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A resurrection dragon has a strong connection with their minions and can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) or [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) as a free action once per turn.
Reawaken! ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A living creature the resurrection dragon can see dies; **Effect** The resurrection dragon uses divine and vital energy to retether the soul to their dead body. The willing creature is returned to life with full Hit Points at the start of their next turn. A creature can only be resurrected by this ability once.
Siphon Life ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet uses a healing effect that restores Hit Points; **Effect** The resurrection dragon redirects vital energies away from the effect, minimizing its impact. The triggering effect rolls the minimum amount on any dice rolls to restore Hit Points, and any flat values for restoring Hit Points (such as the additional Hit Points for a two-action [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) spell), are cut in half. The dragon then gains 4d8 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 round. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 240 feet **Melee** jaws +42 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+19 piercing plus 1d6 void **Melee** claw +42 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+19 slashing **Melee** tail +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+19 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 46, attack +38 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Stabilize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Talking Corpse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1712) (at will) - **9th** [Invoke Spirits](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578) (×2) - **10th** [Harm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1552) (×2), [Raise Dead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645), [Summon Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706) (at will) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Soul Siphoning Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Mortal Fears** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The archdragon imposes the fear of death onto the living and fear of the living onto the undead. Each creature within a 120-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 46 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 3. A living creature can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn within 15 feet of the archdragon or an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature. An undead creature can't reduce its frightened condition below 1 if it ends its turn within 15 feet of the archdragon or a living creature.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but frightened 4. **Necro Puppeteer** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon siphons energy into an [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature, a dying creature, or a corpse they can see within 60 feet. The dragon moves the target creature 30 feet and causes it to unleash a wave of void energy in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 8d8 void damage (DC 43 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). **Soul Siphoning Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The archdragon unleashes a torrent of divine energy dealing 22d6 void damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save) that draws the life force from the creatures within. The dragon gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 20 until their Soul Siphoning Breath recharges. The resurrection dragon can't use Soul Siphoning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Resurrection Archdragon

markdown:
Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes. Most rune dragons keep a variety of runes etched on their scales, which their bodies can magically empower. They typically don't cast spells themselves, instead relying on their ability to create runes on the fly to produce desired effects. A rune dragon often refers to their hoard as their library, as it's full of important historical texts, plays, speeches, textbooks, and spellbooks written by people of different cultures in a variety of languages. While they can often speak a language with the aid of magic, they much prefer to learn languages in the context of their speakers so they can grasp the nuances hidden within the syntax and the magic within their symbols. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Rune Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4109) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 98 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), magic sense (precise) 120 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 120 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156); all common and uncommon languages, one rare language **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +38, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +44, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +42, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +37, [Linguistic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +42, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +39, [Rune Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +44, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +38, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +38 **Str** +7 **Dex** +7 **Con** +8 **Int** +9 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 Magic Sense ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon is aware of any active magical abilities and effects within the listed range. When the dragon [Seeks](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301), it gains the benefits of a 4th-rank [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) spell on the things it sees within the listed range (in addition to the normal benefits of Seeking).
Runic Scales The rune dragon's scales function as runestones. The rune dragon can't use the effects or abilities of the runes etched on its scales, but can transfer these runes to appropriate objects. Transferring a rune to or from an item in this way requires 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). The process is automatic and doesn't require a check, but if the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the rune is destroyed. A rune dragon can have any number of runes etched on its scales, though they typically have only a handful of runes etched on their scales at a time. **Items** up to 3 runes of 18th level or lower etched upon their scales --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +35 **Will** +39 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 460 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), Shifting Runes, [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Canceling Rune ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon is the target of a spell that requires a saving throw; **Effect** The dragon attempts to unmake the spell's foundational runes. They attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) the spell (counteract rank 10, counteract modifier +42). If successful, the dragon is unaffected by the spell; other subjects are affected by the spell normally. The dragon can't use canceling rune again for 1d4 rounds.
Retributive Rune **Trigger** A creature within 15 feet damages the rune dragon; **Effect** With a burst of runic magic, the rune dragon creates a detonating rune on the triggering creature and immediately causes it to detonate if it didn't automatically do so.
The rune dragon gains an additional reaction at the beginning of each of their turns that they can use only for a Retributive Rune. --- **Speed** 100 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+15 piercing plus detonating rune **Melee** claw +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+15 slashing plus detonating rune **Melee** tail +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+15 bludgeoning plus [Improved Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=29) 20 feet **Detonating Rune** The rune dragon's Strikes and abilities can leave a detonating rune on their targets. If a creature would receive a detonating rune while they already have one, instead of adding another rune, their current detonating rune activates, dealing 8d6 damage to the target and expending the rune. The detonating rune's damage type matches the dragon's current Shifting Rune. A creature can use an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=3259) action to remove the rune. Detonating runes fade after 1 minute if not detonated. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Runic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Entangling Rune** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon creates large trapping runes in 3 non-overlapping 15-foot [bursts](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet. Creatures other than the dragon that enter a trapped space or end their turn in the trapped space activate the rune, causing it to entangle the creature. The creature must attempt a DC 46 Reflex save or become [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) for 1 minute or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296). The rune can only trap a single creature at a time. The rune vanishes either when a creature succeeds against the rune, when a creature successfully Escapes the rune, or after 1 minute. A creature adjacent to the rune can use an Interact action to remove the rune. **Runic Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon launches hundreds of exploding runes that detonate upon impact, dealing 20d6 damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The damage type of this ability is determined by Shifting Rune. Creatures that take damage from the breath also gain a detonating rune. The dragon can't use Runic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Shifting Runes** **Effect** The rune dragon chooses between [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. The runes etched upon the dragon shift, forming runes of that energy on their scales. The dragon gains [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2313) to that damage type, and their detonating runes and Runic Breath deal that damage type. Anyone trained in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) can immediately recognize the energy type of the etched rune without a check. **Trail of Runes** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The rune dragon [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), leaving behind a collection of runes wherever it steps. The dragon chooses to leave behind either detonating runes or entangling runes. Every space that the dragon moves through, including spaces occupied by creatures, gains a rune. These runes automatically activate when a creature other than the rune dragon enters the space or ends its turn in the space. The runes can otherwise be removed or vanish like normal.
name: Rune Archdragon

markdown:
Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships. They generally do so not out of an intent to hold the information over others or to use for their own machinations, but simply because the process of learning and gathering information is fulfilling. Their hoards are sparse compared to those of other dragons, as they hold their true treasures—secrets—in their minds. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Whisper Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4110) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 102 **Perception** +33; (35 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +32, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +35, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +36, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +39, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +34, [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +10 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +9 **Communal Knowledge** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689)) The dragon mentally communes with all their current active informants simultaneously. They learn the present state of all the informants, as status, and any new information the informants have learned since they were last in communication. Fragments of extraneous information briefly remain after using Communal Knowledge; for the next hour, the dragon can attempt [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks for any skill with a modifier of +36.
**Information Network** The dragon can attempt a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) in place of a check to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391), recalling intelligence from prior informants. --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +34 **Will** +37 **HP** 370 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Diplomatic Solution** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** The dragon rolls initiative; **Effect** The dragon affects all enemies it can see within 60 feet with [_calm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458) heightened to half the dragon's level rounded up (Will DC 42). The dragon doesn't need to [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect, but if the dragon takes any [hostile action](/Rules.aspx?ID=2251) against those affected, it breaks the effect for all creatures.
Distracting Whisper ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** A mysterious voice whispers something disconcerting in the triggering creature's ear, inflicting a –2 circumstance penalty to the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+16 piercing **Melee** claw +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+16 slashing **Melee** tail +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+16 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **3rd** [Clairaudience](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465) (at will), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) - **4th** [Clairvoyance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1466) (at will), [Embed Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1511) (at will) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) (at will) - **6th** [Truesight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **9th** [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) - **Constant (9th)** [Hidden Mind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1556) **Cogitation Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes a befuddling miasma, dealing 19d6 mental damage in an 80-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature that fails its save is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Cogitation Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge their Cogitation Breath. **Steal Knowledge** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon plucks a fragment of knowledge from the mind of a creature within 60 feet, choosing a skill to affect. The creature must attempt a DC 42 Will save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** For the next minute, the creature takes a –1 status penalty to checks using that skill, and the dragon gets a +1 status bonus to using that skill.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the penalty is –2 and the bonus is +2. **Thought Whispers** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The dragon sends their mind out to seek others' thoughts, affecting all creatures within 80 feet with [_mind reading_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (Will DC 42). **Unveil Secret** ([Fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon delves into the mind of a creature within 60 feet, learning something they'd find embarrassing or shameful unless they succeed at a DC 42 Will save. The target becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 and cannot reduce their frightened condition for 1 minute or until the dragon reveals the secret. As a reaction when the affected creature attempts a check, the dragon can reveal their secret to discomfit them, requiring them to roll twice and take the lower result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.
name: Whisper Archdragon

markdown:
Barrage dragons are largely solitary creatures. They make their lairs in secluded, hard-to-find places like the peaks of mountains or the depths of abandoned mines and quarries. They tend to stay away from large population centers, especially where there's an abundance of arcane magic being used. Even young barrage dragons are usually found alone, and it's suspected that they don't breed in a biological sense. It's instead theorized that as barrage dragons age and grow, the arcane energy that forms them becomes too potent to be contained within their physical form. As it begins to arc and sputter out, uncontained and uncontrollable, it creates a mass of chaotic energy similar to that which formed the first barrage dragons. If the dragon does not find a way to shed its arcane energy, it eventually coalesces into a new barrage dragon. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Barrage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4111) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 106 **Perception** +16; ironsense (precise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +18 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 Ironsense The dragon can detect the presence of iron and other ferrous metals as a precise sense at the listed range. --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 **HP** 140 **Immunities** [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Arcane Whirlwind** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610)) 15 feet. A vortex of fist-sized balls of pure magic swirls around the dragon. Air within the emanation is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for Flying creatures other than the dragon. A creature that enters the aura or begins its turn within the aura takes 4d6 force damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
**Shrapnel Splash** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee weapon or unarmed attack; **Effect** The dragon's blood forms into hard spheres as it erupts from the wound, battering the attacker. The creature who dealt the triggering damage takes 5d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +19 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing **Melee** tail +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** force ball +19 ([Force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d10+6 force **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Scattershot. **Force Pulse** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=666)) The dragon emits pulses of energy that radiates outward in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 1d6+1 force damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). For each action spent, a new pulse of energy is created and each subsequent pulse after the first is 5 feet larger than the previous one. **Scattershot** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=666)) The dragon exhales a cloud of small balls of pure energy that deal 5d4+5 force damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) with no save. The dragon can't use Scattershot again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Barrage Dragon

markdown:
Bog dragons haunt the parables of old, their primal characteristics a raw expression of a primeval world that rebukes the tyranny of time's progression. Their bodies are those of a belly-dragging thing, corpulent and richly shielded by a scabrous hide of spurs and spines. Their claws, much like their wings, are feeble things, vestiges of a more graceful draconic concept that only the eldest bog archdragon might attain. Their faces are masks of terror; their lips curl back in a snarl to expose brutish grins of dagger-like fangs, pock-marked by vicious stomach acids that give their latent breath the malodorous turpitude that stings the senses almost unto dizziness. markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Young Brine Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4119)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Because of this harsh rearing, brine dragons see obedience to superior knowledge as the proper way of the world. This naturally led to a gerontocratic system of governance; humanoids obey brine dragons, and younger dragons at least pretend to listen to elder dragons, with the elemental lord [Kelizandri, the Brackish Emperor,](/Deities.aspx?ID=690) serving as the head of the Brackish Empire. Once a dragon reaches adulthood, they generally believe they know their subjects best and begin discarding the opinions of everyone save for Kelizandri. Adult brine dragons who don't rule a fiefdom or other settlement are rare; most have both the desire to impose their will on the world and the power to force a civilization to follow their orders. This desire to rule creates sovereigns that span the political gamut, from beneficent rulers seeking to optimize the safety and happiness of their subjects to tyrants who view their citizens as ignorant children who would die without their dragon's wisdom.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24

**[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24

Regent's Glower <actions string="Reaction" /> ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon focuses their weighty gaze upon their assailant, filling their foe with dread. The triggering creature takes 4d6 mental damage with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. Any creature who fails the save also becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, fly 90 feet, swim 120 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),

**Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning

<actions string="Single Action" />
caustic spit +18 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)),

**Crushing Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon spews a 50-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of pressurized water that deals 8d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that fails the save is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Crushing Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Crushing Breath.

**Pressurized Pool** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon expels a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) of pressurized water within 120 feet that lasts for 1 minute. A creature other than a brine dragon that enters or begins their turn within a burst must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) until the beginning of their next turn.

</column>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Adult Brine Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4120)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Because of this harsh rearing, brine dragons see obedience to superior knowledge as the proper way of the world. This naturally led to a gerontocratic system of governance; humanoids obey brine dragons, and younger dragons at least pretend to listen to elder dragons, with the elemental lord [Kelizandri, the Brackish Emperor,](/Deities.aspx?ID=690) serving as the head of the Brackish Empire. Once a dragon reaches adulthood, they generally believe they know their subjects best and begin discarding the opinions of everyone save for Kelizandri. Adult brine dragons who don't rule a fiefdom or other settlement are rare; most have both the desire to impose their will on the world and the power to force a civilization to follow their orders. This desire to rule creates sovereigns that span the political gamut, from beneficent rulers seeking to optimize the safety and happiness of their subjects to tyrants who view their citizens as ignorant children who would die without their dragon's wisdom.

A creature that fails the save is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Crushing Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Crushing Breath.

**Pressurized Pool** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon expels a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) of pressurized water within 120 feet that lasts for 1 minute. A creature other than a brine dragon that enters or begins their turn within a burst must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save or become [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) until the beginning of their next turn.

**Tidal Surge** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon sweeps their wings, creating a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of buffeting water that deals 8d8 bludgeoning damage with a DC 32 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. Each unattended object and each creature that fails the save is also pushed away from the dragon 10 feet (20 feet on a critical failure) and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The dragon can't use Tidal Surge again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

This desire to rule creates sovereigns that span the political gamut, from beneficent rulers seeking to optimize the safety and happiness of their subjects to tyrants who view their citizens as ignorant children who would die without their dragon's wisdom. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 45 **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Brine Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4122) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 115 **Perception** +36; (38 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) aquatic echolocation 120 feet, [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +40, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +34, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +38, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +34, [Settlement Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +38, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +32 **Str** +11 **Dex** +6 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 Aquatic Echolocation A brine dragon can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range, but only underwater. --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +33 **Will** +35 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 460 **Immunities** [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524) 15, [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610) 15 Decompression Sickness ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 20 feet. After the dragon uses their Crushing Breath, the pressure of the depths surrounds them for 1 round. Any creature that leaves the aura during this time must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or become dizzy and unbalanced from the sudden change in pressure, treating all squares as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) until the end of their next turn. [Incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures are immune to this aura.
Regent's Glower ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit with a melee attack; **Effect** The dragon focuses their weighty gaze upon their assailant, filling their foe with dread. The triggering creature takes 9d6 mental damage with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. Any creature who fails the save also becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure). --- **Speed** 40 feet, swim 180 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+21 piercing plus benthic bite **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+21 slashing **Melee** tail +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+21 bludgeoning **Ranged** caustic spit +36 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12+10 acid **Bathygenic Barrage** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon uses Pressurized Pool three times. None of the bursts can overlap. **Benthic Bite** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) A creature who takes damage from a brine dragon's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 39 Fortitude save or be [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 3 for 1 round. **Crushing Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon spews a 120-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) of pressurized water that deals 21d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that fails the save is also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. The dragon can't use Crushing Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Crushing Breath. **Pressurized Pool** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon expels a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) of pressurized water within 120 feet that lasts for 1 minute. A creature other than a brine dragon that enters or begins their turn within a burst must succeed at a DC 42 Fortitude save or become [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=70) until the beginning of their next turn. **Tidal Surge** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon sweeps their wings, creating a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of buffeting water that deals 14d8 bludgeoning damage with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. Each unattended object and each creature that fails the save is also pushed away from the dragon 20 feet (30 feet on a critical failure) and is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). The dragon can't use Tidal Surge again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Brine Archdragon

<title level="1" pfs="">[Young Cloud Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4123)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Cloud dragons are the primal dragons of sky and mist. Long, long ago, in the days before folks wrote things down with any sort of consistency, cloud dragons might have been visitors to Golarion, hailing first from the endless air beyond reality. If so, they've settled in quite comfortably. Cloud dragons dwell in the high places of the world, nesting atop the peaks of mountains or the tallest of trees of alpine forests. They revel in flight even more than other dragons, and fly higher than most any of their kin, to the places where the air grows thin and the sky takes on the shades of night. They travel often and journey far.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 10">[Young Cloud Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4123)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=527" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 118

**Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +16

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +7

**Dex** +2

**Con** +5

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +3
</row>

**Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a cloud dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from [fog](/Rules.aspx?ID=2815) and [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 30 

**Fort** +19 

**Ref** +17 

**Will** +21 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 175

</row>

**Immunities**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 26

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, fly 140 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +23 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+13 piercing plus 1d12 electricity

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+13 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+13 bludgeoning

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
horn +21 ([deadly d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 1d12+13 piercing

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 29
- **1st**
[Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) (at will)

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   The dragon recharges their Thundercloud Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

**Dragonmist** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon conjures up a sudden mist in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet. All creatures within the mist become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the mist become concealed to creatures within it. The dragon can use this action again to [dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the mist—anyone else attempting to remove the dragonmist (via [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) or similar) must succeed at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check. A young dragon can have up to three clouds of dragonmist active at a time.

**Thundercloud Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon breathes a thundercloud that deals 11d6 electricity damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Thundercloud Breath again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-637" />
name: Young Cloud Dragon

summary_markdown: Cloud dragons are the primal dragons of sky and mist. Long, long ago, in the days before folks wrote things down with any sort of consistency, cloud …

markdown:
Cloud dragons are the primal dragons of sky and mist. Long, long ago, in the days before folks wrote things down with any sort of consistency, cloud dragons might have been visitors to Golarion, hailing first from the endless air beyond reality. If so, they've settled in quite comfortably. Cloud dragons dwell in the high places of the world, nesting atop the peaks of mountains or the tallest of trees of alpine forests. They revel in flight even more than other dragons, and fly higher than most any of their kin, to the places where the air grows thin and the sky takes on the shades of night. They travel often and journey far. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Cloud Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4126) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 119 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +40, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +40, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +40, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +38, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +37, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +40 **Str** +11 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +8 **Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a cloud dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from [fog](/Rules.aspx?ID=2815) and [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606). --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +39 **Ref** +36 **Will** +43 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=27) **HP** 475 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 42
**Master of Clouds** **Trigger** The dragon rolls initiative; **Effect** A cloud dragon controls their element as easily as they breathe. The dragon uses Dragonmist four times. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 160 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)), **Damage** 4d10+20 piercing plus 2d12 electricity and 4d6 sonic **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 slashing **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 5d12+20 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +38 ([deadly d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+20 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 45 - **7th** [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) (at will), [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) (at will), [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) (at will) - **8th** [Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) - **10th** [Wrathful Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1759) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Thundercloud Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Dragonmist** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon conjures up a sudden mist in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 120 feet. All creatures within the mist become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62), and all creatures outside the mist become concealed to creatures within it. The dragon can use this action again to [dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the mist—anyone else attempting to remove the dragonmist (via [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) or similar) must succeed at a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) check. An archdragon dragon can have up to six clouds of dragonmist active at a time. **Mist Step** ([Teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Requirements** The dragon is within a cloud of dragonmist; **Effect** The dragon dissolves into wisps of cloud, reforming within another cloud of dragonmist within 1 mile. **Shapes in the Fog** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) With a cloud dragon's mastery of their element, creating shapes and images in a fog is child's play. All creatures within or adjacent to one cloud of dragonmist must attempt a DC 45 Will save or be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round (on a critical failure, they are also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1). **Thundercloud Breath** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon breathes a thundercloud that deals 23d6 electricity damage in a 70-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Thundercloud Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Toothsome Air** An archdragon's mist becomes akin to an extension of their body, to be shaped into fangs and claws. Their jaws and claw Strikes can originate from any square of dragonmist that's within 100 feet of the dragon.
name: Cloud Archdragon

<title level="1" pfs="">[Young Crystal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4127)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Crystal dragons consider themselves among the more beautiful dragon species and expect others to recognize this. They see little need to accessorize their crystalline hides but might spend hours each day tending to their appearance. They clean themselves frequently, and each scale must be properly faceted and free of imperfections. A crystal dragon may retreat into its lair if its hide gets damaged, manually removing the damaged scales (a painful process!) and then waiting for the replacements to grow in.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 23

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 7">[Young Crystal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4127)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=585" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=676" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 122

**Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +13, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +15, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +13, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +14, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +11

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +2

**Con** +4

**Int** +2

**Wis** +3

**Cha** +4
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 25 

**Fort** +17 

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +16 

+2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**Immunities**
[fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696)

Shielding Stone <actions string="Reaction" /> **Trigger** A creature at least 10 feet away from the dragon targets them with an attack; **Requirements** The dragon is on the ground and there is at least one unoccupied square between themself and the attacker; **Effect** The dragon conjures a thin slab of stone in an unoccupied square between themself and the attacker. The dragon gains standard [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against the triggering attack. The stone falls on the first person other than the dragon who enters that square, dealing 4d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 22 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. Otherwise, it crumbles harmlessly after 1 round.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d6+9 slashing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +15 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+9 bludgeoning

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   The dragon recharges their Ornamental Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

**Ornamental Breath** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon exhales a torrent of shattered rocks and minerals in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) dealing 7d6 piercing damage (DC 25 Reflex save). A creature who fails the save is weighed down by the stones, becoming [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical failure).

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-638" />
name: Young Crystal Dragon

A crystal dragon may retreat into its lair if its hide gets damaged, manually removing the damaged scales (a painful process!) and then waiting for the replacements to grow in. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 44 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 42 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 [Crystal Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4130) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 123 **Perception** +35; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, [tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +33, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +40, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +35, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +36, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +35, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +36, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +33 **Str** +10 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 43 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +29 **Will** +33 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 350 **Immunities** [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** physical 15 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915)) Cobblegems ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 10 feet. The dragon constantly sheds crystalline scales onto the ground around it. The area is [uneven ground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2370) (DC 38).
Shimmering Armor When the dragon takes a critical hit from any attack that deals physical damage, its hide chips, releasing sharp slivers. All creatures in a 15-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 16d8 piercing damage with a DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save.
Shielding Stone **Trigger** A creature at least 10 feet away from the dragon targets them with an attack; **Requirements** The dragon is on the ground and there is at least one unoccupied square between themself and the attacker; **Effect** The dragon conjures a thin slab of stone in an unoccupied square between themself and the attacker. The dragon gains standard [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) against the triggering attack. The stone falls on the first person other than the dragon who enters that square, dealing 9d6 bludgeoning damage with a DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. Otherwise, it crumbles harmlessly after 1 round. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 180 feet **Melee** jaws +37 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 6d12+8 piercing **Melee** claw +37 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 6d8+8 slashing damage plus crystallize **Melee** tail +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 8d8+8 bludgeoning plus crystallize **Crystallize** ([Incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The claws of an ancient crystal dragon embed shards that slowly turns the flesh of those they Strike to crystal. The target must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure) for 1 minute. Further failed saves against crystallize increase the slowed condition. Once a creature's actions are reduced to 0 by crystallize, that creature becomes [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87), turned into crystal instead of stone. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. Draconic Momentum; The dragon recharges their Ornamental Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Lustrous Lunge** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The dragon strikes a pose that makes its hide sparkle in the light. Each creature within 20 feet must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or become [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round. **Ornamental Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon exhales a torrent of shattered rocks and minerals in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) dealing 19d6 piercing damage (DC 41 Reflex save). A creature who fails the save is weighed down by the stones, becoming [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (clumsy 2 on a critical failure). **Shard Spray** **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The crystal archdragon makes 2 tail Strikes, both using its current multiple attack penalty, then spins in a circle, releasing crystalline projectiles. All creatures within a 15- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 9d6 piercing damage (DC 41 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). A creature who critically fails the save is also exposed to crystallize. This counts as two attacks when calculating the dragon's multiple attack penalty.
name: Crystal Archdragon
