markdown:
As residents of the Outer Plane [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), delight dragons are embodiments of the plane's proclivity toward joy, mischief, passion, and spontaneity. Their massive gossamer wings refract the light of a thousand rainbows between translucent layers. Purple-green scales shimmer in opalescent splendor, while a long, sinuous tail flicks in spiral figures, darting to catch the feet out from under distracted observers. A pair of feathery antennae direct the delight dragon to their next source of amusement—whether play, treat, or general amusement, all surely to become a great time in the dragon's presence, whether a plane's denizens know it or not. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Delight Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4131) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 126 **Perception** +22; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, whimsysense (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Elysium Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +21 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 **Lover of the Arts** Checks by or against the dragon to change a creature's attitude using [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) can be attempted with [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead.
**Whimsysense** Delight dragons can sense the presence of laughter, games, celebrations, and sweets within the listed range. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +19 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 165 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 10 Play Scape ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), divine) 15 feet. Delight dragon magic alters their surroundings, turning weapons into toys and spells into stage effects. All Strikes and other damaging effects used in the aura gain the nonlethal trait and are accompanied by magical sound effects. A creature entering or beginning their turn in the aura must attempt a DC 29 Will save.
**Failure** The creature's first damaging effect (such as a Strike) this round does half damage.
**Critical Failure** All the creature's damaging effects this turn do half damage.
Reactive Tail **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The dragon attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716) the triggering creature. This check doesn't count toward the dragon's multiple attack penalty, and its multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this check. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +22 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** tail +20 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning plus 1d8 spirit and [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 29, attack +21 - **3rd** [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659) - **4th** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Breath of Fresh Air** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon unleashes a stream of magical bubbles in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature within the area takes 7d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature who fails the save is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as they give in to the joyful urge to dance, sing, laugh, or admire the world around them. On a critical failure, the creature is slowed 1 for 1 minute and can't use reactions for as long as they are slowed; they can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turn to end the slowed condition early. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge their Breath of Fresh Air.
name: Young Delight Dragon

markdown:
As residents of the Outer Plane [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40), delight dragons are embodiments of the plane's proclivity toward joy, mischief, passion, and spontaneity. Their massive gossamer wings refract the light of a thousand rainbows between translucent layers. Purple-green scales shimmer in opalescent splendor, while a long, sinuous tail flicks in spiral figures, darting to catch the feet out from under distracted observers. A pair of feathery antennae direct the delight dragon to their next source of amusement—whether play, treat, or general amusement, all surely to become a great time in the dragon's presence, whether a plane's denizens know it or not. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Delight Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4134) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 127 **Perception** +40; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, whimsysense (imprecise) 180 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +43, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +38, [Elysium Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +40, [Games Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +40, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +43, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +39 **Str** +6 **Dex** +11 **Con** +7 **Int** +6 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +7 Lover of the Arts Checks by or against the dragon to change a creature's attitude using [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) can be attempted with [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) instead.
Whimsysense Delight dragons can sense the presence of laughter, games, celebrations, and sweets within the listed range. --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +35 **Ref** +40 **Will** +36 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 400 (can't kill fun) **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 20 **Can't Kill Fun** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Once per day, if the delight archdragon would die, it instead automatically resurrects in its most recently visited lair with half its maximum Hit Points.
Play Scape ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), divine) 30 feet. Delight dragon magic alters their surroundings, turning weapons into toys and spells into stage effects. All Strikes and other damaging effects used in the aura gain the nonlethal trait and are accompanied by magical sound effects. A creature entering or beginning their turn in the aura must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Failure** The creature's first damaging effect (such as a Strike) this round does half damage.
**Critical Failure** All the creature's damaging effects this turn do half damage.
Reactive Tail **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's tail uses a [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) action or a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Effect** The dragon attempts an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check to [Trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716) the triggering creature. This check doesn't count toward the dragon's multiple attack penalty, and its multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this check. --- **Speed** 80 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +40 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+14 piercing plus 2d8 spirit **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+14 slashing plus 2d8 spirit **Melee** tail +38 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+14 bludgeoning plus 2d8 spirit and [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 45, attack +37 - **3rd** [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659) (at will) - **4th** [Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732) (at will) - **5th** [Banishment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; self only) - **8th** [Divine Inspiration](/Spells.aspx?ID=1497), [Moment of Renewal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607) - **9th** [Overwhelming Presence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Breath of Fresh Air** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon unleashes a stream of magical bubbles in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature within the area takes 16d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature who fails the save is also [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 as they give in to the joyful urge to dance, sing, laugh, or admire the world around them. On a critical failure, the creature is slowed 1 for 1 minute and can't use reactions for as long as they are slowed; they can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turn to end the slowed condition early. **Cloud of Giggles** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The delight dragon transforms into a Huge sparkling cloud of gas for 10 minutes. During this time, the dragon gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 20 to physical damage and is immune to precision damage, but they can't cast spells, activate items, or use Strikes or actions that have the [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) or [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait. They can only use their fly Speed, can slip through tiny cracks, and occupy the same space as other creatures. If the dragon ends their turn sharing the same space as other creatures, each of those creatures must attempt a DC 45 Will save or be [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) from a fit of giggles until the end of the dragon's next turn. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this action as long as it has space to take its original size. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge their Breath of Fresh Air. **Let's Play!** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The dragon conjures up a magical arena to play a game, making up rules on the spot. Any creature inside the dragon's aura take 14d6 mental damage from the dragon's baffling explanation (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). Creatures who fail the save are [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (1 minute on a critical failure). Regardless of result, all targets are immune for 24 hours.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Pearl Dragonet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4135)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pearl dragonets are fortune magic incarnate.

 Nobody takes small, round dragonets seriously, but they're lucky to have around. Social and relatively defenseless, pearl dragonets have thrived by providing good luck in exchange for food and protection. This reputation for bringing fortune—which the dragonets encourage—has led to the popularity of good-luck charms featuring this plump dragonet species and the belief that harming a pearl dragonet can incur a lifetime of ill luck.

Pearl dragonets have pearlescent scales and a round body with stumpy legs. Their minute wings cannot support flight, so they rely on magic to fly. They have long tails that can be up to five times the length of their bodies. This odd build exacerbates their ungainly, waddling gait, which is punctuated by head-over-heel rolls and erratic flight paths.

Pearl dragonets are attentive, with an acute ability to understand the world around them. They are commonly found in the mountains of Tian Xia, living close to remote or insular highland communities that show due respect for their insight.

Communities seeking to attract pearl dragonets often hang up round, red paper lanterns, though both the dragonets and the villagers take minor offense toward anyone who compares the relative shape of the dragonets and the lanterns.

([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 17

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 15

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 12
</column>

</column>

The dragonet chooses [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) or [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) when using this ability, which gains the corresponding trait. If the dragonet chooses fortune, all creatures in the mist gain a bit of luck; the first time in the next round each affected creature attempts an attack roll or skill check, they roll twice and use the better result. If the dragonet chooses misfortune, each creature in the mist must attempt a DC 17 Will save. On a failure, the first time in the next round that creature attempts an attack roll or skill check, it must roll twice and use the worse result. The dragonet can't use Fortune Fog again for 1d4 rounds.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Tidepool Dragonet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4136)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Tidepool dragonets are lithe, eel-like sovereigns of their miniature tidal territories. Found in the marine environments that their name suggests, tidepool dragonets have strong jaws adapted to cracking open shells. While shellfish are their main food source, they are adaptive foragers. They'll happily catch and eat octopuses, tidal fish, starfish, sea anemones, and seabirds if the opportunity arises. Their long, webbed claws make them nimble hunters and allow them to reach into rocky recesses. They have muscular wings that work well both above and below water.

Tidepool dragonets are proud bullies who habitually harass wildlife and fishers. They frequently steal brightly painted floats, flags, and shiny hooks to decorate their tidal caves. Prized pieces are reserved for ornamenting themselves, and they cover their scales with shards of glass, necklaces of coral, or crowns of silverware retrieved from wrecks.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Pest Drake Swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4137)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Pest drakes are the feral descendants of valuable racing “pace drakes.” Prized for their colors, speed, and homing ability, pace drakes have been bred, traded, and raced throughout Golarion. Their popularity invariably led to the establishment of feral populations, as these naturally nervous creatures used their homing ability to establish protective flocks. Readily agitated, these rat-sized dragonets are dangerous in large numbers. They are easily overlooked in urban areas, however, as familiarity breeds complacency from those who see these dragonets every day with little incident. 

Many pest drakes are a dun or dusty brown in color, though some flocks retain the brilliant blues, reds, and greens of their captive relatives. Both varieties have many tiny spines that resemble hairs and short, stubby noses that earned them the derogatory nickname of “dragon-winged rats.” Much like other urban animals, pest drakes are highly adaptable, adept at scavenging food from trash piles or discarded foodstuffs. People likewise find the tiny dragons cute and will feed them, despite laws or warnings.

Pest drakes live in cities and ruins. They build their lairs atop hidden stone ledges and within inaccessible monument alcoves.

There, their acidic mess often does irreparable damage before it is discovered and removed. Such lairs are usually full of tiny trinkets swiped off the street, such as earrings, coins, and nails they pull from cart wheels.

<title level="1" pfs="">[Young Executor Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4138)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Executor dragons tend to be aligned with [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), but there are some who would consider themselves otherwise. Gods like [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), and [Trudd](/Deities.aspx?ID=451) all have executor dragon allies, acting as either generals in their godly armies, bodyguards, heralds, or as loyal retainers in the gods' efforts on Golarion. In some instances, executor dragons choose instead to align themselves with the deities who are not of Heaven, but instead have other divine allies. [Psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) speak of one executor dragon that serves [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295) as both bodyguard and pet, gorging itself at the endless feasts that Urgathoa holds nearly daily.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 9">[Young Executor Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4138)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Large" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Dragon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=581" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Draconic Codex
](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 132

**Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet

**Languages**
[Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156)

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Plane Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +6

**Dex** +2

**Con** +3

**Int** +1

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +2
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 27 

**Fort** +18 

**Ref** +16 

**Will** +21 

+2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 155

</row>

**Immunities**
[paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)

**Rage of the Divine** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** Raw divine power flashes through the executor dragon harming anyone who dares strike it so brazenly. The creature who dealt the triggering attack takes 4d8 spirit damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 60 feet, fly 150 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d10+9 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d12+9 bludgeoning

**Divine Innate Spells** DC 28
- **Cantrips (5th)**
[Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971)
- **3rd**
[Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478)
- **5th**
[Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496)

**Divine Utterance** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The dragon screams the truth of the divine, dealing 8d8 sonic damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Divine Utterance again for 1d4 rounds.

**Draconic Frenzy** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order.

**Draconic Momentum**   The dragon recharges their Divine Utterance whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

</column>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-641" />
name: Young Executor Dragon

Executor dragons tend to be aligned with [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), but there are some who would consider themselves otherwise. Gods like [Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=292), [Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=285), and [Trudd](/Deities.aspx?ID=451) all have executor dragon allies, acting as either generals in their godly armies, bodyguards, heralds, or as loyal retainers in the gods' efforts on Golarion. In some instances, executor dragons choose instead to align themselves with the deities who are not of Heaven, but instead have other divine allies. [Psychopomps](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=458) speak of one executor dragon that serves [Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=295) as both bodyguard and pet, gorging itself at the endless feasts that Urgathoa holds nearly daily. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Adult Executor Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4139) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 132 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Plane Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +27, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Divine Sanctification** Once an executor dragon reaches adulthood, they are expected to sanctify as either [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521); they gain the corresponding trait and weakness 10 to the opposing sanctification. --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +23 **Will** +28 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 235 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) **Weaknesses** divine sanctification Aura of Authority ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet. The divine majesty of an adult executor dragon can't be denied. A creature that enters or begins their turn in the aura must attempt a DC 30 Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's aura of authority for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute.
**Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 3 for 10 minutes.
Rage of the Divine ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** Raw divine power flashes through the executor dragon harming anyone who dares strike it so brazenly. The creature who dealt the triggering attack takes 6d8 spirit damage with a DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save. --- **Speed** 70 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519)), **Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning **Divine Innate Spells** DC 33 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Bullhorn](/Spells.aspx?ID=1971) - **5th** [Crisis of Faith](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478) - **7th** [Divine Immolation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1496) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of a Small or Medium humanoid. This does not change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Divine Utterance** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697)) The dragon screams the truth of the divine, dealing 11d8 sonic damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Divine Utterance again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Divine Utterance whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.
name: Adult Executor Dragon

Like the rest of their imperial cousins, the forest dragon likely originated from the elemental plane they embody, namely the [Plane of Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25). That some forest dragons yet reside in the recently returned Eternal Forest supports this theory, though this only answers the location of origin, not how the dragons came to be. The Elemental Lord [Shumunue](/Deities.aspx?ID=695) might resemble a forest dragon, but she is not one herself, so the mystery of the dragons' progenitor remains unanswered to this day. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Recall Knowledge - Elemental](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Forest Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4142) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 136 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Muan](/Languages.aspx?ID=108) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +19 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +16 **Will** +20 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=27) **HP** 180 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Countered by Metal** If the forest dragon takes damage from a metal item or effect with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait, they lose woodland passage and vanishing tracks until the end of their next turn.**Frightful Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 27. Creatures with the [animal](/Traits.aspx?ID=531), [fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), or [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) trait take a –2 circumstance penalty to the save.
Fed by Water ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The forest dragon is targeted with a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) spell or effect; **Effect** The forest dragon gains 30 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet; woodland passage **Melee** jaws +23 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing plus 1d6 poison **Melee** claw +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (animal; fungi; and plants only) - **Constant (1st)** [Vanishing Tracks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737) **Azure Swarm Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon unleashes a swarm of insects that deals 10d6 piercing damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) before dispersing. A creature that critically fails takes 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The dragon can't use Azure Swarm Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures grabbed at a time; one in each of their two front claws, and one with their tail. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d8+6 bludgeoning, DC 29 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Azure Swarm Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Woodland Passage** The forest dragon [ignores difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and greater difficult terrain from non-magical [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.
name: Young Forest Dragon

**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 27. Each living creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 7d10 void damage (DC 34 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Creatures made entirely of water and plant creatures use the outcome one degree of success worse than they rolled. The dragon regains Hit Points equal to half of the highest damage a single creature takes from this effect. All non-creature plant life in the area withers and dies, eliminating non-magical undergrowth and any associated [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372), and [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). **Forest Shape** When casting one with plants, a forest dragon can become a tree of the same size and age as themselves, using their own AC. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 3d8+7 bludgeoning, DC 34 **Woodland Passage** The forest dragon [ignores difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and greater difficult terrain from non-magical [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.

Fed by Water ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** The forest dragon is targeted with a [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) spell or effect; **Effect** The forest dragon gains 60 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 200 feet; woodland passage **Melee** jaws +43 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+20 piercing plus 4d6 poison **Melee** claw +43 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Primal Innate Spells** DC 45 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Know the Way](/Spells.aspx?ID=1582) - **2nd** [Entangling Flora](/Spells.aspx?ID=1515) - **3rd** [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524) (animal; fungi; and plants only) - **4th** [One with Plants](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) - **6th** [Tangling Creepers](/Spells.aspx?ID=1714) - **8th** [Migration](/Spells.aspx?ID=1600) - **Constant (1st)** [Vanishing Tracks](/Spells.aspx?ID=1737) **Azure Swarm Breath** ([Primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon unleashes a swarm of insects that deals 20d6 piercing damage 4d6 [persistent poison](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save) before dispersing. A creature that critically fails takes 1d6 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The dragon can't use Azure Swarm Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures grabbed at a time; one in each of their two front claws, and one with their tail. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Azure Swarm Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Draining Blight** ([Healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon draws moisture from the living creatures surrounding them to heal their own wounds. Each living creature in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes 12d10 void damage (DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). Creatures made entirely of water and plant creatures use the outcome one degree of success worse than they rolled. The dragon regains Hit Points equal to half of the highest damage a single creature takes from this effect. All non-creature plant life in the area withers and dies, eliminating non-magical undergrowth and any associated [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366), [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372), and [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). **Forest Shape** When casting one with plants, a forest dragon can become a tree of the same size and age as themselves, using their own AC. **[**Greater Constrict**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 4d8+10 bludgeoning, DC 45 **Lignifying Bite** ([Plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) When a creature made of flesh is reduced to 0 Hit Points by the dragon's jaws Strike, that creature must attempt a DC 45 Fortitude save. If it fails, it dies, and its flesh, made of earth in a metaphysical sense, turns to wood. The wood is living and might grow into a tree over time, but it can't be used as a body or piece of a body for [_raise dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1645) or other magic that would return the creature to life. **Ultimate Remedy** ([primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon can cure most ailments. The dragon attempts to a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) a [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) afflicting a creature within 30 feet. **Woodland Passage** The forest dragon [ignores difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) and greater difficult terrain from non-magical [plants](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) and [fungi](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), such as bushes, vines, and undergrowth.
name: Forest Archdragon

markdown:
Magma dragons represent a brash and volatile aspect of dragonkind. Territorial and unpredictable, magma dragons are known for protecting their hunting grounds with a fierce and proud demeanor that brooks no challengers. Passionate in the extreme, magma dragons in their draconic forms can often seem utterly bestial, impulsively jumping at threats and challenges with a wild abandon, but this belies an intellect that many underestimate. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Magma Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4146) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 140 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +18 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 175 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 25
Wing Deflection **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon raises a wing, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the dragon is [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), they descend 10 feet after the attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet, burrow 30 feet, fly 100 feet; magma burrow **Melee** jaws +21 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d10+10 slashing **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+10 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d10+10 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 28, attack +18 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **5th** [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) (at will) **Pyroclastic Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon breathes a blast of magma that deals 5d6 fire damage and 3d12 bludgeoning damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Pyroclastic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Pyroclastic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Magma Burrow** A magma dragon's burrow Speed functions only when the dragon is [Burrowing](/Rules.aspx?ID=3373) through magma or molten lava.
name: Young Magma Dragon

If the dragon is [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), they descend 10 feet after the attack. --- **Speed** 50 feet, burrow 50 feet, fly 200 feet; magma burrow **Melee** jaws +40 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Melee** claw +40 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 slashing **Melee** tail +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+20 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+20 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 44, attack +36 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Ignition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565) - **9th** [Breathe Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457) (at will), [Fireball](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) (at will) **Pyroclastic Breath** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon breathes a blast of magma that deals 11d6 fire damage and 6d12 bludgeoning damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Pyroclastic Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Cataclysm** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The magma archdragon releases its primal fury in full. Magma rips from the ground in a 30-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 240 feet. Each creature in the area takes 14d6 fire damage and 4d12 bludgeoning damage, with a DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon takes on the appearance of a Medium humanoid. This does not change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horn Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Pyroclastic Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Magma Burrow** A magma dragon's burrow Speed functions only when the dragon is [Burrowing](/Rules.aspx?ID=3373) through magma or molten lava **Magma Tomb** ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon spits a molten boulder at a target within 120 feet. This deals 14d6 fire damage and 6d12 bludgeoning damage, with a DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. If the creature fails its save, it's encased in magma that instantly cools and has [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) 12, HP 50, and BT 25. The encased creature can't [breathe](/Rules.aspx?ID=2439) and is [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) ([Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) DC 44). **Volcanic Purge** If the next action the dragon uses is Pyroclastic Breath, the magma clings to those it damages. Each creature that fails its save against Pyroclastic Breath takes 6d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and as long as it has this persistent fire damage, it also takes a –10-foot status penalty to its Speeds.
name: Magma Archdragon

markdown:
Wily pranksters and enthusiastic show-offs, mocking dragons amuse their friends and humiliate their foes using trickery, satire, and bombast to disgrace the powerful and lift the spirits of the oppressed. Among the most likely of dragons to interact closely with humanoids, mocking dragons often settle in or near communities where they can easily find targets for their wiles. Mocking dragons are inveterate entertainers, though they're more likely to recite satirical poems or witty quips than compose ballads. Experts in disguise, mocking dragons sometimes change their form to better spy on interesting people, ferret out embarrassing information, or execute elaborate ruses. More serious dragons often view mocking dragons as feckless showboaters, but beneath their jovial exterior, mocking dragons harbor a burning desire to fight injustice. They're easily recruited into campaigns against tyrants, and younger dragons often work to remove gang leaders or landlords that oppress working folk. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Mocking Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4150) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 144 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +18, [Politics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +16, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +16 **Str** +5 **Dex** +3 **Con** +2 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +6 --- **AC** 26 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +19 **Will** +16 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 135 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Graceless Domain ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 90 feet. A creature who enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a DC 23 Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to the graceless domain aura for 1 minute. The dragon can exclude creatures from this aura.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** For 1 round, the first time the creature rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Failure** For 1 minute, the first time each round the creature rolls an Athletics, Acrobatics, or Performance skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Critical Failure** For 1 minute, every time the creature rolls an Athletics, Acrobatics, or Performance skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Jeer at Failure** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A foe within 90 feet becomes prone or critically fails on a Strike; **Effect** The mocking dragon laughs dismissively at the foe, attempting an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the creature. They do not take a penalty if the creature doesn't understand their language. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 90 feet **Melee** jaws +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+9 piercing **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+9 slashing **Melee** tail +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus Vex **Ranged** spit +17 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+6 acid **Occult Innate Spells** DC 26 - **4th** [Blistering Invective](/Spells.aspx?ID=1970) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of any Small, Medium, or Large humanoid, or any Tiny animal. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Hilarious Exhalation** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes their breath in one of two ways. The dragon can't use Hilarious Exhalation again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Laughing Gas** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon breaths a cloud of mood-altering gas in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). This has the effect of [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) (Will DC 26) with a duration of 1 round.
  • **Slippery Puddle** The dragon expels a glob of viscous goo at a solid surface at a range of 60 feet. This creates a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) with the area effect of [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547) (DC 26).
**Insistent Heckling** The dragon can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) targets within 90 feet. Targets become immune to the mocking dragon's attempts to Demoralize them for 1 minute after each attempt. **Vex** **Requirements** The dragon's last action was a successful tail Strike; **Effect** The mocking dragon attempts a [Dirty Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=6472) against the target of their tail Strike. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the creature's multiple attack penalty. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hilarious Exhalation whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.
name: Young Mocking Dragon

**Insistent Heckling** The dragon can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) targets within 90 feet. Targets become immune to the mocking dragon's attempts to Demoralize them for 1 minute after each attempt. Creatures who are Demoralized by the ancient mocking dragon are [Stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 while they are frightened. **Humiliating Mark** On a critical hit, the dragon's claws inflict [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620) on the target as if they had rolled a success. **Improved Vex** **Requirements** The dragon's last action was a successful tail Strike; **Effect** The ancient mocking dragon attempts a [Dirty Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=6472) against the target of their tail Strike. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the creature's multiple attack penalty.
name: Ancient Mocking Dragon

summary_markdown: Wily pranksters and enthusiastic show-offs, mocking dragons amuse their friends and humiliate their foes using trickery, satire, and bombast to …

markdown:
Wily pranksters and enthusiastic show-offs, mocking dragons amuse their friends and humiliate their foes using trickery, satire, and bombast to disgrace the powerful and lift the spirits of the oppressed. Among the most likely of dragons to interact closely with humanoids, mocking dragons often settle in or near communities where they can easily find targets for their wiles. Mocking dragons are inveterate entertainers, though they're more likely to recite satirical poems or witty quips than compose ballads. Experts in disguise, mocking dragons sometimes change their form to better spy on interesting people, ferret out embarrassing information, or execute elaborate ruses. More serious dragons often view mocking dragons as feckless showboaters, but beneath their jovial exterior, mocking dragons harbor a burning desire to fight injustice. They're easily recruited into campaigns against tyrants, and younger dragons often work to remove gang leaders or landlords that oppress working folk. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Mocking Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4153) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 145 **Perception** +39; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +34, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +38, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +38, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +38, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +41, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +41, [Politics Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +36, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +36, [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) +34 **Str** +9 **Dex** +7 **Con** +6 **Int** +7 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +10 --- **AC** 45 **Fort** +33 **Ref** +38 **Will** +35 **HP** 420 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Graceless Domain ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 90 feet. A creature who enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a DC 42 Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to the graceless domain aura for 1 minute. The dragon can exclude creatures from this aura.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** For 1 round, the first time the creature rolls an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36), [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34), or [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Failure** For 1 minute, the first time each round the creature rolls an Athletics, Acrobatics, or Performance skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Critical Failure** For 1 minute, every time the creature rolls an Athletics, Acrobatics, or Performance skill check, it must roll twice and take the worse result.
**Jeer at Failure** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A foe within 90 feet becomes prone or critically fails on a Strike; **Effect** The mocking dragon laughs dismissively at the foe, attempting an [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) check to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the creature. They do not take a penalty if the creature doesn't understand their language. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+19 piercing **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 4d8+19 slashing plus humiliating mark **Melee** tail +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+19 bludgeoning plus Improved Vex **Ranged** spit +36 ([Acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6+14 acid **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42 - **10th** [Blistering Invective](/Spells.aspx?ID=1970) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of any Tiny, Small, Medium, Large, or Huge animal, beast, or humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes, but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Hilarious Exhalation whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Gales of Laughter** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dragon surrounds a creature within 90 feet with cacophonous laughter, [deafening](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) it for 1 round and causing 10d4 [persistent mental damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) unless it succeeds on a DC 42 Will save. **Hilarious Exhalation** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes their breath in one of two ways. The dragon can't use Hilarious Exhalation again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Laughing Gas** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon breaths a cloud of mood-altering gas in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). This has the effect of [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583) (Will DC 42) with a duration of 1 round.
  • **Slippery Puddle** The dragon expels a glob of viscous goo at a solid surface at a range of 60 feet. This creates a 15-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) with the area effect of [_grease_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1547) (DC 42).
**Insistent Heckling** The dragon can [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) targets within 90 feet. Targets become immune to the mocking dragon's attempts to Demoralize them for 1 minute after each attempt. Creatures Demoralized by the ancient mocking dragon are [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 while they are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76). **Humiliating Mark** On a critical hit, the dragon's claws inflict [_outcast's curse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1620) on the target as if they had rolled a success. **Improved Vex** **Requirements** The dragon's last action was a successful tail Strike; **Effect** The ancient mocking dragon attempts a [Dirty Trick](/Feats.aspx?ID=6472) against the target of their tail Strike. This attempt neither applies nor counts toward the creature's multiple attack penalty.
name: Mocking Archdragon

markdown:
Oath dragons are the embodiment of a cause that they pursue with unerring determination. They draw their power not from mere words, but from the meaning and ideals that those words carry. Their dedication has led to the widespread imagery of oath dragons fighting alongside noble champions trying to bring goodness where only corruption is thought to dwell. Yet, there is nothing more terrifying than the power of an oath dragon who swears to a violent cause or who is dragged into damnation by their loyalty. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Young Oath Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4154) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 148 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +22, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +19, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Binding Oath** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon forges a bond with a willing creature over an hour-long ceremony, making them a bonded companion. The dragon can always sense their distance from the companion and if they're moving closer or farther away. A bonded companion automatically critically succeeds on [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) saving throws against the oath dragon's abilities and spells. This link is permanent and neither can form a new link until one member dies. If the dragon or the bonded companion dies, the survivor learns the identity of the killer, but the shock leaves the survivor [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 24 hours and prevents forming a new link for 1 month. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +22 +2 status to all saves vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) **HP** 175 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Heartening Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 30 feet. All allies in the aura gain a +1 status bonus to attack and damage rolls. The dragon's bonded companion also gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 5.
**Shielding Wing** **Trigger** The oath dragon or a bonded companion within 15 feet is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon raises its wing to protect itself or the bonded companion, providing a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. If the dragon is [flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312), it descends 10 feet after the attack is complete. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+11 piercing plus 1d6 spirit **Melee** claw +19 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus 1d6 spirit **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d6 spirit and [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 10 feet **Divine Innate Spells** DC 26, attack +18 - **2nd** [Share Life](/Spells.aspx?ID=1669) (at will) - **3rd** [Ring of Truth](/Spells.aspx?ID=1656) (at will) - **5th** [Spiritual Guardian](/Spells.aspx?ID=1688) **Rituals** DC 26 - **3rd** [Geas](/Rituals.aspx?ID=12) (willing targets only) **Castigating Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) The dragon unleashes a pronouncement that strikes at the soul of its enemies, dealing 7d10 spirit damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The dragon can't use Castigating Breath for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** Whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike, the dragon recharges their Castigating Breath. **Inspiring Word** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) All allies within the dragon's heartening aura become [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) for 1 round. Allies can use this extra action to [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316), [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304), [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), or [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167). **Oathbreaker's Curse** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) If a bonded companion willingly breaks their oath, they retain the benefit of being a bonded companion but are immediately cursed with no initial saving throw. The cursed creature can attempt saving throws as noted to prevent the curse from worsening. **Saving Throw** DC 32 Will; **Effect** The cursed creature is [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67) 1 and can't reduce their doomed value below 1 while they're cursed. The first time the cursed creature encounters the oath dragon each day, they must attempt a saving throw. In addition, each time the creature critically fails a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) or [spell attack](/Rules.aspx?ID=2248) against the oath dragon, they must attempt a saving throw. On a failure, their doomed value increases by 1, to a maximum of doomed 2.
name: Young Oath Dragon

markdown:
Oath dragons are the embodiment of a cause that they pursue with unerring determination. They draw their power not from mere words, but from the meaning and ideals that those words carry. Their dedication has led to the widespread imagery of oath dragons fighting alongside noble champions trying to bring goodness where only corruption is thought to dwell. Yet, there is nothing more terrifying than the power of an oath dragon who swears to a violent cause or who is dragged into damnation by their loyalty. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 49 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 47 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 44 [Oath Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4157) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 149 **Perception** +43; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +37, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +42, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +45, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +42, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +45 **Str** +9 **Dex** +4 **Con** +9 **Int** +6 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +12 **Binding Oath** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon forges a bond with a willing creature over an hour-long ceremony, making them a bonded companion. The dragon can always sense their distance from the companion and if they're moving closer or farther away. A bonded companion automatically critically succeeds on [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) saving throws against the oath dragon's abilities and spells. This link is permanent and neither can form a new link until one member dies. If the dragon or the bonded companion dies, the survivor learns the identity of the killer, but the shock leaves the survivor [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 2 for 24 hours and prevents forming a new link for 1 month. --- **AC** 50 **Fort** +37 **Ref** +34 **Will** +42 +2 status to all saves vs. [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) **HP** 400 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Heartening Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 50 feet. All allies in the aura gain a +2 status bonus to attack and damage rolls. The dragon's bonded companion also gains [fast healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=62) 20.
**Shielding Wing** **Trigger** The oath dragon or a bonded companion within 15 feet is targeted with an attack; **Effect** The dragon raises its wing to protect itself or the bonded companion, providing a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. The roar can be heard up to 1 mile away under the open sky. Any enemy who hears it is cowed by the dragon's resolve and must attempt a DC 45 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and becomes temporarily immune for 1 hour.

On a failure, their doomed value increases by 1, or 2 on a critical failure, to a maximum of doomed 3.

markdown:
Rime dragons are the embodiment of the frigid cold and ice. These hardy creatures have adapted to harsh arctic climates, able to survive indefinitely in sub-zero temperatures. Rime dragons are shades of white and pale blue that perfectly camouflage them in their natural habitat, resembling glaciers or mounds of snow when curled up. They can break through large slabs of ice and dive into frozen waters in search of prey. Due to their natural lower body temperature, they can slow their digestive tract, which allows them to live a long time on little food. Large prey—such as whales, caribou, or moose—offer the best sustenance, but they aren't picky when it's time to eat. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Young Rime Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4158) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 152 **Perception** +16; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, snow vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Architecture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Arctic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +13, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +12, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +11, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +2 **Con** +6 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +2 **Arctic Hunter** A rime dragon's natural camouflage allows them to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=2404) in arctic terrain without cover. A rime dragon also [ignores difficult terrain and greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) from ice and snow and doesn't risk falling when crossing ice.
**Snow Vision** Snow doesn't impair the rime dragon's vision; they ignore concealment from snowfall. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 98 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 5 --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 30 feet **Melee** jaws +14 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+5 piercing plus 1d6 cold and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** claw +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+5 slashing plus 1d6 cold **Melee** tail +12 ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+5 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold **Blizzard Breath** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon breathes out a gust of frost and ice that deals 6d8 cold damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Blizzard Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Blizzard Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Piercing Ice** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The dragon has [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) from Rime Shield; **Effect** The dragon launches the collected ice shards from its scales in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), dealing 3d6 piercing damage and 2d6 cold damage (DC 24 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The dragon then loses the AC bonus and temporary Hit Points from Rime Shield. **Rime Shield** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The rime dragon's last action was to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at least 40 feet; **Effect** Water vapor collected on the dragon's scales quickly freezes over. The dragon gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last until the end of the dragon's next turn.

Rime dragons are the embodiment of the frigid cold and ice. These hardy creatures have adapted to harsh arctic climates, able to survive indefinitely in sub-zero temperatures. Rime dragons are shades of white and pale blue that perfectly camouflage them in their natural habitat, resembling glaciers or mounds of snow when curled up. They can break through large slabs of ice and dive into frozen waters in search of prey. Due to their natural lower body temperature, they can slow their digestive tract, which allows them to live a long time on little food. markdown:
A shining, rune-inscribed scale is all that most people, even adventurers, see of a sage dragon during their lifetimes. Even then, only those familiar with dragon lore will likely recognize the scale as anything other than an elaborate spell scroll. Collectors and scholars of esoteric lore, sage dragons become increasingly reclusive as they age, and, even in their youths, they're prone to work through trusted intermediaries. For those able to locate and gain the trust of such a creature, even the youngest sage dragons are fonts of difficult-to-find knowledge and rare magical techniques. The oldest sage dragons are living combinations of gargantuan libraries and endless grimoires, accumulating the secrets of the Universe, and beyond, in their isolated lairs. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Sage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4162) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 156 **Perception** +21; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +15, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +20, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +17, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +6 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +19 +2 status to all saves vs. spells **HP** 150 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Eldritch Interference ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 90 feet, DC 25. Overlapping fields near the dragon make spellcasting unpredictable. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's eldritch interference for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** For 1 round, when the creature [Casts a Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) that either deals damage or restores Hit Points, they gain a status bonus to the spell's damage or healing equal to the spell's rank.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** For 1 round, when the creature Casts a Spell that either deals damage or restores Hit Points, they take a status penalty to the spell's damage or healing equal to the spell's rank.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.
**Divert Essence** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 90 feet [Casts a Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) that targets the dragon; **Effect** The sage dragon steals energy from the spell to protect itself, gaining [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to damage from spells equal to the triggering spell's rank until the beginning of their next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 60 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 piercing plus sanguine knowledge **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+8 slashing **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d12+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** thoughtbarb +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+4 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **5th** [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Overwhelming Proclamation whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Mystic Facility** The dragon can activate scrolls and wands of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells. **Overwhelming Proclamation** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon exclaims hidden truths that only they have been able to understand, dealing 10d6 mental damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a critical failure, creatures are also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. The dragon can't use Overwhelming Proclamation again for 1d4 rounds. **Sanguine Knowledge** The first time each encounter a dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if the dragon had succeeded at a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about that creature.
name: Young Sage Dragon
