The oldest sage dragons are living combinations of gargantuan libraries and endless grimoires, accumulating the secrets of the Universe, and beyond, in their isolated lairs. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Ancient Sage Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4164) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 157 **Perception** +33; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +29, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +35, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +31, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +31, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +35, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +31, [Scribing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +33 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +4 **Int** +9 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 42 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +31 **Will** +33 +2 status to all saves vs. spells **HP** 330 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Eldritch Interference ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 90 feet, DC 40. Overlapping fields near the dragon make spellcasting unpredictable. A creature that enters or begins its turn in the area must attempt a Will save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's eldritch interference for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** For 1 round, when the creature [Casts a Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) that either deals damage or restores Hit Points, they gain a status bonus to the spell's damage or healing equal to the spell's rank.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** For 1 round, when the creature Casts a Spell that either deals damage or restores Hit Points, they take a status penalty to the spell's damage or healing equal to the spell's rank.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the effect lasts 1 minute.
**Divert Essence** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Trigger** A creature within 90 feet [Casts a Spell](/Rules.aspx?ID=3144) that targets the dragon; **Effect** The sage dragon steals energy from the spell to protect itself, gaining [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to damage from spells equal to the triggering spell's rank until the beginning of their next turn. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 90 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+18 piercing plus sanguine knowledge **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+18 bludgeoning **Ranged** thoughtbarb +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d6+10 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 40 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **5th** [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601) - **7th** [Energy Aegis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512) - **9th** [Detonate Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1488), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710) - **Constant (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Overwhelming Proclamation whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Mystic Facility** The dragon can activate scrolls and wands of any tradition as if the spells they contain were occult spells. **Overwhelming Proclamation** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon exclaims hidden truths that only they have been able to understand, dealing 19d6 mental damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 40 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). On a critical failure, creatures are also [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. The dragon can't use Overwhelming Proclamation again for 1d4 rounds. **Reflexive Understanding** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The dragon was targeted by a spell of 8th rank or lower in the last round; **Effect** The dragon analyzes the spell's inner workings. For the next 10 minutes, the dragon can cast the spell at the same rank as if it were one of their innate occult spells. **Reveal Shame** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The dragon has successfully [Recalled Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about an enemy within 90 feet; **Effect** The dragon utters an embarrassing secret about the creature, unnerving them and their allies. Every enemy within 90 feet that can hear the secret must attempt a DC 40 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round and frightened 3. **Sanguine Knowledge** The first time each encounter a dragon hits and deals damage to a creature with their jaws Strike, they receive information about the creature as if the dragon had succeeded at a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about that creature.
name: Ancient Sage Dragon

A shining, rune-inscribed scale is all that most people, even adventurers, see of a sage dragon during their lifetimes. Even then, only those familiar with dragon lore will likely recognize the scale as anything other than an elaborate spell scroll. Collectors and scholars of esoteric lore, sage dragons become increasingly reclusive as they age, and, even in their youths, they're prone to work through trusted intermediaries. markdown:
Imperial sea dragons are associated with elemental water and oversee rain, storms, rivers, and oceans. They live adjacent to the civilizations of Tian Xia, ready to aid mortal affairs and prove their worth. For some sea dragons, providing assistance in matters of the weather and arcane knowledge is a duty. For others, it's a perk that leads to valuable tributes or delicious meals. Visiting petitioners are all too eager to provide, for it's said that when a sea dragon is pleased, the rain is gentle and plentiful. In contrast, when they're angered, the storm spares none from its fury. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 24 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 [Young Sea Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4166) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 159 **Perception** +15; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +14, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +16, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +15, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +13 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 --- **AC** 26 fed by metal **Fort** +17 **Ref** +18 **Will** +15 **HP** 140 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Countered by Earth** If the sea dragon is targeted by a spell with the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) trait, Liquefy ends and they can't use it until the end of their next turn.
**Fed by Metal** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) When a sea dragon is damaged by a spell or unarmed Strike of a creature with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait, or a metallic weapon, the dragon's scales transform and grant them a +1 item bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 24
**Liquefy** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Trigger** The dragon takes [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage; **Effect** The dragon turns momentarily into water while maintaining their shape. They gain fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 10, including against the triggering effect, until the end of their next turn. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 50 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Melee** jaws +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+11 piercing plus hyponatremia **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 2d8+11 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +20 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+11 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)Grab (Monster Core 359) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 26 - **1st** [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) - **2nd** [Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517) (self only) - **Constant (2nd)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Black Water Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon shoots a ball of water that deals 9d6 bludgeoning damage in a 20- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 40 feet (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). All nonmagical fire in the radius is extinguished. The dragon can't use Black Water Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a third with their tail. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d8+6 bludgeoning, DC 26 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Black Water Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Hyponatremia** ([Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) A living creature that takes damage from the sea dragon's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 26 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 as a surge of excess water floods its body.
name: Young Sea Dragon

The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a third with their tail. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Black Water Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 3d8+6 bludgeoning, DC 30 **Hyponatremia** ([Water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) A living creature that takes damage from the sea dragon's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 32 Fortitude save or become sickened 1 as a surge of excess water floods its body.

markdown:
Imperial sea dragons are associated with elemental water and oversee rain, storms, rivers, and oceans. They live adjacent to the civilizations of Tian Xia, ready to aid mortal affairs and prove their worth. For some sea dragons, providing assistance in matters of the weather and arcane knowledge is a duty. For others, it's a perk that leads to valuable tributes or delicious meals. Visiting petitioners are all too eager to provide, for it's said that when a sea dragon is pleased, the rain is gentle and plentiful. In contrast, when they're angered, the storm spares none from its fury. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Ancient Sea Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4168) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 160 **Perception** +30; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [wavesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=84) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Thalassic](/Languages.aspx?ID=113) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +32, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +33, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +27, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +32, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 39 fed by metal **Fort** +29 **Ref** +32 **Will** +27 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 350 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Countered by Earth** If the sea dragon is targeted by a spell with the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) trait, Liquefy ends and they can't use it until the end of their next turn.
**Fed by Metal** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) When a sea dragon is damaged by a spell or unarmed Strike of a creature with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait, or a metallic weapon, the dragon's scales transform and grant them a +1 item bonus to AC until the start of their next turn.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 36
**Vortex** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) 40 feet. Water in the aura that's also in the same body of water as the dragon is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for Swimming creatures who don't have the water trait.
**Liquefy** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) **Trigger** The dragon takes [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage; **Effect** The dragon turns momentarily into water while maintaining their shape. They gain fire [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 20, including against the triggering effect, until the end of their next turn. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 140 feet, swim 70 feet; [_water walk_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Melee** jaws +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 piercing plus hyponatremia **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +32 ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 38 - **3rd** [Environmental Endurance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517) (self only) - **5th** [Control Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1473), [Create Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476) (at will) - **Constant (2nd)** [Water Walk](/Spells.aspx?ID=1756) **Rituals** DC 38 - **8th** [Control Weather](/Rituals.aspx?ID=116) (doesn't require secondary casters) **Black Water Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) The dragon shoots a ball of water that deals 18d6 bludgeoning damage in a 30- foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 60 feet DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. All nonmagical fire in the radius is extinguished. The dragon can't use Black Water Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a third with their tail. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Black Water Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 3d10+8 bludgeoning, DC 38 **Hyponatremia** ([](/Traits.aspx?ID=732)) A living creature that takes damage from the sea dragon's jaws Strike must succeed at a DC 38 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 2 as a surge of excess water floods its body. Tidal Wave (arcane, manipulate, water); **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon slams their body down, sending a towering wave outward. This wave deals 10d12 bludgeoning damage in a 90-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 38 Reflex save. Structures and unattended objects in the area take full damage with no saving throw.
**Critical Success** The creature takes no damage but is pushed 30 feet from the dragon.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is pushed 60 feet from the dragon.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is pushed 120 feet from the dragon, and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but double damage.
name: Ancient Sea Dragon

markdown:
When the imperial dragons first arrived in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), each was well-attuned to the elemental planes they hailed from. As the war between the [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) raged on and the benevolent lords fell one by one, the [Planes of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) and [Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) sealed themselves away. For the dragons who originated from the Corroded Core, their very existence was now at stake. These dragons flew from mountaintop to mountaintop, seeking refuge from the then-perilous Tian Xia overrun by warring yaoguai. Finally, upon the mountain peaks of Chenlun, Gossamer, Kelsang, Kimu, Kullan, Kyojin, and the Wall of Heaven, celestial beings answered the dragons' cries for help. What the dragons no longer had in elemental [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), these celestials provided through divine blessings and essence. With their nature changed, the dragons as a species survived. In time, their proximity to the sky and the deities they serve became their namesake. Their past as elemental metal dragons became but a faded memory and creation myth for all but the sky dragons themselves to remember. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Sky Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4170) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 164 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), mist vision, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +20, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +18, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Mist Vision** Fog and mist don't impair a sky dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from [fog](/WeatherHazards.aspx?ID=4) and [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606). --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +16 **Will** +21 **HP** 155 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Countered by Fire** If the sky dragon takes [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage, the elemental magic of metal within them is tempered. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and AC, and their jaws Strikes don't deal electricity damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Steel Lightning Breath.
**Fed by Earth** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a sky dragon is targeted by an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) spell or effect, they crystallize the minerals in their scales to create a light-bending or light-scattering layer for camouflage. For 1 minute, the dragon is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while flying.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 26 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+12 piercing plus 1d12 electricity **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d4+12 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 28 - **1st** [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) - **2nd** [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of its front claws, and a last with their tail. **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d6+6 bludgeoning, DC 26 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Steel Lightning Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Perfected Flight** A sky dragon can hover in place without spending an action, and they automatically succeed at all [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=2371). **Steel Lightning Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586)) The dragon shoots a ball of electricity that deals 5d12 electricity damage in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 40 feet (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Steel Lightning Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Sky Dragon

When the imperial dragons first arrived in [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), each was well-attuned to the elemental planes they hailed from. As the war between the [Elemental Lords](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=8) raged on and the benevolent lords fell one by one, the [Planes of Metal](/Planes.aspx?ID=24) and [Wood](/Planes.aspx?ID=25) sealed themselves away. For the dragons who originated from the Corroded Core, their very existence was now at stake. These dragons flew from mountaintop to mountaintop, seeking refuge from the then-perilous Tian Xia overrun by warring yaoguai. Finally, upon the mountain peaks of Chenlun, Gossamer, Kelsang, Kimu, Kullan, Kyojin, and the Wall of Heaven, celestial beings answered the dragons' cries for help. What the dragons no longer had in elemental [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505), these celestials provided through divine blessings and essence. With their nature changed, the dragons as a species survived. In time, their proximity to the sky and the deities they serve became their namesake. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls and AC, and their jaws Strikes don't deal electricity damage. **Fed by Earth** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) When a sky dragon is targeted by an [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) spell or effect, they crystallize the minerals in their scales to create a light-bending or light-scattering layer for camouflage. For 1 minute, the dragon is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) while flying.
**[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 38 --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 160 feet **Melee** jaws +35 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 piercing plus 3d12 electricity **Melee** claw +35 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +33 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Divine Innate Spells** DC 40 - **1st** [Gentle Landing](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [Gust of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550) - **4th** [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577) **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. They can't use Steel Lightning Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Stunning Electricity** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) The dragon rapidly coils, then unleashes crackling lightning that deals 5d12 electricity damage in a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Each creature in the area must attempt a DC 40 Fortitude save; regardless of the outcome, the creature then becomes temporarily immune for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, is stunned for 1 round, and can't [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) for 4 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is stunned for 2 rounds, and can't fly for 1 minute.
name: Ancient Sky Dragon

markdown:
There's no clear explanation for why sovereign dragons are the way they are today. Some guess it might have been a deal to fulfill personal ambition, to go from those tasked with selecting rulers to becoming the rulers themselves. Others wonder if the lands of Tian Xia were corrupted in the past, and the earth dragons walked away from their elemental natures to save themselves. Perhaps they too became [empyreal dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399), but then returned to the lands of mortals when no suitable ruler rose to the throne. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Young Sovereign Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4174) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 168 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), sanctified sight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +21, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +18, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +18, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +20 **Str** +7 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +6 **Sanctified Sight** The dragon can automatically detect if a creature, effect, or item is [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), and whether it’s [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). --- **AC** 31 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +18 **Will** +24 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 195 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 28
Violent Retort **Trigger** A creature within the sovereign dragon's reach critically hits the dragon; **Effect** The sovereign dragon makes a claw or tail Strike against the creature, after applying all the effects of the critical hit to the dragon. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+13 piercing plus 1d6 mental **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+13 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+13 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **6th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of any Large or smaller humanoid. summary_markdown: There's no clear explanation for why sovereign dragons are the way they are today. Some guess it might have been a deal to fulfill personal ambition, …

markdown:
There's no clear explanation for why sovereign dragons are the way they are today. Some guess it might have been a deal to fulfill personal ambition, to go from those tasked with selecting rulers to becoming the rulers themselves. Others wonder if the lands of Tian Xia were corrupted in the past, and the earth dragons walked away from their elemental natures to save themselves. Perhaps they too became [empyreal dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=399), but then returned to the lands of mortals when no suitable ruler rose to the throne. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Adult Sovereign Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4175) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 168 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), sanctified sight, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +31, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +32, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +25, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +27, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +27 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +7 **Sanctified Sight** The dragon can automatically detect if a creature, effect, or item is [sanctified](/Traits.aspx?ID=519), and whether it’s [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521). --- **AC** 37 **Fort** +27 **Ref** +23 **Will** +29 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 275 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 15 **[Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 34
Violent Retort **Trigger** A creature within the sovereign dragon's reach critically hits the dragon; **Effect** The sovereign dragon makes a claw or tail Strike against the creature, after applying all the effects of the critical hit to the dragon. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus 2d6 mental **Melee** claw +30 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 36 - **7th** [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) (at will) - **8th** [Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693), [Wall of Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The dragon takes on the appearance of any Large or smaller humanoid. This doesn't change their Speed or attack and damage bonuses with their Strikes but might change the damage type their Strikes deal (typically to bludgeoning). **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a third with their tail. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Sovereign's Rebuke whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Gleaming Armor** ([Light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** The dragon's armor glows with a golden light. The dragon gains a +2 status bonus to AC and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524), [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), and [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) damage. Each enemy in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must succeed at a DC 36 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65). All these effects last until the end of the dragon's next turn. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 3d10+8 bludgeoning, DC 36 **Inspire Envoy** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon chooses a mortal and offers their august blessing in exchange for the creature carrying out a specific directive, such as defeating a tyrant or protecting a sacred site. If the creature agrees, until its next daily preparations, it gains a +1 status bonus to the dragon's choice of attack rolls, AC, or all of the following: Perception, Will saves, and Charisma-based skill checks. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this benefit, which they're swift to do if the mortal dares to defy the dragon's directive. **Sovereign's Rebuke** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes a roar charged with psychic energy, dealing 16d6 mental damage in a 40-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 36 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The dragon can't use Sovereign's Rebuke again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Adult Sovereign Dragon

All these effects last until the end of the dragon's next turn. **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 4d10+12 bludgeoning, DC 46 **Inspire Envoy** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon chooses a mortal and offers their august blessing in exchange for the creature carrying out a specific directive, such as defeating a tyrant or protecting a sacred site. If the creature agrees, until its next daily preparations, it gains a +3 status bonus to the dragon's choice of attack rolls, AC, or all of the following: Perception, Will saves, and Charisma-based skill checks. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) this benefit, which they're swift to do if the mortal dares to defy the dragon's directive. **Sovereign's Rebuke** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon unleashes a roar charged with psychic energy, dealing 24d6 mental damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature who fails their save is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round (or [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by the dragon for 1 round on critical failure). The dragon can't use Sovereign's Rebuke again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Sovereign Archdragon

markdown:
Heralds of—and heralded by—the primal forces of thunder and lightning, stormcrown dragons meld the majesty of fierce storms and mighty dragons. Driven by emotion and curiosity, these mercurial dragons can change from friend to foe or threatening to thoughtful in mere moments. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Stormcrown Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4178) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 171 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), motionsense (precise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +16 **Str** +4 **Dex** +6 **Con** +3 **Int** +3 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +2 Motionsense A stormcrown dragon can detect the movement of nearby creatures through vibration and air movement.This precise sense works only against creatures and objects that moved in the previous round. --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +21 **Will** +15 +2 status to all saves vs. [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676) **HP** 155 **Immunities** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65), [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 10 **Weaknesses** [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) 10 [**Frightful Presence**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet, DC 25
**Superconductor** **Trigger** The dragon is targeted by an attack or spell; **Effect** The dragon uses the displaced air or magical trajectory of the attack as a channel for its electrical energy. The dragon attempts a storm bolt Strike against the attacker, regardless of range. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +21 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+10 piercing **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)), **Damage** 1d10+10 bludgeoning plus 2d6 sonic **Melee** antler +16 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 piercing plus 2d12 electricity **Ranged** storm bolt +21 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d12+6 electricity **Primal Innate Spells** DC 28 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Gale Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1994) - **3rd** [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) **Coronal Cloak** When the dragon spends an action to Hover while in stormy weather, they become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) until they [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) or until the beginning of their next turn, whichever comes first. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Galvanic Barrage whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Galvanic Barrage** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon exhales a massive bolt of lightning at one creature within 100 feet, dealing 5d12 electricity damage with a DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The lightning then arcs to a creature of the dragon's choice within 30 feet of the previous target with the same effect. The dragon can't target the same creature more than once, and they must have [line of effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2382) to all targets. The bolt can continue to arc this way until the dragon runs out of targets or until a target critically succeeds at their save. The dragon can't use Galvanic Barrage again for 1d4 rounds. **Smooth Flying** The stormcrown dragon doesn't fall if they don't [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) during their turn, and they're unaffected by [strong winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=2821) or similar effects unless they choose to be.
name: Young Stormcrown Dragon

The dragon attempts a storm bolt Strike against the attacker, regardless of range. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +41 ([Finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 piercing **Melee** claw +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 slashing **Melee** tail +39 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)), **Damage** 3d10+18 bludgeoning plus 3d6 sonic **Melee** antler +37 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+18 piercing plus 2d12 electricity **Ranged** storm bolt +41 ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d12+10 electricity **Primal Innate Spells** DC 44 - **Cantrips (9th)** [Gale Blast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1994) - **3rd** [Wall of Wind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753) (at will) - **6th** [Cursed Metamorphosis](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479) (at will) - **8th** [Punishing Winds](/Spells.aspx?ID=1643) - **10th** [Nature Incarnate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1612) **Command Lightning** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Requirements** The dragon's last action was to use Draconic Frenzy or [Cast a Spell](/Actions.aspx?ID=2734) with the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=527) or [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) trait and they're in an area of stormy weather; **Effect** The dragon directs a bolt of lightning at a creature within 100 feet, dealing 5d12 electricity damage with a DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save **Coronal Cloak** When the dragon spends an action to Hover while in stormy weather, they become [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) until they [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) or until the beginning of their next turn, whichever comes first. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Galvanic Barrage whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Furious Cloudburst** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** The dragon must be outdoors and aboveground; **Effect** The dragon exhales a massive bolt of lightning directly upward to create a terrible storm centered on the dragon. [Strong winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=2821) blow in a 120- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) that lasts for 10 minutes; this creates [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) for both flying creatures and creatures on the ground. Creatures in the area except for the dragon take a –2 circumstance penalty to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks and a –4 circumstance penalty to ranged attacks due to the wind and rain. This counts as stormy weather for the dragon's Command Lightning and Coronal Cloak, and the dragon can Command Lightning once per turn as a single action (instead of a free action that requires using Draconic Frenzy or Casting a Spell). **Galvanic Barrage** ([Electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The dragon exhales a massive bolt of lightning at one creature within 100 feet, dealing 12d12 electricity damage with a DC 44 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. The lightning then arcs to a creature of the dragon's choice within 60 feet of the previous target with the same effect. The dragon can't target the same creature more than once, and they must have [line of effect](/Rules.aspx?ID=2382) to all targets. The bolt can continue to arc this way until the dragon runs out of targets or until a target critically succeeds at their save. The dragon can't use Galvanic Barrage again for 1d4 rounds. **Smooth Flying** The stormcrown dragon doesn't fall if they don't [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) during their turn, and they're unaffected by [strong winds](/Rules.aspx?ID=2821) or similar effects unless they choose to be. **Storm Howl** Whenever a creature critically fails a save against one of the stormcrown dragon's abilities that deals electricity damage, that creature is also permanently [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66).
name: Stormcrown Archdragon

markdown:
Time dragons are implacable enemies of the sinister [hounds of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=702), also denizens of the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=46). Although hounds of Tindalos also seek to prevent any tampering with the flow of time, time dragons relentlessly hunt down every hound they encounter, despite the apparent common ground between them. No dragon has told the reason for this enmity to outsiders, but scholars speculate that time dragons and hounds of Tindalos might have different, competing opinions for what the proper flow of time should look like. Occasionally, those stalked by the hounds are rescued by time dragons who appear from nowhere to slay their pursuers, although this isn't necessarily a lucky break if the intervening dragon also objects to their activities. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 30 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 [Young Time Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4182) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 176 **Perception** +20; (21 initiative) [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +21, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +24, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +22, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +24, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +18, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +20 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +7 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +24 **Ref** +19 **Will** +22 **HP** 245 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) **Immortal** Time dragons age, but don't die from old age.
**Starflight** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) A time dragon can survive in outer space and fly at incredible speed, taking 3d20 hours to reach a destination within the same solar system or 3d20 days for destinations beyond. They can carry up to six passengers of at least one size category smaller than themself, protecting them from the void of space.
**Decelerating Dodge** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with a ranged Strike or spell attack; **Effect** The dragon slows time to evade, gaining [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+12 slashing **Melee** tail +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (5th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Time Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=2611) - **3rd** [Behold the Weave](/Spells.aspx?ID=2601), [Time Pocket](/Spells.aspx?ID=2610) - **4th** [Morass of Ages](/Spells.aspx?ID=2603) - **5th** [Stagnate Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=2605) - **Constant (5th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge their Timeshift Breath. **Timeshift Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon breathes a blast of temporal energy, warping time in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Creatures in the area must attempt a DC 30 Will save. The dragon can't use Timeshift Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature shifts briefly forward in time, vanishing and reappearing at the beginning of the time dragon's next turn in the same location (or an adjacent space if their former location is occupied), unaware that any time has passed. While timeshifted, time pauses for that creature, and it cannot take actions or be affected by anything else.
name: Young Time Dragon

**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature shifts briefly forward in time, vanishing and reappearing at the beginning of the time dragon's next turn in the same location (or an adjacent space if their former location is occupied), unaware that any time has passed. While timeshifted, time pauses for that creature, and it cannot take actions or be affected by anything else.
name: Ancient Time Dragon

markdown:
Time dragons are implacable enemies of the sinister [hounds of Tindalos](/Monsters.aspx?ID=702), also denizens of the [Dimension of Time](/Planes.aspx?ID=46). Although hounds of Tindalos also seek to prevent any tampering with the flow of time, time dragons relentlessly hunt down every hound they encounter, despite the apparent common ground between them. No dragon has told the reason for this enmity to outsiders, but scholars speculate that time dragons and hounds of Tindalos might have different, competing opinions for what the proper flow of time should look like. Occasionally, those stalked by the hounds are rescued by time dragons who appear from nowhere to slay their pursuers, although this isn't necessarily a lucky break if the intervening dragon also objects to their activities. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Time Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4185) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 177 **Perception** +38; (42 initiative) [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Dwarven](/Languages.aspx?ID=157), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Utopian](/Languages.aspx?ID=36) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +36, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +46, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +39, [Dimension of Time Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +46, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +39, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +40 **Str** +10 **Dex** +7 **Con** +12 **Int** +9 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +6 Time Portal ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per century; **Effect** Over the course of 10 minutes, the time archdragon opens a portal to any point in time and travels through, taking up to 10 creatures along. The portal remains active for 1 week, connecting the two time periods, though only the time archdragon and creatures traveling with them can see or pass through it. --- **AC** 48 **Fort** +44 **Ref** +37 **Will** +40 **HP** 570 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) Chronowarp Aura ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 30 feet. Time shifts strangely in the presence of a time archdragon. Each creature starting their turn in the aura must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save.
**Success** The creature is unaffected and temporarily immune for 24 hours.
**Failure** The creature's age advances to elderly or regresses to youth (determined randomly) for 1 round. If elderly, the creature is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2; if a youth, the creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but enfeebled 3 or clumsy 3.
**Immortal** Time dragons age, but don't die from old age.
**Starflight** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) A time dragon can survive in outer space and fly at incredible speed, taking 3d20 hours to reach a destination within the same solar system or 3d20 days for destinations beyond. They can carry up to six passengers of at least one size category smaller than themself, protecting them from the void of space.
**Decelerating Dodge** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Trigger** The dragon is targeted with a ranged Strike or spell attack; **Effect** The dragon slows time to evade, gaining [concealment](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 60 feet, fly 200 feet **Melee** jaws +43 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 piercing **Melee** claw +43 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 slashing **Melee** tail +41 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+20 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 46 - **Cantrips (10th)** [Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Time Sense](/Spells.aspx?ID=2611) - **3rd** [Behold the Weave](/Spells.aspx?ID=2601), [Time Pocket](/Spells.aspx?ID=2610) (at will) - **4th** [Morass of Ages](/Spells.aspx?ID=2603) - **5th** [Quicken Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=2604), [Stagnate Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=2605) - **6th** [Awaken Entropy](/Spells.aspx?ID=2600) - **7th** [Behold the Weave](/Spells.aspx?ID=2601), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; Dimension of Time and Universe only) - **8th** [Quandary](/Spells.aspx?ID=1644) - **9th** [Bilocation](/Spells.aspx?ID=2566), [Foresight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537) - **10th** [Freeze Time](/Spells.aspx?ID=1538) - **Constant (9th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge their Timeshift Breath. **Second Chance** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per round; **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet (including the time dragon) fails a saving throw or skill check; **Effect** The target rerolls the check, but must use the second result. The target is then temporarily immune to Second Chance for 24 hours. **Temporal Eddies** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) or [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to their Speed, leaving behind afterimages of their past and future selves in each space they move through. Until the end of the dragon's next turn, they can use any of these spaces as the origin of their Strikes, spells, and other abilities. Observers also perceive the dragon in all these spaces simultaneously and must succeed at a DC 15 [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to target the dragon or fail to affect it. A creature can take a [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) action to attempt to identify the dragon's true location with a successful DC 46 Will save. **Timeshift Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The dragon breathes a blast of temporal energy, warping time in a 80-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Creatures in the area must attempt a DC 46 Will save. The dragon can't use Timeshift Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is slowed 2 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature shifts briefly forward in time, vanishing and reappearing at the beginning of the time dragon's next turn in the same location (or an adjacent space if their former location is occupied), unaware that any time has passed. While timeshifted, time pauses for that creature, and it cannot take actions or be affected by anything else.

markdown:
Stories across Golarion warn children about the monsters that dwell in the shadows, but no story captures the reality of the umbral dragon. Natives of [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), these occult dragons are at home in the darkness, using it to their advantage both in and out of battle. Umbral dragons rarely worry about fighting fairly, emerging from hiding to strike unwary foes and using their magical breath without compunction. Similarly, in social situations, they often remain in the shadows, operating through other creatures. Even in direct conversation, they frequently obscure their true intent, preferring to avoid ever being pinned down with a solid answer. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Young Umbral Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4186) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 180 **Perception** +22; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), voidsense (precise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +20, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +23 **Str** +6 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +3 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +3 **Voidsense** The dragon can taste animating void energy, allowing them to sense creatures with [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) as a precise sense. It can also sense [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creatures with void healing, like [shadows](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=469), when they are inside solid objects. --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +22 **Ref** +19 **Will** +22 +2 to all saves vs. [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693) **HP** 195 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) Umbral Pall ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) 90 feet. Shadows surround the dragon. The radius of all light from light sources and the areas of light spells in the aura are halved.
**Pin Shadow** ([shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's claw uses a [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658) action or leaves a square during a move action it's using; **Requirements** The triggering creature is in [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) or [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) and has a shadow; **Effect** The dragon makes a claw Strike against the triggering creature. On a hit, the trigger creature also has its shadow pinned in place, restricting its movement. The creature treats the area of the dragon's aura as [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) until it uses an [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) action to unpin its shadow. If the area was already difficult terrain, the creature treats it as greater difficult terrain. --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 140 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus 1d8 void **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing **Melee** tail +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning **Occult Innate Spells** DC 27 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (×3) **Devour Undead** The dragon's jaws Strikes have the benefit of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840) property rune and deal an additional 3d6 force damage to undead. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges its Tenebrous Breath whenever it scores a critical hit with a Strike. **Tenebrous Breath** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon breathes a blast of darkness that deals 12d6 void damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). [Undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creatures take force damage instead of void damage. The dragon can't use Tenebrous Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Young Umbral Dragon

markdown:
Stories across Golarion warn children about the monsters that dwell in the shadows, but no story captures the reality of the umbral dragon. Natives of [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36), these occult dragons are at home in the darkness, using it to their advantage both in and out of battle. Umbral dragons rarely worry about fighting fairly, emerging from hiding to strike unwary foes and using their magical breath without compunction. Similarly, in social situations, they often remain in the shadows, operating through other creatures. markdown:
Underworld dragons make their homes close to fault lines, active or dormant volcanoes, and regions with natural seismic activities. Locations with hot springs and fume holes are particularly popular, as they provide many alchemical and magical ingredients. Aside from vaults stuffed with treasure, underworld dragon lairs are also laboratories, dedicated to uncovering secrets in cultivation, elements, magic, and any other mysteries of interest. Several underworld dragon families have operated such labs for tens of thousands of years. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 23 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 18 [Young Underworld Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4190) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 184 **Perception** +13; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +11, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +13, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +17, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +10, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +12, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +11 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +3 **Int** +4 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +1 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair an underworld dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +13 **Will** +15 **HP** 115 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) 5 **Countered by Water** If the underworld dragon takes damage from a spell with the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait, the elemental magic of fire within them is momentarily dampened. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, and their jaws Strikes don't deal fire damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Exploding Red Breath.
**Fed by Wood** When an underworld dragon is struck by a weapon made primarily of wood or affected by a spell with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait, the dragon's internal fiery essences are stoked with the added fuel. Their Exploding Red Breath recharges, and the fire damage from the dragon's jaws deals an additional die of damage until the end of the dragon's next turn.
**[**Frightful Presence**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 23 --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 30 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** jaws +18 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus 1d6 fire **Melee** claw +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+8 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+8 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 25 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **2nd** [Everlight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518) **Adamantine Claws** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** one per hour; **Effect** The dragon infuses their body with [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915). For the next minute, their melee Strikes are treated as adamantine and ignore half the [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) of any object hit. **Exploding Red Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The dragon breathes a blast of fire that explodes in a 20-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 40 feet, dealing 7d6 fire damage (DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save also take 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The dragon can't use Exploding Red Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a last with their tail. **[**Constrict**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 2d8+4 bludgeoning, DC 25 **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Exploding Red Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike.

summary_markdown: Underworld dragons make their homes close to fault lines, active or dormant volcanoes, and regions with natural seismic activities. Locations with …

markdown:
Underworld dragons make their homes close to fault lines, active or dormant volcanoes, and regions with natural seismic activities. Locations with hot springs and fume holes are particularly popular, as they provide many alchemical and magical ingredients. Aside from vaults stuffed with treasure, underworld dragon lairs are also laboratories, dedicated to uncovering secrets in cultivation, elements, magic, and any other mysteries of interest. Several underworld dragon families have operated such labs for tens of thousands of years. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 37 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 [Ancient Underworld Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4192) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 184 **Perception** +28; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), scent (imprecise) 60 feet, smoke vision **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Petran](/Languages.aspx?ID=109), [Pyric](/Languages.aspx?ID=110), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +32, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +29, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +29, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +24, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +3 **Con** +6 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Smoke Vision** Smoke doesn't impair an underworld dragon's vision; they ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from smoke. --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +30 **Ref** +25 **Will** +28 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=27) **HP** 295 **Immunities** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585) 15 **Countered by Water** If the underworld dragon takes damage from a spell with the [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait, the elemental magic of fire within them is momentarily dampened. Until the end of their next turn, they take a –1 circumstance penalty to attack rolls, and their jaws Strikes don't deal fire damage. This limitation ends if the dragon uses Exploding Red Breath.
**Fed by Wood** When an underworld dragon is struck by a weapon made primarily of wood or affected by a spell with the [plant](/Traits.aspx?ID=668) or [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) trait, the dragon's internal fiery essences are stoked with the added fuel. Their Exploding Red Breath recharges, and the fire damage from the dragon's jaws deals an additional die of damage until the end of the dragon's next turn.
**[**Frightful Presence**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 35
Sweltering Heat ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) 10 feet. Each creature that ends its turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude saving throw or become [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) while it remains in the aura. --- **Speed** 40 feet, burrow 60 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** jaws +32 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+16 piercing plus 3d6 fire **Melee** claw +32 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+16 slashing and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** tail +30 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 37 - **Cantrips (8th)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485) - **8th** [Everlight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1518), [Wall of Fire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748) **Adamantine Claws** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** one per hour; **Effect** The dragon infuses their body with [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915). For the next minute, their melee Strikes are treated as adamantine and ignore half the [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) of any object hit. **Exploding Red Breath** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The dragon breathes a blast of fire that explodes in a 40-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385) within 75 feet, dealing 22d6 fire damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail the save also take 4d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). The dragon can't use Exploding Red Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Coiling Frenzy** The dragon makes one claw Strike and one tail Strike in either order, each against the same target. If either Strike hits, the dragon can attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target with that limb as a free action. The dragon can have up to three creatures [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) at a time; one in each of their front claws, and a last with their tail. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Exploding Red Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **[**Greater Constrict**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 3d8+9 bludgeoning, DC 37 **Scorch Earth** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The dragon plunges their body into the ground, [Burrows](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) up to their [burrow Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2348), and then scorches the land within a 60-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Creatures underground or on the surface in the area take 8d6 fire damage (DC 37 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). A creature that critically fails also becomes [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute.
name: Ancient Underworld Dragon

Vizier dragons are primarily concerned with the education and cultivation of people. People fascinate vizier dragons in all their riotous, contradictory complexity. Why do some people excel, and others falter and fail? Why does one person become a saint and the next a tyrant? Why do some become legends in their own time, while others labor in ignominy? **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Young Vizier Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4194) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 188 [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +18, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +23, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +21, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +21, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +4 **Int** +3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +20 **Will** +22 +1 status to all saves vs. [magic](/Skills.aspx?ID=27) **HP** 230 **Immunities** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696), [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) **[**Frightful Presence**](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64)** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 27 --- **Speed** 40 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 40 feet **Melee** jaws +24 ([Fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing plus 2d6 fire **Melee** claw +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing **Melee** tail +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+12 bludgeoning **Melee** horns +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 1d12+12 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **3rd** [Humanoid Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560) (at will) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one horns Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Powerful Utterance whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Powerful Utterance** The dragon expresses their power in one of two ways. The dragon can't use Powerful Utterance again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Ardent Proclamation** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), fire) The dragon breathes a blast of flame in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) that deals 12d6 fire damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
  • **Chastising Word** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The dragon chides the targets strictly enough to leave the mind reeling. Each creature within a 30-foot cone must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute (or stupefied 2 on a critical failure).
**Word of Advice** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The dragon is a skilled mentor, and commands its students like a general. An ally within 60 feet of the dragon can [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) once and [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) once, in any order. If it hits, the Strike deals an additional 2d6 fire damage. **Word of Caution** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** Wise people listen when a vizier dragon speaks. An ally within 60 feet of the dragon gains [immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to the vizier dragon's Powerful Utterance and [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to all other damage for 1 round.

Vizier dragons are primarily concerned with the education and cultivation of people. People fascinate vizier dragons in all their riotous, contradictory complexity. Why do some people excel, and others falter and fail? Why does one person become a saint and the next a tyrant?
