markdown:
Vorpal dragons are most notorious for their keen [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) magic. Even the least of these dragons can sever a foe's head in a single lucky stroke. Unusually, however, the subject survives as a bipartite creature: a headless body controlled by a detached head that still thinks and speaks, and which can outlive said body's destruction. It's not uncommon for would-be dragonslayers to be sent home carrying their own heads under one arm as a lesson in humility. The unluckier ones, alas, may be kept as trophies—and, in a very literal sense, conversation pieces—while the dragon devours their greater portion. Some might even wear their favorite severed heads as a macabre necklace. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Young Vorpal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4198) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 192 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +20, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +16, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +18, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +18 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +4 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 28 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +15 **Will** +18 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 150 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), decapitation, [paralysis](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), sleep **Resistances** fire 10, physical 5 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) bludgeoning) **Cutting Rebuke** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's chin blade that the dragon can see makes a Strike against the dragon; **Effect** The dragon makes a chin blade Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 35 feet, fly 100 feet **Melee** chin blade +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus vorpal binding **Melee** claw +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+10 slashing **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 2d12+10 slashing **Ranged** pinion +17 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+7 slashing **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Feast of Blood whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Feast of Blood** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon inhales sharply, pulling blood from wounds and into the dragon's maw. Each creature in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes their current [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) plus 6d6 void damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The dragon gains [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half the greatest amount of void damage dealt to a single creature this way. The dragon can't use Feast of Blood again for 1d4 rounds. **Vorpal Binding** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The vorpal dragon beheads its foes— sometimes without killing them. A vorpal dragon's chin blade Strikes count as having the [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon property rune for the purpose of [weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893). Whenever the vorpal dragon critically succeeds on a chin blade Strike, it deals an additional 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), which can be ended normally.
When a vorpal dragon rolls a natural 20 on a chin blade Strike, the target must attempt a DC 28 Fortitude save; this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. On a failure, the creature is beheaded. A creature beheaded this way does not automatically die; instead, their head and body live on, disconnected but mystically linked, with the head controlling the body from afar. A beheaded creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) as they attempt to coordinate movements from an unusual perspective, or clumsy 4 and [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) if their head can't perceive their body from its location. These conditions are suppressed if the body holds its head, and don't apply to actions performed exclusively by the head, mind, or voice. If the body is killed, the head can live on, but destroying the head kills the body as well. This effect can be reversed by taking a 2-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to firmly reattach the severed head to the correct body. Whether attaching the severed head to a different headless body is effective is at the GM's discretion.
name: Young Vorpal Dragon

Vorpal dragons are most notorious for their keen [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) magic. Even the least of these dragons can sever a foe's head in a single lucky stroke. Unusually, however, the subject survives as a bipartite creature: a headless body controlled by a detached head that still thinks and speaks, and which can outlive said body's destruction. It's not uncommon for would-be dragonslayers to be sent home carrying their own heads under one arm as a lesson in humility. The unluckier ones, alas, may be kept as trophies—and, in a very literal sense, conversation pieces—while the dragon devours their greater portion. Some might even wear their favorite severed heads as a macabre necklace. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 31 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 [Adult Vorpal Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4199) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 192 **Perception** +23; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 90 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Talican](/Languages.aspx?ID=112) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +27, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +20, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +5 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +20 **Will** +23 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 230 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), decapitation, [paralysis](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 15, physical 10 (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2915) bludgeoning) **Cutting Rebuke** **Trigger** A creature within reach of the dragon's chin blade that the dragon can see makes a Strike against the dragon; **Effect** The dragon makes a chin blade Strike against the triggering creature. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** chin blade +27 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing plus vorpal binding **Melee** claw +27 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d6+14 slashing **Melee** tail +25 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [sweep](/Traits.aspx?ID=708)), **Damage** 3d12+14 slashing **Ranged** pinion +23 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d8+10 slashing **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 35 - **4th** [Seal Fate](/Spells.aspx?ID=2024) - **6th** [Field of Razors](/Spells.aspx?ID=1370) **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Feast of Blood whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Feast of Blood** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The dragon inhales sharply, pulling blood from wounds and into the dragon's maw. Each creature in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) takes their current [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) plus 10d6 void damage (DC 33 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). The dragon gains [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) equal to half the greatest amount of void damage dealt to a single creature this way. The dragon can't use Feast of Blood again for 1d4 rounds. **Heads Will Roll!** **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** The vorpal dragon swoops across the battlefield, sweeping their axe-bladed tail through their foes. The dragon [Flies](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) twice; at any point during this movement, the dragon makes up to two chin blade Strikes, each against a different creature within reach. The dragon's multiple attack penalty doesn't increase until all Strikes are made. **Vorpal Binding** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) The vorpal dragon beheads its foes— sometimes without killing them. A vorpal dragon's chin blade Strikes count as having the [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon property rune for the purpose of [weaknesses](/Rules.aspx?ID=2893). Whenever the vorpal dragon critically succeeds on a chin blade Strike, it deals an additional 3d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), which can be ended normally.
When a vorpal dragon rolls a natural 20 on a chin blade Strike, the target must attempt a DC 33 Fortitude save; this has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. On a failure, the creature is beheaded. A creature beheaded this way does not automatically die; instead, their head and body live on, disconnected but mystically linked, with the head controlling the body from afar. A beheaded creature is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 and [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) as they attempt to coordinate movements from an unusual perspective, or clumsy 4 and [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) and [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66) if their head can't perceive their body from its location. These conditions are suppressed if the body holds its head, and don't apply to actions performed exclusively by the head, mind, or voice. If the body is killed, the head can live on, but destroying the head kills the body as well. This effect can be reversed by taking a 2-action activity, which has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, to firmly reattach the severed head to the correct body. Whether attaching the severed head to a different headless body is effective is at the GM's discretion.
name: Adult Vorpal Dragon

markdown:
Canny, patient, and creative, wailing dragons are adepts of sound and psychology. With both their physiology and innate connection to arcane magic allowing them to produce and shape sound waves, these reclusive creatures are heard much more often than seen. Named for their tendency to use uncanny keening vocalizations to communicate across long distances and startle prey, wailing dragons haunt locations that create interesting or useful echoes, such as canyons or caverns. Though ruthless predators when needed, most wailing dragons devote their energy toward investigating the effects sound has on the environment and the bodies and minds of creatures. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Young Wailing Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4202) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 196 **Perception** +17; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), echolocation (precise) 120 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +14, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +11, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +11, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +15, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +14 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +0 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Echolocation** A wailing dragon can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. --- **AC** 23 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +12 +2 status to all saves vs. [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) **HP** 120 **Immunities** [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 10 **Echolocation** A wailing dragon can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range.
Subsonic Drone ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) 90 feet, A creature who enters or begins their turn in the area must attempt a DC 21 Fortitude save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's subsonic drone for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 3.

markdown:
Canny, patient, and creative, wailing dragons are adepts of sound and psychology. With both their physiology and innate connection to arcane magic allowing them to produce and shape sound waves, these reclusive creatures are heard much more often than seen. Named for their tendency to use uncanny keening vocalizations to communicate across long distances and startle prey, wailing dragons haunt locations that create interesting or useful echoes, such as canyons or caverns. Though ruthless predators when needed, most wailing dragons devote their energy toward investigating the effects sound has on the environment and the bodies and minds of creatures. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Adult Wailing Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4203) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 196 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), echolocation (precise) 120 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +19, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +17, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +19, [Music Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +17, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +0 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +5 **Echolocation** A wailing dragon can use its hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +22 **Will** +17 +2 status to all saves vs. [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) and [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) **HP** 215 **Immunities** [deafened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=66), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 15 Subsonic Drone ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) 90 feet, A creature who enters or begins their turn in the area must attempt a DC 26 Fortitude save. Regardless of the result of the saving throw, the creature is temporarily immune to this dragon's subsonic drone for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 3.
**Destructive Interference** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) **Trigger** A foe within 90 feet uses an [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) effect; **Effect** The young wailing dragon releases a burst of noise, attempting to drown out the triggering sound. Anyone that can hear the Eerie Call must attempt a DC 29 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) by the sound. A fascinated creature must spend at least 1 action [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the dragon each turn, and the dragon can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this affect for up to 1 minute. **Natural Mimic** The dragon can imitate any sound or voice that it has heard. This has the effect of 2nd-rank [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (DC 29) but is a nonmagical effect. **Resounding Roar** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=765)) The dragon unleashes a thunderous call that deals 11d6 sonic damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). markdown:
What is a wish? A wish dragon would call it the truest expression of self—each individual wish reveals something of deep importance to the wisher, and no two wishes are ever quite the same. Whether the wish is one of creation or destruction, justice or vengeance, love or hate, wish dragons seek to understand what makes it important to the wisher and view their ability to make it a reality as a gift that helps them make sense of the world. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Young Wish Dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4206) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 200 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +17, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +19, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +20, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +19, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +19 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +4 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 29 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +18 **Will** +21 +2 status to all saves vs. [arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534) **HP** 180 **Immunities** [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Best Wishes** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 20 feet. Being near a wish dragon gives other creatures a greater sense of what they truly wish for, bolstering their sense of purpose while also distracting them from the task at hand. Creatures in the aura take a –1 status penalty to Perception but gain a +2 status bonus to Will saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) effects. The wish dragon can't gain the benefit of their own aura or other actions that use the aura, and they can choose to exclude any creatures from any benefit of the aura or action that uses the aura.
**Wished Away** **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** The dragon wishes not to be hurt by this attack, gaining [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 15 to all damage against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 30 feet, fly 120 feet **Melee** jaws +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+11 piercing **Melee** claw +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d6+11 slashing **Melee** tail +19 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d10+11 bludgeoning **Arcane Innate Spells** DC 29 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **4th** [Cozy Cabin](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), [Creation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477), [Safe Passage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) **Breath of Loss** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The dragon breathes a warm, gentle breeze of mist that creates the feeling of when deepest desires are achieved. As the breeze dissipates, the feeling of loss overwhelms creatures' senses, dealing 11d6 mental damage in a 30-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 29 Will save). A creature that critically fails its save is also consumed with the memory of the moment and is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1. The wish dragon can't use Breath of Loss again for 1d4 rounds. **Destructive Wish** **Requirements** An illusion from Wish Come True is currently active; **Effect** The wish dragon [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the illusion of Wish Come True, which shatters into magical fragments, dealing 6d6 force damage with a DC 29 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save to all creatures in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the illusion. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Breath of Loss whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Wish Come True** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon creates an illusion in an unoccupied square within 60 feet that appears to all other creatures that can see it to be the manifestation of their deepest material desire. This has the effects of [_illusory object_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (though the illusion might appear to be a creature to certain people) that last for 10 minutes. The wish dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the illusion. Each non-wish dragon creature who begins their turn within 200 feet in [line of sight](/Rules.aspx?ID=2383) of the illusion must attempt a DC 29 Will save. If any creature within reach of the illusion [Interacts](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to touch it, the illusion ends and that creature is invigorated, gaining 2d4 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature disbelieves the illusion and becomes temporarily immune to Wish Come True for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 for 1 round.
name: Young Wish Dragon

The wish dragon can't use Breath of Loss again for 1d4 rounds. **Destructive Wish** **Requirements** An illusion from Wish Come True is currently active; **Effect** The wish dragon [Dismisses](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the illusion of Wish Come True, which shatters into magical fragments, dealing 10d6 force damage with a DC 45 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save to all creatures in a 50-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) of the illusion. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges Breath of Loss whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Grant Wish** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per week; **Effect** The wish dragon instantly grants the benefits of a critical success with the [_wish_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=125) ritual to a mortal creature. This has no cost. That creature specifies what they wish for, but the interpretation is up to the wish dragon. A summoned wish dragon can't use this ability. **Wish Come True** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [olfactory](/Traits.aspx?ID=664), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Frequency** once per 10 minutes; **Effect** The dragon creates an illusion in an unoccupied square within 60 feet that appears to all other creatures that can see it to be the manifestation of their deepest material desire. This has the effects of [_illusory object_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (though the illusion might appear to be a creature to certain people) that last for 10 minutes. The wish dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the illusion. Each non-wish dragon creature who begins their turn within 200 feet in [line of sight](/Rules.aspx?ID=2383) of the illusion must attempt a DC 45 Will save. If any creature within reach of the illusion [Interacts](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) to touch it, the illusion ends and that creature is invigorated, gaining 3d6 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The creature disbelieves the illusion and becomes temporarily immune to Wish Come True for 1 hour.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The creature is stupefied 2 for 1 round. **Wish You Would** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Requirements** An [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) from Wish Come True is active; **Effect** The dragon targets one creature within 200 feet currently [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) by Wish Come True, making them believe that the object of their desires is under attack from all those around them and the dragon is the only one they can trust. The target must succeed at a DC 44 Will Save or they become [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) by the dragon for 1 minute. The target may attempt a new save at the end of each of their turns. If the illusion from Wish Come True dissipates, their controlled state immediately ends.
name: Wish Archdragon

markdown:
Wyrmwraiths dwell in remote areas marred by catastrophes and death, where they can better hide their undead nature or indulge in their grim research. Their lairs often have a single, hidden and highly defensible antechamber where the wyrmwraith confers with its agents as it engages in a war with distant rivals through expendable proxies. This dimly lit antechamber is usually filled with death and many undead servants, some raised from the bodies of those whose usefulness has come to an end. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 41 **[Recall Knowledge - Undead](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 41 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 39 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 [Wyrmwraith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4210) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 202 **Perception** +29; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 120 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +30, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +30, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +33, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +33, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +33 **Str** -5 **Dex** +9 **Con** +0 **Int** +7 **Wis** +7 **Cha** +9 --- **AC** 39 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +32 **Will** +35 **HP** 280 ([void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83)) **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision, [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) **Resistances** all damage 20 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [ghost touch](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509); double resistance vs. non-magical) [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 90 feet, DC 36. On a critical failure, a creature is also [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) until the start of their next turn.
Sunlight Powerlessness A wyrmwraith in sunlight is [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 2.
**Vitality Transfer** ([divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Trigger** The wyrmwraith succeeds at a saving throw to resist [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage but still takes damage; **Effect** The wyrmwraith transfers all vitality damage from the effect to a single [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature of their choice within 120 feet that they control or that's [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=75) or [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=78) to them. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 80 feet **Melee** spectral jaws +34 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+17 void **Melee** spectral claw +34 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+17 void plus divine dispelling **Divine Innate Spells** DC 35 - **3rd** [Bind Undead](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449) (at will) - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (×3; undead targets only), [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) - **6th** [Vampiric Exsanguination](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735) - **7th** [Divine Wrath](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499) (×3), [Execute](/Spells.aspx?ID=1519) - **8th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (undead targets only) **Divine Rituals** DC 35 - **2nd** [Create Undead](/Rituals.aspx?ID=117) **Decaying Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) The wyrmwraith unleashes a torrent of void energy that deals 18d6 void damage to all creatures in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 38 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). They can't use Decaying Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Divine Dispelling** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A wyrmwraith's claws rend divine magic. Whenever a wyrmwraith hits a creature with a spectral claws Strike, the wyrmwraith can attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=3280) an ongoing [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) spell effect on the creature. **Draconic Frenzy** The wyrmwraith makes two claw Strikes and one jaws Strike in any order. **Ectoplasmic Form** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) A wyrmwraith can push through the ectoplasmic veil to temporarily assume a physical form made of ectoplasm. The wyrmwraith loses the [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) trait for 1d4 rounds, though they can return to their incorporeal form as a free action before then. Once this ability ends, the wyrmwraith can't use this ability again for 1d4 rounds. While in their ectoplasmic form, the wyrmwraith's AC increases to 43 and they gain 100 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). They lose their immunity to precision and [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) damage and all their resistances. Their jaws and claw Strikes deal piercing and slashing damage, respectively, instead of void damage. **Phase Lurch** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) **Requirements** The wyrmwraith is in their ectoplasmic form; **Effect** The wyrmwraith Strides, but can pass through walls or material obstacles as though they were [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632). They must begin and end their movement outside of any physical obstacles, and passing through solid material is [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). **Void's Embrace** ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) If a victim succeeds at a saving throw against this [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566) while in sunlight, the curse ends. While a creature has this curse, they bypass the resistances of the wyrmwraith that cursed them; **Saving Throw** DC 38 Will; **Stage 1** [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) in any light (1 hour); **Stage 2** gains [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 30 feet but is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) in any light (1 hour); **Stage 3** as stage 2, but also has [void healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=83) (1 hour); **Stage 4** [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) and can't awaken (1 day); **Stage 5** dead and becomes a [wraith](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3243), its body crumbling to ash. **Wings of Shadow** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)) **Frequency** once per round; **Effect** The wyrmwraith spreads its spectral wings wide covering all near it in shadow. Each living creature within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) is exposed to void's embrace.

markdown:
Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons. These dragons are schemers, always looking to manipulate and control others, either for personal gain or simply for the thrill of watching their machinations play out. Conspirator dragons see themselves above others and typically speak with infantilizing tones and words. However, as most conspirator dragons meet others while in disguise, they do their best to maintain their disguise. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 45 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 43 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 [Conspirator Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4212) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 50 **Perception** +36; (38 to [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302)) [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111), [Tien](/Languages.aspx?ID=173) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +36, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +41, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +39, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +39, [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +37, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +37, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +35, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +37, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +6 **Con** +8 **Int** +7 **Wis** +8 **Cha** +11 --- **AC** 44 **Fort** +32 **Ref** +34 **Will** +36 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 450 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) Forgetfulness Aura ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 120 feet. Each creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a DC 42 Will save. On a failure, the creature's memory of the dragon, including any disguise the dragon used, fades away 1 minute after the creature leaves the aura. The memories are filled in with false memories to explain details, such as attributing the dragon's Smoke Breath to a nearby fire or any collateral damage in a settlement to past battles. On a success, the creature becomes immune to the aura for 24 hours. The aura is suppressed while the dragon is wearing their Conjured Disguise.
Retract Body **Trigger** The dragon is hit or critically hit by an attack made by a creature the dragon can see; **Effect** The dragon retracts the targeted body part or twists away to avoid the attack, gaining a +2 circumstance bonus to AC against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 60 feet, climb 60 feet, fly 220 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+18 piercing **Melee** claw +38 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d6+18 slashing **Melee** tail +36 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning **Ranged** mental blast +38 ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 6d6+10 mental plus numbing blast **Occult Innate Spells** DC 42 - **4th** [Charm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463) (at will), [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) (at will) - **9th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [Mind Probe](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [Mind Reading](/Spells.aspx?ID=1602) (at will), [Rewrite Memory](/Spells.aspx?ID=1655) **Conjure Disguise** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The dragon conjures a perfect flesh suit replica of a humanoid they've seen of their size or smaller and compresses themself into it, along with generating appropriate clothing for the humanoid. This process takes 1 minute to complete, during which the dragon is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58). If the dragon stops or is interrupted in this process, the suit is destroyed. Once the process is complete, the dragon can remain in this disguise indefinitely.
The transformation has the effects of [Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55), except that the disguise is not actively magical in nature and doesn't register as magical to detect magic and similar effects. The dragon loses Retract Body while transformed.
If the dragon is critically hit while wearing the disguise, the suit is destroyed and immediately explodes. This has the effects of Detonate Disguise, except that creatures use the outcome one degree of success better than they rolled on their save. **Detonate Disguise** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The dragon is wearing their conjured disguise; **Effect** The dragon erupts from the disguise, destroying it. The explosive revelation deals 21d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) with a DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save. A creature that fails its save is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 round as it becomes covered in scraps from the disguise. Any creature sharing a space with the dragon after they erupt is pushed into the nearest empty space. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one tail Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** When the dragon scores a critical hit with a Strike, they recharge Smoke Breath. **Numbing Blast** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature damaged by the dragon's mental blast is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of the dragon's next turn. **Rushed Transformation** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) **Frequency** once per hour; **Effect** Using the aid of magic and an exhausting amount of effort, the dragon quickly reshapes their body into the form of a generic humanoid figure. This has the effects of humanoid form except that it lasts only 1 minute, and the dragon doesn't gain the +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) as the transformation makes use of the dragon's body to crudely mimic a humanoid form. The dragon can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the effect. Whenever the effect ends, the dragon leaves behind scraps of magically conjured flesh, which could give away the dragon's presence. **Smoke Breath** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) The dragon unleashes a noxious cloud of smoke that deals 18d6 poison damage in a 70- foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save). The smoke remains for 1 minute. This has the effects of [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), except it fills the cone's area. The dragon can't use Smoke Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Sneak Attack** The dragon's Strikes deal an additional 4d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) targets.
name: Conspirator Archdragon

The three major celestial planes—[Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=41), [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=44), and [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=40)—each have their own respective dragons. Empyreal dragons have a direct connection to Heaven. Using the blessings of Heaven, empyreal dragons protect others and intercede against wickedness. Empyreal dragons are wise, considerate, and compassionate. When speaking with others, empyreal dragons are patient and understanding. **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 51 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 49 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 46 [Empyreal Archdragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4213) **Source** [Draconic Codex ](/Sources.aspx?ID=277) pg. 66 **Perception** +40; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) (imprecise) 30 feet, [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Diabolic](/Languages.aspx?ID=117), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165); [_truespeech_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +36, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +43, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +36, [Heaven Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +37, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +36, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +41, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +37, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +35 **Str** +10 **Dex** +8 **Con** +9 **Int** +7 **Wis** +9 **Cha** +8 --- **AC** 49 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +36 **Will** +40 +2 status to all saves vs. [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579) **HP** 450 **Immunities** [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Weaknesses** [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) 20 **Inspiring Presence** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 60 feet. The mere sight of an empyreal dragon motivates other creatures. Creatures within the aura gain a +1 status bonus to saving throws and skill checks. The empyreal dragon can't gain the benefit of their own aura or other actions that use the aura, and they can choose to exclude any creatures from any benefit of the aura or action that uses the aura.
Divine Deflection **Trigger** The dragon is critically hit by an attack; **Effect** Divine power intercedes, preventing some of the damage. The dragon gains [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) 20 to all damage against the triggering attack. --- **Speed** 90 feet, fly 220 feet **Melee** jaws +41 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 piercing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** claws +41 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d8+20 slashing plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** tail +39 ([Holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d10+20 bludgeoning plus 1d8 spirit **Melee** wing +39 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+20 slashing plus 1d8 spirit **Divine Innate Spells** DC 46, attack +38 - **7th** [Holy Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1557) (at will), [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (at will; self only) - **9th** [Heal](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) - **10th** [Manifestation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1592) - **Constant (5th)** [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) **Direct Halo** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The dragon tosses their halo to a square within 90 feet. While the halo is deployed in this way, the dragon loses their inspiring presence aura, and the aura instead [emanates](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) from the halo with the same emanation radius. The dragon can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) to recall the halo from any distance. The halo is made of pure light—it doesn't occupy space and can't be targeted or destroyed in any way. **Draconic Frenzy** The dragon makes two claw Strikes and one wing Strike in any order. **Draconic Momentum** The dragon recharges their Spirit Breath whenever they score a critical hit with a Strike. **Halo Pulse** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) The dragon chooses one effect to impose on creatures in their inspiring presence aura. The dragon can't use Halo Pulse again for 1d4 rounds.
  • **Repulsion** Each creature must succeed at a DC 46 Fortitude save or be [pushed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) back until it's no longer in the aura.
  • **Restoration** ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) Each creature recovers 11d8 Hit Points.
  • **Restriction** ([incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Creatures must succeed at a DC 46 Will save or be [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 (slowed 2 on a critical failure) while they remain within the aura. Regardless of the result, a creature is then temporarily immune to restriction for 1 minute.
**Luminous Form** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The dragon transforms into a being of pure light for 1 minute, shedding bright light in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). While in their heavenly form, the dragon's melee Strikes deal an additional 1d6 fire damage and 1d6 spirit damage. Creatures with the [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) trait that can see the light must make a DC 46 Fortitude save or be [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) for 1 round (blinded for 1 minute on a critical failure); they are then temporarily immune for 1 minute. **Spirit Breath** ([Divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737)) The dragon unleashes a blast of holy fire that deals 19d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage in a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) (DC 46 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The dragon can't use Spirit Breath again for 1d4 rounds.
name: Empyreal Archdragon

Amelekanas are amphibious ambush predators native to rivers and lakes across Castrovel. An amelekana looks similar to a giant snail, with an opalescent spiraling shell and eyeless face sporting a crown of six antennae, but that's where the resemblance ends. Instead of one giant foot, the amelekana has fleshy tubes standing upright through holes in its shell and a host of tiny armored legs, like those of a crab. Yet perhaps the most unusual feature of the amelekana is that it's actually a symbiotic community of two separate organisms, neither of which can live without the other. Like many creatures on Castrovel, amelekanas have some basic psychic ability—in their case, a sense for nearby living creatures. A hunting amelekana submerges itself to obscure its location. When prey draws near, the amelekana rises from the water and releases compressed gases to launch its symbiotes—blobby amoebas the size of a bread loaf—at their victims. These amoebas act as external digesters for the stomachless amelekana, using their acidic coating to dissolve prey and absorb nutrition. Once its amoebas have had their fill, the amelekana slurps them back up, providing the amoeba colony with a safe haven inside its body in exchange for a portion of the processed nutrients. A typical amelekana is 9 feet long and weighs up to 2,000 pounds. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 21 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16 [Amelekana](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4214) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 246 **Perception** +14; [lifesense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=67) 100 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +10, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +10, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12 **Str** +5 **Dex** +1 **Con** +4 **Int** -3 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +14 **HP** 72 **Reflective Shell** An amelekana submerged in water gains a +4 circumstance bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks. If the water is moving, this bonus increases to +8. --- **Speed** 20 feet, swim 20 feet **Melee** pseudopod +13, **Damage** 2d4+8 bludgeoning **Ranged** lob amoeba +14 **Lob Amoeba** The amelekana flings a symbiotic amoeba at a target within 40 feet—this is a ranged attack that doesn't count toward the amelekana's multiple attack penalty, and its multiple attack penalty doesn't apply to this attack.
**Success** The target takes 2d4+8 bludgeoning damage and the symbiotic amoeba lands in a square adjacent to the target.
**Failure** The target takes no damage, but the symbiotic amoeba still spawns in a square adjacent to the target.
**Critical Failure** The target takes no damage and the lobbed amoeba dies on impact. **Symbiotic Amoeba** An amoeba produced by the amelekana is a Tiny, mindless, semi-independent creature controlled by the amelekana. It has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait, AC 15, 1 Hit Point, and the same skills and saving throws as the amelekana. Each round, the amelekana can [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=2724) a Minion to make an amoeba [Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=2304) or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) 20 feet and make a pseudopod Strike (**Melee** pseudopod +13, **Damage** 2d4 acid). If an amelekana is killed, its symbiotic amoebas are also killed by the host creature's psychic backlash. An amelekana can have up to three extant symbiotic amoebas at a time.
Amelekana Shells Amelekana shells’ swirling shine warps light in water to the point where the shells almost disappear, making them quite valuable. Among terrestrial cultures, amelekana shells are often used in jewelry. Athamarus and other aquatic cultures have long sought some way to turn the unusual shells into armor or other gear that might aid in underwater hunting, but few have succeeded so far. [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) a single piece of [stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48)-enhancing amelekana shell armor would be a worthwhile endeavor indeed.
name: Amelekana

<title level="1" pfs="">[Arctic Bloom](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4215)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Arctic blooms (also known as yaiafinetis) grow only during periods of extended twilight, thriving across the cold tundra of the Crown of the World exclusively in the winter months. To survive in such harsh conditions, arctic blooms sprout independent polyps called dusk poppies that detach and then creep across the tundra in search of food for their matriarch. 

Dusk poppies sprout from spores that attach to carcasses, but they only blossom during the long twilight unique to the Crown of the World. The perfect conditions for their development are so rare that arctic blooms have developed a vigorous appetite. They feed unceasingly. Much of their energy goes to developing more polyps, and one plant can easily spawn a small infestation of these deadly bulbs. 

The plants send out runner shoots that quickly develop purple bulbs. These bulbs, the plant's polyps, sprout many long, hooked vines before detaching from the mother plant. Dusk poppies are short lived but active, foraging for a week or so before they shrivel and die. The poppies aren't dangerous unless handled, as they secrete a fine dust that seizes the nervous system of creatures who come in contact with them. 

**Paralyzing Poison** ([poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 19 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 (1 round); **Stage 2** off-guard and clumsy 2 (1 round); **Stage 3** [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) (1 round)

**Pheromones** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542)) 5 miles. Arctic blooms communicate with others of their kind via airborne pheromones. They can use this form of communication to determine their relative distance to each other, call for help if they are under attack or need sustenance, announce that they've detected prey, and convey similar basic concepts. Pheromones coat everything within the aura and allow dusk poppies to perceive with their pheromones.

**Feed** <actions string="Three Actions" />  The arctic bloom draws nutrients from a living creature it has swallowed, regaining 3d8+24 HP. The swallowed creature takes 2d12+8 bludgeoning damage.

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_5_TreasureAndRewards.png">Violet Dusk Poppy Dye</title>

Some natives of the Crown of the World hunt yaiafineti matriarchs and harvest their petals, which are used to create an expensive, glimmering violet dye. Although the polyps are far less useful for this purpose, skilled trackers follow their vine tracks back to their matriarch.
</aside>

<title level="1" pfs="">[Blackfrost Prophet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4217)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

With their hulking physiques and the potent connection to the Crown of the World, saumen kar who succumb to [blackfrost](/Curses.aspx?ID=51) become especially dangerous blackfrost dead. Those who guarded the Temple of Aqakaru in life continue to do so in undeath, albeit even more vigilantly and much more violently. Blackfrost prophets, as these wardens are sometimes known, maintain their formidable intellect and innate connection to their boreal environment, though the curse of blackfrost has stripped them of their memories and filled their hearts with evil.

Capstan swabbies are constructs specially designed for seafaring. Sea captains looking for unwavering loyalty and competence can do far worse than a swabbie. Built to resemble and assume the place of a ship's capstan—the winch that raises and lowers the anchor—a capstan swabbie is a round, stout cylinder of wood wrapped in heavy-duty rope. Spars spaced evenly around its frame complete the look, while castors on its base give the swabbie mobility. A capstan swabbie also sports a few small pegs around its diameter. It can use these appendages to “grab” other ropes from the rigging to help sail the ship. Some crews decorate their capstan swabbies with paint or rags to give the constructs a personal touch. A capstan swabbie instinctively protects its captain's ship. Using short ropes attached to its sides, the swabbie can lash and trip foes or make emergency repairs to itself or its vessel. Far more dangerous, of course, is the anchor to which the swabbie's longest rope is attached. A capstan swabbie can swing this anchor in wide arcs and reel it back in at a moment's notice. **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 25 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 [Capstan Swabbie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4218) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 250 **Perception** +12; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +17 **Str** +6 **Dex** +2 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 **Attached Anchor** A capstan swabbie is attached to its anchor by a 100-foot-long rope, most of which it keeps coiled around its body. If the capstan swabbie tries to move more than 100 feet from its attached anchor, its Speed is reduced to 10 feet as it drags the anchor along with it. If this rope is severed (AC 12 when uncoiled, Hardness 4, HP 16), the capstan swabbie can move freely, but its ranged anchor Strike loses the [tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=804) trait.
**Mate's Expertise** The capstan swabbie can use [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) to [Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2292) an ally's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check (to hoist or lower sails or the anchor, for instance). On a success or critical success, the capstan swabbie grants its ally a +2 circumstance bonus to the triggering check. **Items** anchor --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +17 **Ref** +12 **Will** +10 **HP** 115 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Rules.aspx?ID=2311), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** anchor +18 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning **Melee** rope +18 ([Disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d6+9 slashing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Ranged** anchor +14 ([thrown 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [tethered](/Traits.aspx?ID=804)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning **Jury-Rig** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The capstan swabbie uses built-in tools to quickly patch itself up. The swabbie gains 10 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321). The swabbie can also use this action on an adjacent section of a ship. The temporary Hit Points last for 1 minute. The target is then temporarily immune for 1 hour. **Loose the Pawls** The capstan swabbie spins around rapidly, its loose ropes whipping everyone in reach. The capstan swabbie makes a rope Strike against each creature within 10 feet and compares its attack roll to the AC of each creature within 10 feet.
Constructed Crews Only the wealthiest captains can afford to craft or purchase a capstan swabbie, but there might be another way to obtain one. According to legend, the ancient Jistka Imperium possessed a “mechanical navy”—a fleet of warships crewed by constructs under the command of a powerful mage. Treasure hunters plying the right waters might be lucky enough to find an intact capstan swabbie from this bygone empire.
name: Capstan Swabbie

<title level="1" pfs="">[Creeping Evil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4219)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Religious philosophers have noted that while the virtuous are often filled with a righteous conviction that inures them from hopelessness and fear, the wicked are beset by constant doubt, driving them to commit even greater atrocities in an effort to stave off their insecurities. Sometimes, when this cycle reaches a crisis point, persistent doubt can even break from the psyche that created it, adopting a physical form known as a creeping evil.

While [demons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=387), [devils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=389), and other [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=600) are those most likely to spawn a creeping evil, a mortal tyrant gripped by constant fear of rebellion or a murderer compelled to kill again in order to cover up their previous crimes can manifest one as well. Often, a creeping evil will linger near the creature that created it, sparing their life but lurking in the shadows as a constant reminder of their failures. Only when the creature summons their courage and finally confronts their fear head-on does the creeping evil turn on them. Other creeping evils abandon their creators as soon as they spawn, slithering down lightless alleyways or into abandoned sewer tunnels to search for new souls to corrupt.

Though its body has the consistency of flowing tar, a creeping evil's predator-like cunning and unnatural ability to vary its volume at a whim set it apart from the living oozes often encountered by adventurers.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Ooze](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">

</column>

</row>

pseudopod +20 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)),
**Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning plus 2d6 void and [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45)

**Slithering Strike** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The creeping evil [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice, then makes a pseudopod Strike. The target is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against this Strike unless it has seen a Slithering Strike before.

**Stir Doubts** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The creeping evil reaches psychically into a creature's innermost thoughts, taking hold of that creature's insecurities and bringing them to front of mind. One creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by the creeping evil takes 4d8 mental damage (DC 28 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save). A creature that fails the save is also [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (or frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**Variable Volume** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=658)) The creeping evil squeezes itself into a container or other space large enough to fit a Tiny creature. It can exit the container by taking this action again, immediately returning to its normal size. If creatures or objects would occupy the creeping evil's space, it automatically pushes those creatures and objects out of the way to fill a space.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Osoyo's Doubt</title>

While the creeping evil the PCs face in Egede is most likely the product of some unnamed demon spawned by the Worldwound, they will encounter another such evil when they defeat the animate dream Ogmunzorius in Chapter 3 of “Dreamers of the Nameless Spires.” Though this creeping evil is still nascent, it is a manifestation of Osoyo's own fear of failure, and the PCs can use it as a weapon during their final confrontation with the Blackfrost Whale.
</aside>

markdown:
Crownbound constellations are the essence of stars granted sentience by the goddess [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281), who created them to survey the vastness of space and help her craft the galaxy. Their intergalactic travels once took them far, but [Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=287) sought to ensnare Desna's creation. The Mother of Beasts lured them to Golarion and then, exploiting the power of Golarion's sun, corrupted their ability to star travel. Stranded on the planet and unable to bear sunlight, these creatures traveled to the Crown of the World, where they have settled ever since. **[Recall Knowledge - Astral](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Crownbound Constellation](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4220) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 252 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +16, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +21, [Lamashtu Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +14, [Star Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +16, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16 **Str** +7 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** +4 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 30 [all-around vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49) **Fort** +19 **Ref** +16 **Will** +13 **HP** 98 **Immunities** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586), [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517), precision, [swarm mind](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=78) **Resistances** bludgeoning 5, piercing 8, slashing 8 **Weaknesses** area damage 8, splash damage 8 **Solar Instability** The crownbound constellation is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73) and [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) when in direct contact with the rays of the sun.
**Starlight** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) 15 feet. The glow of the crownbound constellation creates dim light. It can suppress or resume this ability as a free action.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** star greatsword +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d12+10 slashing and starburst **Ranged** star composite longbow +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+7 piercing and starburst **Dazzling Assault** ([[visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) Each enemy in the crownbound constellation's space takes 7d4 bludgeoning damage (DC 23 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that fail this save are also [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of their next turn. **Starburst** ([Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) A creature that takes damage from a crownbound constellation's attack must succeed at a DC 23 Fortitude save or be [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) for 1 minute. **Star Weapons** The crownbound constellation reforms its two weapons as [_+1 striking_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) common or uncommon simple or martial weapons. These star weapons can't be [disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383), [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=60), [dropped](/Rules.aspx?ID=2152), [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46), or [stolen](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410). Ranged star weapons don't require ammunition and those with a reload of 2 have a reload of 0 instead. When the crownbound constellation dies or uses its swarm shape its star weapons disappear. **Swarm Shape** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The crownbound constellation disperses into a shapeless swarm of Small stars. It drops all held, worn, and carried items. While discorporated, the crownbound constellation can't use [attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540) actions, but it has a [fly Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2350) of 40 feet and can move through areas small enough for its individual stars to fit without having to [Squeeze](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372). It can use Swarm Shape again to coalesce back into its normal form, at which point its star weapons reappear.
An Eon's Long Memory Crownbound constellations have not forgotten [Lamashtu's](/Deities.aspx?ID=287) deception, or the commission set for them by [Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=281). Crownbound constellations frequently attack those they assume to be in league with the Mother of Monsters, and their hatred for her has severed their connection to Desna, just as Lamashtu planned.
name: Crownbound Constellation

The kithangian—a horrifying amalgam of [horse](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=431) and [scorpion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=466) also known as a “beast demon”—is a foul fiend born of the souls of mortals who tormented animals in life. Kithangians tend to roam wild areas rife with animals to torture, though humanoid settlements with plenty of pets or livestock also make for tantalizing hunting grounds. Uncontested for too long, a kithangian's presence has a corrupting influence on the local fauna, which birth fiendish monstrosities until the demon is vanquished. If they realize they're being tracked by vengeful druids or demon slayers, a kithangian assumes the shape of an unassuming animal so they can get the jump on their pursuers. **[Recall Knowledge - Fiend](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Kithangian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4221) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 253, [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 92 **Perception** +19; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Chthonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=114), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +18, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +20, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +16 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +20 **Ref** +15 **Will** +19 +1 status to all saves vs. magic **HP** 180 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 10, [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) 10 **[All-Around Vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49)**
**Animal Kindness Vulnerability** Kithangians find kindness to animals revolting. The first time each round that a kithangian sees someone heal or otherwise provide aid to a creature that has the animal trait, the kithangian takes 3d6 [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) damage. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** pincer +21 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d12+9 slashing plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Melee** stinger +21 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+9 piercing plus kithangian venom **Divine Innate Spells** DC 25 - **1st** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470) (at will; animals only) - **3rd** [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622) (×2) - **4th** [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) - **Constant (4th)** [Speak with Animals](/Spells.aspx?ID=1680) **Divine Rituals** DC 25 - **1st** [Demonic Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=142) **Animal Killer** A kithangian's melee Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to animals. **[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The kithangian transforms into a Medium or Large animal. This doesn't affect their statistics, but it might change the damage type of their Strikes. **Kithangian Venom** ([Poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** DC 25 Fortitude; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** 2d6 poison damage (2 rounds); **Stage 2** 2d6 poison damage and [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (2 rounds); **Stage 3** 3d6 poison damage and sickened 2 (2 rounds) **Rasping Tongues** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) **Frequency** once per round; **Requirements** The kithangian has a creature [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) in one or both pincers; **Effect** Barbed tongues slither from the faces in the kithangian's pincers. The tongues burrow into grabbed creatures and inject their minds with psychic screams. Each grabbed creature takes 2d8 piercing damage and 2d8 mental damage. A creature can resist the mental damage by attempting a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Will save.
name: Kithangian

<title level="1" pfs="">[Desa-Desa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4222)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Also known as “bubble cats” or “gas cats,” desa-desas are small, vaguely feline predators that hunt the jungles of Castrovel. A typical desa-desa is roughly the size and shape of a bobcat. Its head is reptilian, sporting large eyes and slender fangs that protrude from its lips. Desa-desa fur tends to match their environment—most are dark green and brown—and is punctuated by patterns of small, bubble-like bladders that protrude from the creature's skin like transparent boils. Every desa-desa has a different bladder pattern, and this distinctive anatomical feature is key to the creature's survival.

Desa-desas' lives revolve around a little-understood (and likely magical) process by which the creatures transform water into its constituent gaseous elements. A desa-desa stores these gases in the polyp-like bladders, which are lined with an electricity-conducting material that allows the creature to create sudden blasts of flame in all directions.

As a predator, the desa-desa's most dangerous attack is its bite, which passes along its bubble venom, which can ravage the fluids in the victim's own body. This reaction causes water in the target's cells to split rapidly, creating bubbles of gas that percolate through the creature's flesh to its skin, where it balloons outward in large, agonizing blisters before popping. The combustible gases in these blisters also make a creature particularly susceptible to fire attacks—such as the desa-desa's defensive blast. A typical desa-desa is 4 feet long counting the tail, and weighs just 30 pounds.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 18

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 16

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/DesaDesa.png" />
</column>

</row>

<title level="2" right="Creature 2">[Desa-Desa](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4222)</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Small" />
<trait label="Animal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=531" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 254

**Perception** +10; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**
[Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +7, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +8

<row gap="medium">
**Str** +2

**Dex** +5

**Con** +1

**Int** -4

**Wis** +4

**Cha** +1
</row>

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">
**AC** 18 

**Fort** +5 

**Ref** +11 

**Will** +8 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 34

</row>

**Resistances**
[fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 4

**Weaknesses**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 2

**Volatile Gases** Whenever a desa-desa takes electricity damage, it must attempt a DC 18 Fortitude save. On a failure, the sparks cause the desa-desa to immediately use Fire Blast if it can, and the desa-desa becomes [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 40 feet, climb 20 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
bite +11,
**Damage** 1d4+2 piercing plus bubble venom

**Bubble Venom**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) A creature bit by a desa-desa breaks out in large, painful blisters which expand and pop as water in the victim's body turns into volatile gases. Magical healing of any kind [counteracts](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) the bubble venom; **Saving Throw** DC 18 Fortitude; Maximum Duration 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 2 and 1d6 piercing (1 round); **Stage 2** weakness to fire 4 and 2d6 piercing (1 round)

**Fire Blast** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604)) The desa-desa releases a burst of explosive gases in all directions, dealing 3d6 fire damage to all creatures in a 5-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). The desa-desa can't use Fire Blast again for 1d4 rounds.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Cat Gases</title>

Castrovelian elven scholars have long noted the utility of the gases contained in desa-desa bubble bladders. Researchers at Tolris University in Qabarat have used the gases to create hotter forges, bottled air for diving, and even lift for airships. The former head of the department, Shimah Teph, believed that the cats could keep a ship aloft indefinitely, while feeding them just a few scraps of meat, and managed to pilot a test flight to Laubu Mesa before dying of desa-desa-inflicted wounds.
</aside>
name: Desa-Desa
