markdown:
Sakuachi is the youngest scion in a long line of Erutaki seers. Like her forebears, she was born and raised in Aaminiut, a small town on the Crown of the World northeast of Avistan. Her mother Sialuk and her grandmother Seshu both taught Sakuachi how to see patterns in the ice below, read the stars above, and interpret the signs of the natural world all around. It was astronomy, above all other pursuits, which appealed most to Sakuachi. As a girl, she spent many long nights studying constellations, practicing with her astrolabe, and divining omens from the wisps and waves of the aurora. Before she was 5 years old, she'd already learned the names of every star configuration, the positions of the visible planets, and how to predict and interpret the movement of the celestial mantle. By the time she was a teenager, Sakuachi was the town's best astronomer. All the while, Elder Seshu looked proudly upon her granddaughter. But Seshu also bore the burden of a terrible secret—she knew that, one day, her beloved Sakuachi would be forced to reckon with an ancient and terrible evil. On 21 Erastus of 4722 ar, at the close of Sakuachi's final summer before adulthood, unnatural blue lights beamed up from the center of the Crown of the World. Around Golarion, this occasion was known as the night of the Missing Moment. To Elder Seshu, it was a sign that the time had come for Sakuachi to fulfill her grim destiny. That night, Seshu dreamt of an ancient being of white fur, bestial horns, and glowing runic tattoos: Ainamuuren, the Last Saumen Kar. Seshu had befriended Ainamuuren long ago, and for years she'd listened to his dire warnings of a great evil that lurked beneath the Crown of the World. She hadn't seen her friend in many seasons, though, so his dream visit came as a surprise. In this dream, Ainamuuren told Seshu that his strength was fading, and that soon he wouldn't be able to maintain the magic that keeps the ancient evil sealed beneath the ice. To renew the seal, Ainamuuren explained, the saumen kar's magic would need to be renewed through the sacrifice of a god, a quest which only a mortal could undertake. As Seshu's dream vision faded to darkness, one of Ainamuuren's runes glowed brighter and longer than all the others. Early the following morning, Seshu met with the other elders and told them the time had come to send one of their own on a holy quest. As Seshu had predicted, her granddaughter Sakuachi eagerly volunteered to take on this quest. Together, the elders agreed that Sakuachi—young and good of heart— should take on this great task. With her, the town sent four companions: the hunter Deniigi, the guide Innumma, the storyteller Pamak, and the navigator Ulikuq. Together, the group gathered and took a solemn vow to return to their homeland with a sacrificial god. Before the party left, Seshu took Sakuachi aside. “To honor the one who has given us this quest,” Seshu told the girl, “you must take his symbol as your own.” Seshu held the nape of Sakuachi's neck firmly in her grasp, so much so that it hurt. Though the grip was tight, Sakuachi felt a warmth in her grandmother's palm. When her grandmother withdrew her palm, Sakuachi bore one of Ainamuuren's runes like a fresh tattoo. In his travels, the guide Innumma had heard of a land called Sarkoris, where gods still roamed [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), but no one in Aaminiut had ever been there. The town's most skilled sorcerer had visited a city called Skywatch long ago, so she teleported the group there to expedite their journey. Yet when they arrived, Sakuachi and her companions discovered the city strangely abandoned and full of danger. The streets seemed to writhe and twist under the group's feet, confusing their minds. They became separated, and Sakuachi barricaded herself in the city's observatory after being chased by violent raiders. Glancing through the observatory's telescope, Sakuachi prayed to the stars above for help in her challenging endeavor. Amazingly, the stars answered—the telescope formed a magical conduit with astronomers on another planet, and they teleported a group of adventurers to Skywatch to help her. Even more astonishing, these “gatewalkers” bore the same rune of Ainamuuren as she did. The young seer believed in many wonders, but not coincidences. From then on, she knew that their destinies were intertwined, and she'd need to stay close to her strange new friends if she was to fulfill her quest. Campaign RoleThe party first comes in contact with Sakuachi and her companions during their exploration of the abandoned city of Skywatch at the beginning of “They Watched the Stars.” Based on what the player characters learned about the alien entity Osoyo from the astronomers of Loskialua in the “The Seventh Arch,” their investigation into the cause of the Missing Moment overlaps significantly with Sakuachi's quest to find and sacrifice a living god. Indeed, by the time the characters and their new friends escape Skywatch's dangers and reach safety on the shores of Mendev, their employer, Dr. Etward Ritalson, tasks the characters with helping Sakuachi fulfill her quest. Sakuachi's success will ultimately be the player characters' success, so it's important they see her as a key to solving their own mystery. By the adventure's end, Sakuachi will need to sacrifice her spirit guide to seal away Osoyo once more, but this doesn't mean she's the star of the story. In fact, as they'll soon learn, the player characters are the primary instigators of the troubles that set Sakuachi's quest in motion in the first place. Even if she views the characters with suspicion, Sakuachi knows that her fate—and the fate of her people—depends upon the characters. How this story ends is, ultimately, the party's decision to make. Sakuachi is more a scholar more than a fighter. During the times when she's accompanying the characters and a battle occurs, such as in Chapter 4 of “They Watched the Stars,” she rarely (if ever) directly attacks a foe. In the heightened stress of combat, her connection to Ainamuuren may manifest as visions and flashes of memory as the saumen kar attempts to communicate with her, which allows her to Recall Knowledge about one or more of the creatures in combat using her [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) skill, though it also prevents her from participating in combat. Beyond providing information, she also can use spells like [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651) or [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) to support the party. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Sakuachi](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4243) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 280 **Perception** +14 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Empyrean](/Languages.aspx?ID=115), [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) **Skills** [Astronomy Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +15, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +10, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +12, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +15, [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +0 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Search the Stars** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Requirements** Sakuachi is holding an astrolabe and can see the stars; **Effect** Sakuachi spends 1 hour attempting to gain a vague glimpse of the future written in the stars. She attempts a DC 24 [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) check.
**Critical Success** Sakuachi receives an accurate vision of an event to come within the next week. While this vision can be vague and confusing, it's typically true.
**Success** As critical success, but the vision is of an event that will occur within the next day. If nothing noteworthy is to happen within the next day, she sees nothing written in the stars.
**Failure** Sakuachi sees nothing written in the stars.
**Critical Failure** Sakuachi misinterprets the writing in the stars and receives an inaccurate vision of an upcoming event. Such a vision looks the same as one achieved with a critical success, but is typically false. **Items** [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=40), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365), [standard astrolabe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4027), [Sun Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3584), [winter clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +11 **Ref** +10 **Will** +14 **HP** 60 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spear +9, **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Ranged** spear +10 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Occult Prepared Spells** DC 18, attack +12 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) - **1st** [Bless](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [Liberating Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=2354), [Sleep](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675) - **2nd** [Augury](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [Silence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1674)
name: Sakuachi

Brawny, quiet, and stoic, Deniigi is one of Aaminiut's finest hunters. Among Sakuachi's companions, she's the unofficial “muscle” of the group. Deniigi has never traveled far outside of her village and prefers to speak in her native Erutaki. She's likely to be suspicious of the PCs, though she respects characters who show bravery in battle, especially those who put themselves in harm's way to protect Sakuachi, acknowledging such acts with a silent nod of approval. Her bear-claw daggers were gifts from her older siblings, each made from a trophy taken from one of their many kills. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Deniigi](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4244) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 282 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Hunting Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +8, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12 **Str** +4 **Dex** +1 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Arctic Forager** While using [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) in arctic environments, if Deniigi rolls a failure or a critical failure, she gets a success instead. If Deniigi rolls a success, she can provide food for 4 additional creatures that eat about as much as a human, or 8 creatures on a critical success.
**Snow Tracker** In an arctic environment, Deniigi can [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) while moving at full speed. **Items** [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) (3), [Hide Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=44), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365), [Sun Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3584), [winter clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 75 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spear +12, **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Ranged** spear +12 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6+4 piercing **Melee** dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Ranged** dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing **Cooperative Hunting** After Deniigi attempts a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) at a Large or larger target (regardless of success or failure), the next Strike one of her allies makes against the same target gains a +2 circumstance bonus to the attack roll.

The most well-traveled guide in Sakuachi's party is also their most talented explorer, forager, and weather forecaster. Amiable and good humored, Innumma is always ready to cheer up his fellows or offer some expert advice. He's the companion most interested in the PCs and hearing about their adventures thus far. He playfully teases any character who makes an avoidable mistake but is equally good-humored about his own failings, especially when it comes to predicting the weather. “How boring life would be,” he says, “if everyone were perfect!” Innumma's affable demeanor darkens only when he speaks of Pamak, whom he had come to regard as a younger sibling. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 29 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 [Innumma](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4245) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 283 **Perception** +12 **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +8, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +8, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +10, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +8, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +10, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +12, [Weather Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +12 **Str** +2 **Dex** +2 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +0 **Predict Weather** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Requirements** Innumma is outdoors; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** Innumma spends 10 minutes observing the environment, trying to predict the day's weather. He attempts a DC 20 [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) or [Weather Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) check. This check is reduced to DC 15 in an arctic environment such as the Crown of the World.
**Critical Success** Innumma accurately predicts the day's weather. He learns if a weather event will occur in the region within the next 24 hours, and if so, what the event is and approximately when it will occur. He gains a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks made to prepare for this weather event.
**Success** As critical success, but Innumma gains only a +1 circumstance bonus to Survival checks made to prepare for any weather event that might occur.
**Failure** Innumma can't determine anything about the day's weather.
**Critical Failure** Innumma misinterprets the environment's indicators, predicting a weather event as if he had earned a critical success but which will not occur that day. **Items** [Padded Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=40), [Spear](/Weapons.aspx?ID=365), [Sun Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3584), [winter clothing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2715) --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +10 **Ref** +10 **Will** +13 **HP** 55 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** spear +9, **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Ranged** spear +9 ([thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing

<title level="1" pfs="">[Ulikuq](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4246)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Humble and aloof, the astronomer Ulikuq is the group's best sailor and navigator, despite being just a few moons older than Sakuachi. Ulikuq has literally seen [Sakuachi's](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4243) destiny written in the stars, and they respect her all the more for it. As the adventure progresses, Ulikuq grows increasingly suspicious of the characters, especially once their actions during the Missing Moment are revealed near the end of Chapter 2 of “They Watched the Stars.” They even blame the characters for the things they did while under Osoyo's control. “The Blackfrost Whale would not have found purchase in your souls,” they say, “were there not already some darkness there.” If tensions with Ulikuq flare, however, Sakuachi is quick to tamp them down, reminding her friend that Osoyo is an evil they all must work together to defeat.

This check is reduced to DC 15 in an arctic environment such as the Crown of the World. On a success, the severity of environmental conditions during the journey is reduced by one step for 24 hours (two steps on a critical success). This changes moderate damage to minor damage, winds that create [greater difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2586) cause only difficult terrain, and so on.

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ [Leiko](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4249) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 39 **Perception** +10; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118), [Shae](/Languages.aspx?ID=71), [Sussuran](/Languages.aspx?ID=111) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +13, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +9, [Netherworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +11, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +11, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +13 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +3 **Wis** +2 **Cha** +3 **Shadow Shift** Being made partially of shadow herself, Leiko is [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2404) even to creatures that can see clearly in those light levels. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) (15) --- **AC** 21 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +11 **Will** +10 **HP** 45 **Immunities** precision **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5, [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) 5 **Counterattack** **Trigger** Leiko is targeted by an attack from an adjacent creature that misses due to Leiko being [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62); **Requirements** Leiko is aware of the attack; **Effect** Leiko makes a Strike against the attacker.
**Slip** **Trigger** A creature moves adjacent to Leiko; **Effect** Leiko teleports to a clear space adjacent to another creature she can see within 30 feet. --- **Speed** 25 feet, fly 35 feet; swift steps, tenebral form **Melee** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing plus 1d6 cold **Ranged** dagger +13 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+5 piercing plus 1d6 cold **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **4th** [Vapor Form](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738) (at will) - **7th** [Interplanar Teleport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1576) (self only; to [Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) or the [Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26) only) **Bide** Leiko prepares to take action against her foes, watching her opponent and waiting for the right opportunity to respond. Leiko gains a second reaction until the start of her next turn, though she still can't use more than one reaction on the same triggering action. **Swift Steps** Leiko's movement doesn't trigger reactions. **Tenebral Form** Leiko can [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) at full Speed in [_vapor form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1738).
name: Leiko

**Stalk Goggles Specialist** As long as he wears his [_greater stalk goggles_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1821), the Looksee Man can't be [flanked](/Rules.aspx?ID=2375) by creatures of 4th level or lower. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing **Ranged** dagger +12 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [thrown 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4 piercing **Wizard Prepared Spells** DC 21, attack +14 - **Cantrips (2nd)** [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Tangle Vine](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713), [Telekinetic Hand](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715) - **1st** [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Thunderstrike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721) - **2nd** [Acid Grip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1436), [Blazing Bolt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450) (2), [Gecko Grip](/Spells.aspx?ID=1541) **School of Protean Form Spell** 1 Focus Point - **1st** [Scramble Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=1904) **Occult Innate Spells** DC 21 - **1st** [Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) **Shadow Stride** ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693)) **Requirements** The Looksee Man is in [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403); **Effect** The Looksee Man Strides. He has a +10-foot status bonus to his Speed during this Stride. The DC from shadow blending increases to 11 during this Stride, and the Looksee Man remains [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) by dim light until the end of the movement, even if he leaves dim light during the [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).
name: The Looksee Man

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature,_ **[Recall Knowledge - Construct](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37)): DC 27 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 25 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22 [Apothecary's Cabinet](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4252) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 81 **Perception** +11; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +15 **Str** +6 **Dex** -3 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +0 **Cha** -5 --- **AC** 24 (20 when broken) **Fort** +17 **Ref** +11 **Will** +11 construct armor **HP** 80 **Hardness** 10 **Immunities** [bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68), [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=73), [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [nonlethal attacks](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Construct Armor** Like normal objects, an apothecary's cabinet has Hardness. This Hardness reduces any damage it takes by an amount equal to the Hardness. Once an apothecary's cabinet is reduced to fewer than half its Hit Points, or immediately upon being damaged by a critical hit, its construct armor breaks and its Armor Class is reduced to 20. --- **Speed** 15 feet **Melee** drawer +17 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+9 bludgeoning **Ranged** poison bottle +14 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711)), **Damage** 2d6+3 bludgeoning plus poison stock **Poison Stock** When the cabinet hits a creature with its poison bottle Strike, roll 1d4 to determine what kind of poison splashes onto the struck creature: **1** nerve poison ([clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1); **2** weakening poison ([enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1); **3** noxious poison ([sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1); **4** clouded mind poison ([stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1). The target must attempt a DC 24 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes 1d6 poison damage.
**Failure** The creature takes 2d6 poison damage and is afflicted with the rolled poison's associated condition until the end of its next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes 2d6 poison damage and is afflicted with the rolled poison's associated condition for 1d4+1 rounds.
name: Apothecary's Cabinet

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579), [polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The eshmok turns into their fungus form or back into their normal humanoid wasp form. In fungus form, they lose their fly speed and can't make sting Strikes. In wasp form, they can't make tendril Strikes.

**Eshmok Infection**  ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669)) **Saving Throw** Will DC 26; **Maximum Duration** 6 rounds; **Stage 1** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) (1 round); **Stage 2** confused and [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=89) (1 round); **Stage 3** confused, [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and quickened (1 round). A creature that is quickened by eshmok infection can use the extra action each round for only [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) actions. A creature that is confused by an eshmok infection never considers an eshmok in fungal form as a target.

</column>
name: Sickly Eshmok

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 28 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 26 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 23 [Ilverani Sentry](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4258) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 162 **Perception** +17; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54) **Skills** [Arctic Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +13, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +11, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +17, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +17 **Str** +4 **Dex** +4 **Con** +2 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +0 **Arctic Forager** While using [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) to [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) in arctic environments, if the Ilverani sentry rolls a failure or a critical failure, they get a success instead. If the sentry rolls a success, they can provide food for 4 additional creatures that eat about as much as a human, or for 8 creatures on a critical success.
**Snow Walker** The Ilverani sentry ignores the effects of [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) from snowy environments. When moving through snow, an Ilverani sentry minimizes traces left behind and makes it harder for others to find them. The DC of checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) an Ilverani sentry through snowy terrain gains a +4 status bonus. **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358), _[+1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [ composite longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434)_ (20 arrows), [Leather Armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41), [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) --- **AC** 25 **Fort** +15 **Ref** +17 **Will** +15 **HP** 115 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** shortsword +18 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+9 piercing **Ranged** composite longbow +18 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [reload 0](/Traits.aspx?ID=254), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+8 piercing **Hunt Prey** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) The Ilverani sentry designates a single creature they can see and hear, or one they're Tracking, as their prey. The sentry gains a +2 circumstance bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) the prey and to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the prey. The first time the sentry hits the designated prey in a round, they deal an additional 1d8 precision damage. The sentry ignores the penalty for making ranged attacks within their second range increment. These effects last until the sentry uses Hunt Prey again.
name: Ilverani Sentry

**Immunities**

**Resistances**
[electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, physical 10 (except bludgeoning)

**Shattering Death** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555)) When the blackfrost guecubu is destroyed, they shatter and fill the air around them with a cloud of razor-sharp shards of ice. Creatures in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 8d8 cold damage (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Creatures that critically fail this save also take 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, burrow 15 feet; tundra glide

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
jaws +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 2d8+8 piercing plus blackfrost and wrathful misfortune

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 26
- **4th**
[Shape Stone](/Spells.aspx?ID=1667) (at will)

**Break Ground** <actions string="Two Actions" />  ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585), [transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) The blackfrost guecubu stomps, and the ground breaks and ripples in a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387), erupting in razor-sharp, crisscrossing spikes of rock that deal 3d8 piercing damage to creatures in the area (DC 26 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The area becomes [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366) and [hazardous terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2587), with each square dealing 3 piercing damage to a creature that moves through it. A guecubu is immune to these effects. Ground under the effects of a [consecrate](/Rituals.aspx?ID=115) ritual or a [_circle of protection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=38) spell tuned against evil or chaos can't be affected by this ability.

**Shove into Ice**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=585)) When the blackfrost guecubu successfully [Shoves](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) a creature into an ice or frozen earth barrier, the target must succeed at a DC 26 Reflex save or become merged with the barrier, with the effects of [_one with stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1619) until the target [escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 26).

**Tundra Glide**   The blackfrost guecubu can [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=2310) through any frozen earth, ice, or snow, not including rock. When they do so, the blackfrost guecubu moves at their full [burrow Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2348), leaving no tunnels or signs of their passing.

**Wrathful Misfortune**  ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566)) A creature damaged by a blackfrost guecubu must succeed at a DC 26 Will save or become cursed with consummate bad luck. The creature becomes [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 2. Each time that creature rolls initiative, on their next roll they must roll twice and use the worse result; this is a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654) effect.

</column>
name: Blackfrost Guecubu

_Nethys Note: No description has been provided for this creature._ **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 38 **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 38 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 36 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 [Ilakni](/NPCs.aspx?ID=4260) **Source** [Gatewalkers (Hardcover)](/Sources.aspx?ID=290) pg. 167 **Perception** +22; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Erutaki](/Languages.aspx?ID=54), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +22, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +23, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +22 **Str** +6 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +0 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +22 +1 circumstance vs. [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=578) and [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=669) **HP** 195 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) **Resistances** slashing 10 **Weaknesses** [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 --- **Speed** 25 feet, burrow 25 feet (through blackfrost ice only) **Melee** tentacle +22 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 2d8+12 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Ranged** blackfrost ice shard ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [range 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 2d6+6 piercing plus 2d6 cold and [blackfrost](/Curses.aspx?ID=51) **Blackfrost Breath** Ilakni breathes out a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of black wind. All creatures in the area take 10d6 cold damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save); a creature that fails the save is also exposed to [blackfrost](/Curses.aspx?ID=51). Ilakni can't use Blackfrost Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Blackfrost Ice Shard** A creature struck by Ilakni's blackfrost ice shard is exposed to [blackfrost](/Curses.aspx?ID=51). **[Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=57)** 1d8+12 bludgeoning, DC 30 **Siphon Mind** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The target creature is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by Ilakni, is helpless, or is willing, and is adjacent to Ilakni; **Effect** Ilakni extrudes a seething forest of tendrils from his mouth to wrap around the target's head. These tendrils begin siphoning away the target's mind to make them an empty vessel, ready to be inhabited by Osoyo in the future. The target must attempt a DC 30 Will save.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Siphon Mind for 24 hours.
**Success** The target takes 3d6 mental damage.
**Failure** The target takes 6d6 mental damage and becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1, or increases its stupefied value by 1 to a maximum of stupefied 4. If the creature dies as a result of this mental damage, its body remains alive while its mind and soul move on. The body can remain alive without its soul and mind for many years, as long as it is cared for. If Osoyo escapes from its prison, a body in this state becomes animated by Osoyo's will, transforming into an entity with the same statistics as Ilakni but directly controlled by the Blackfrost Whale. If the body is restored to life before this event, this development can be avoided.
**Critical Failure** As critical failure, but 12d6 mental damage and stupefied 2 (or increases its stupefied value by 2, to a maximum of 4).
name: Ilakni

Living creatures are [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 while in Equendia's Great Despair aura. They can't naturally recover from this fear while in the area but recover instantly once they leave it. When a creature first enters the area, it must succeed at a DC 27 Will save (after taking the penalty from being frightened) or be [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85) for 1d4 rounds. The creature is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** fist +24 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 2d8+13 bludgeoning plus 1d6 cold and blackfrost rot **Ranged** composite longbow +22 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d6+13 piercing **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **3rd** [Enthrall](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516) (at will) - **4th** [Honeyed Words](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693) - **5th** [Command](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [Subconscious Suggestion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692), [Truespeech](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728) - **6th** [Dominate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501) **Blackfrost Rot** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) This affliction can't be reduced below stage 1, nor the damage from it healed, until successfully treated with [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467) or a similar effect; the affliction can then be removed as a normal disease. A creature that dies from cold damage while cursed with blackfrost rot rises as a [blackfrost dead](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=333) after 24 hours.
**Saving Throw** DC 30 Fortitude; **Stage 1** 4d6 cold damage (1 minute); **Stage 2** weakness to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 5 and [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1 (1 day); **Stage 3** [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to cold 10 and drained 2 (1 day). **Frozen Breath** ([Cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [divine](/Traits.aspx?ID=579)) Equendia exhales a 60-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386) of icy shards that deal 6d6 cold and 6d6 slashing damage (DC 30 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). She can't use Frozen Breath again for 1d4 rounds. **Hissing Command** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541)) Equendia utters a swift command in [Necril](/Languages.aspx?ID=165) to strike at adjacent enemies. Any blackfrost zombies within 30 feet can use a reaction to attempt a melee Strike. **Invasive Blackfrost** If a PC is critically hit by any of Equendia's Strikes, the wound takes on an unnerving blackened appearance, as if from advanced frostbite or charring. For the next 24 hours, the PC is no longer immune to blackfrost.
name: Equendia

markdown:
<title level="1" pfs="">[Eron Of Jandelay](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4264)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

Rarely seen, the erons of Jandelay serve an important function on their apocalyptic home plane. These spindly birdlike creatures tower over the landscape on seven thin legs, covered in ironlike feathers they can hurl with a flick of a wing. Scavengers and collectors, erons dispose of intrusive elements they discover on Jandelay, stashing them in nests for later assortment. Such detritus typically consist of dead adventurers and their gear; an eron won't hesitate to attack a living adventurer, though, and they make little distinction between living or dead “clutter” that doesn't belong.

[Unfettered Movement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732)

**Mythic Power 3 [Mythic Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323)**   

**• Mythic Skill** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate Cost 1 Mythic Point](/Traits.aspx?ID=835)) Effect The eron of Jandelay attempts its next Acrobatics or Thievery skill check with a +4 bonus and is considered to have mythic proficiency for that check.

**• Remove a Condition** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate Cost 1 Mythic Point](/Traits.aspx?ID=835)) Effect The eron of Jandelay ends one condition affecting them.

**Snatch Prize** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The eron attempts to Steal an object from a creature, using the tips of its immense talons with shocking agility. The object must be 2 Bulk or less. If the creature with the target object is in combat or on guard, the eron can still try to Steal the object if it attempts its Thievery check at mythic proficiency.

**Unbalancing Rip  Requirements The eron of Jandelay's last action was to damage a Large or smaller creature with a claw Strike** <actions string="Free Action" />  Effect The eron rips at the required creature's flesh. That creature takes 1d8 persistent bleed damage and is off-guard until the start of the eron's next turn.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons//">Remnant Hunters</title>

When an eron of Jandelay finds itself on a doomed world, they often seek out an eclectic list of items, remains, and sometimes even creatures from that planet, almost as if they were picking and choosing their favorite parts of that world before it is no more. Whether the planet is literally on its last days makes no difference to the visiting eron. To these ageless monitors, the actual timing of a world's end is irrelevant to their hunt. Sometimes, valuable scavenged treasures can  be discovered amid larger objects or vehicles an eron has been carrying with it via telekinetic haul.
</aside>
name: Eron Of Jandelay

Natbakhs are native to the peaks and subterranean caverns of the windswept dimension of Leng and haunt the dark skies of that nightmare realm. **[Recall Knowledge - Beast](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 32 **[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Natbakh](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4265) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 89 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164); (can't speak any language) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +6 **Con** +5 **Int** -2 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 34 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +28 **Will** +23 [mythic resilience](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) (Ref) **HP** 320 **Immunities** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) **Resistances** [mythic resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) 14 --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 150 feet **Melee** jaws +29 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+14 piercing plus 1d6 persistent bleed **Melee** tentacle tongue +29 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **Mythic Power** 3 [Mythic Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323) **• Mythic Skill** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Cost 1 Mythic Point; Effect The natbakh attempts its next Athletics or Stealth skill check with a +4 bonus and is considered to have mythic proficiency for that check. **• Remove a Condition** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) Cost 1 Mythic Point; Effect The natbakh ends one condition affecting them. **Drain Dreams** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Frequency once per round; Requirements The natbakh's previous action was a successful jaws Strike that damaged a creature; Effect The natbakh's suckered tongue latches on. The creature must succeed at a DC 34 Will save or become stupefied 1 (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) for 1 week as their dreams are slurped away. If the creature becomes stupefied in this way, the natbakh regains 32 HP. Draining dreams from a creature that's already stupefied doesn't restore any HP, but increases the victim's stupefied value by 1, to a maximum of stupefied 5. **Nightmare Screech** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The natbakh shrieks in a 50-foot cone that echoes more in the minds of those in the area than in the ears. Those in the area recall shrieks like this in forgotten nightmares, and the memories of wounds suffered in dreams cause nauseating pain. Creatures in the area take 10d8 mental damage and must attempt a DC 34 Will save. The natbakh can't use Nightmare Screech again for 1d4 rounds. **Critical Success** The creature takes no damage.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is sickened 2
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is sickened 2, and is slowed 1 for as long as they remain sickened (or 1 minute, whichever comes first). **Writhing Clutches** A natbakh can Fly at half Speed while it has a Huge or smaller creature grabbed or restrained, or at full speed when it has a Medium or smaller creature grabbed or restrained, carrying that creature along with it.
Nightmare Eruptions In places where the boundaries between Golarion and Leng are thin, such as the depths of the Kodar Mountains, natbakhs have a particularly horrifying method of entering the waking world. If they consume the dreaming persona of someone sleeping near such thin places, they can burst forth from that sleeper's body, killing them in life as well as in their dream, often leaving the now-dead dreamer's home in splinters before flying off to build a lair in the mountains. Such “nightmare eruptions” are rare, but it doesn't take many natbakhs to leave a mark in a region unprepared for their arrival.
name: Natbakh

Variant male [omen dragon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2954) **[Recall Knowledge - Dragon](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Kandirvek](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4269) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 42 **Perception** +26; darkvision, scent (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +25, [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +26, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) +24, [Fortune-Telling Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +30, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +28, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +28 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +8 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +4 --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +26 **Ref** +27 **Will** +28 +2 status to all saves vs. [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662) **HP** 252 **Immunities** [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Untethered to Fate** As omen dragon.
**Challenge Fate** ([misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) As omen dragon. --- **Speed** 50 feet, fly 160 feet **Melee** jaws +28 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 piercing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Melee** tail +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d10+11 bludgeoning plus 1d8 mental **Melee** wing +26 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+11 slashing plus 1d8 mental **Occult Innate Spells** DC 34 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Guidance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) - **6th** [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566) (at will), [Mindlink](/Spells.aspx?ID=1603) (at will), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) (×3) - **7th** [Retrocognition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1652) **Destiny Breath** ([Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) As young omen dragon, but 13d6 mental damage, 40-foot cone, and DC 34. Draconic Frenzy ; As omen dragon. **Draconic Momentum** As omen dragon. **Prophetic Wings** As adult omen dragon. **Walk the Timelines** ([Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) As adult omen dragon.

Variant [sinspawn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3192) **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 35 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 33 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 [Pridespawn Sentinel](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4270) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 56 **Perception** +23; darkvision, sin scent (imprecise) 30 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +23, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +24, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23 **Str** +7 **Dex** +5 **Con** +6 **Int** +1 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 **Items** _+1 [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) [bec de corbin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=272)_, _+1 [resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2786) [raiment](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2791) [leather armor](/Armor.aspx?ID=41)_ --- **AC** 32 **Fort** +25 **Ref** +22 **Will** +20 +1 status to saves vs. magic, +4 status to saves vs. [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) **HP** 215 **Immunities** [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64) **Resistances** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 Reactive Strike [reaction] --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _bec de corbin_ +26 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784), [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d8+13 piercing plus 1d6 electricity **Melee** jaws +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)), **Damage** 3d4+13 piercing plus sinful bite **False Pride** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The pridespawn sentinel stumbles back and appears frightened and intimidated by a creature within 30 feet that it can see. The targeted creature must attempt a DC 32 Will save; regardless of the result, that creature is temporarily immune to False Pride for 24 hours.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature becomes overconfident and careless as they believe themselves to be more intimidating than they actually are. They're [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the next attack the pridespawn sentinel attempts against them this round.
**Failure** As success, but the creature's false pride causes them to become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round as they spend one of their actions posturing and bragging about their superiority. They're off-guard as long as they remain slowed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but for 1 minute. **Sinful Bite** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) A creature hit by the jaws of a pridespawn sentinel must attempt a DC 29 Will save as it's assailed by arrogant thoughts.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1.
**Failure** The creature is sickened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is sickened 2 and also [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute. **Sneak Attack** A pridespawn sentinel deals 1d6 extra precision damage to creatures who are off-guard.
name: Pridespawn Sentinel

Risen runelords are created by a powerful ritual invented by Runelord Xanderghul—a ritual called _raise runelord_. All risen runelords are at least level 11 creatures. Once created, a risen runelord has no capacity to become more powerful, as they are manifestations of their legacy at a specific point in time (although given enough time and resources, a risen runelord could potentially find a way to overcome this limitation on self-improvement). A risen runelord has a modicum of free will, but as long as the one who created them via _raise runelord_ lives, they're bound to their creator's desires. A risen runelord ordered to undertake obviously self-destructive actions might, at the GM's discretion, resist the command and could potentially even break free of their creator's control. Since _raise runelord_ draws upon a runelord's legacy as it exists within the Dreamlands, no risen runelord can ever sleep or dream. Xanderghul can use a risen runelord in a special way: he can use any risen runelord (including ones he didn't create) as a spare body. If he's slain, his soul can instantly jump to the closest risen runelord to replace them and continue the fight, unless the PCs can disable this defense during the second adventure in Revenge of the Runelords. Regardless of level, all risen runelords are mythic creatures and are immune to [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) effects. **[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Risen Runelord](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4271) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +26, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 [mythic resilience](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) (Will) **HP** 195 **Immunities** [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mythic resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) 6 **Beyond Life** A risen runelord may drink or eat for pleasure or entertainment, but they need not do so for nourishment and never suffer the effects of thirst or starvation.
**Vanishing Death** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When a risen runelord is slain, their body and (if manifested) polearm vanishes as the trapped legacy that was drawn upon to create them is set free, exploding outward in a riot of swirling color. This release of arcane energy hammers the minds of nearby creatures. All creatures adjacent to a risen runelord during their vanishing death must succeed at a DC 27 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 24 hours (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) as fragments of their own memories are temporarily scrambled or hidden. Other objects carried by the risen runelord fall to the ground.
 --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** runelord's polearm +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+9 mental **Manifest Runelord's Polearm** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Requirements** The risen runelord isn't holding a runelord's polearm; **Effect** The risen runelord causes their runelord's polearm to appear in one of their hands. The exact appearance of the polearm depends on the type wielded by the runelord in life, but these weapons are purely spiritual constructs that inflict mental damage on a Strike and vanish as soon as the risen runelord releases the weapon or dies. **Mythic Power 3 [Mythic Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323)** **• Recharge** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The risen runelord regains an expended spell slot or can use Quickened Casting again. **• Remove a Condition** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The risen runelord ends one condition affecting them. **Quickened Casting** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) Frequency once per day; **Effect** If the risen runelord's next action is to cast a cantrip or spell that's 4th-rank or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the risen runelord's spellcasting action, the risen runelord attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.
<H2 Class="Title">Risen Runelord Spells</H2> A risen runelord's prepared spells depend upon their sinful legacy. When creating higher-level risen runelords, use these selections as thematic guidelines when selecting higher-rank spells. A risen runelord prepares arcane spells but doesn't require a spellbook to do so, nor can they learn new spells—the spells they have prepared upon their creation are it.
**Envy Spells**—6th [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), [_repulsion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1650); 5th [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [_veil of privacy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1739); 4th [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [_unfettered movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1732); 3rd [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [_levitate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1584), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677); 2nd [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_environmental endurance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1517), [see the unseen](/Feats.aspx?ID=2815); 1st [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524); Cantrips (6th) [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
**Gluttony Spells**—6th [_summon undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1706), [_vampiric exsanguination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1735); 5th [_invoke spirits_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1578), [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722), [_wave of despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757); 4th [_grim tendrils_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742); 3rd [_bind undead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1449), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [_vampiric feast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736); 2nd [_blood vendetta_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475), [_ghostly carrier_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1543); 1st [_enfeeble_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_goblin pox_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1545); Cantrips (6th) [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_frostbite_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_void warp_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
**Greed Spells**—6th [_cursed metamorphosis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1479), [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492); 5th [_elemental form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1510), [_impaling spike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571), [_wall of stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1751); 4th [_aerial form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1437), [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477), [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745); 3rd [_insect form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1575), [_one with stone_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1619), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752); 2nd [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1514), [_humanoid form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1560), [_shrink_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1672); 1st [_fleet step_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1531), [_mending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597), [_pest form_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1626); Cantrips (6th) [_gouging claw_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1546), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
**Lust Spells**—6th [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [_never mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1614); 5th [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1601), [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), [_subconscious suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1692); 4th [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615), [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693); 3rd [_dream message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503), [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622); 2nd [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_laughing fit_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1583), [_stupefy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691); 1st [_charm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1463), [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470), [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524); Cantrips (6th) [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
**Pride Spells**—6th [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630); 5th [_dizzying colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1500), [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570); 4th [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [_mirage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1604), [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742); 3rd [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), [_hypnotize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622); 2nd [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480), [_revealing light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653); 1st [_disguise magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1491), [_illusory object_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [_phantasmal minion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1631); Cantrips (6th) [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_figment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [_sigil_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1673)
**Sloth Spells**—6th [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1492), [_teleport_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1720); 5th [_sleep_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1675), [_slither_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1676), [_toxic cloud_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722); 4th [_confusion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745), [_translocate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724); 3rd [_cozy cabin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1474), [_slow_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [_wall of thorns_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1752); 2nd [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475), [_marvelous mount_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1594), [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606); 1st [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476), [_gentle landing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1542), [_sure strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709); Cantrips (6th) [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598), [_prestidigitation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639), [_telekinetic hand_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1715)
**Wrath Spells**—6th [_chain lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), [_wall of force_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1749); 5th [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530), [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559), [_wall of ice_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1750); 4th [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721), [_wall of fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1748); 3rd [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753); 2nd [_blazing bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1450), [_noise blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [_telekinetic maneuver_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717); 1st [_breathe fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1457), [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1550), [_tailwind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1711); Cantrips (6th) [_caustic blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1461), [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [_ignition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1565), [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [_telekinetic projectile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718)
Lust And Pride Of the seven sins, lust and pride are unique among the runelords for these sins were only ever represented by a single runelord—and both still live during the events of this Adventure Path. This may change once the events of Revenge of the Runelords come to a conclusion, in which case the sinful foci for lust and pride become potential options for risen runelords of Sorshen or Xanderghul, but during Revenge of the Runelords, no risen runelords of lust or pride can exist.
Risen Runelord Gear A risen runelord's polearm is an extension of their being and cannot exist without them, vanishing upon their death. This doesn't prevent a risen runelord from claiming, carrying, or using other items, and most higher-level risen runelords keep real magic items that remain behind after their death.
name: Risen Runelord

**Bloodline Focus**   When Ayavah Refocuses, she completely fills her focus pool.

**Corruption of Sin**   When Ayavah casts a bloodline spell using a Focus Point or one of the following spells: _blister_,  _disintegrate_, _divine wrath_, _enlarge_, _fear_, or _slow_, she can choose to either give a target a –1 status penalty to AC for 1 round or gain a +2 status bonus to Intimidation checks for 1 round.

**Legendary Sculptor**   Ayavah makes [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks associated with sculpture at legendary proficiency. If she rolls a failure on a Crafting check involving sculpture, she gets a success instead.

**Reach Spell** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) If the next action Ayavah takes is to Cast a Spell that has a range, increase that spell's range by 30 feet.

**[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 42 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 40 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 37 [Gavergash](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4273) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 49 **Perception** +25; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=8) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +25, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +28, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +25, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +26, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +24 **Str** +8 **Dex** +5 **Con** +7 **Int** +5 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +6 **Items** _+2 greater [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [flaming](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2838) [shock](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2847) [bec de corbin](/Weapons.aspx?ID=272)_, _[hollow robes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4046)_ --- **AC** 35 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +25 **Will** +26 [mythic resilience](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) (Will saves) **HP** 320 **Resistances** [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555) 15, [mythic resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) 14 **Weaknesses** [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=586) 10, [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) 10 **Furious Roar** **Trigger** The troll warleader takes electricity or fire damage; **Effect** The warleader uses their Primordial Roar and, if they're aware of the damage's source, can Stride toward it. If the warleader has [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), they attempt a DC 15 flat check to remove it. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** _bec de corbin_ +31 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [Razing](/Traits.aspx?ID=784), [Reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [Shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=694), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 3d8+14 piercing plus 1d6 electricity and 1d6 fire **Melee** jaws +28 ([reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+14 piercing **Melee** claw +28 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+14 slashing **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 39, attack +31 - **Cantrips (7th)** [Electric Arc](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509), [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Frostbite](/Spells.aspx?ID=1539), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718) - **1st** [Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Fear](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) - **2nd** [Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [See the Unseen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [Stupefy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1691) - **3rd** [Earthbind](/Spells.aspx?ID=1506), [Haste](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677) - **4th** [Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Weapon Storm](/Spells.aspx?ID=1758) - **5th** [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569), [Impaling Spike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1571), [Toxic Cloud](/Spells.aspx?ID=1722) - **6th** [Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [Phantasmal Calamity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630), [Vibrant Pattern](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741) **Mythic Power** Gavergash has 3 Mythic Points.
  • **Recharge** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)); **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** Gavergash regains an expended spell.
  • **Remove a Condition** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)); **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** Gavergash ends one condition affecting him.
**Awaken Totem** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) **Requirement** Gavergash is adjacent to a totem; **Trigger** Gavergash used a previous action this round to cast a spell; **Effect** Gavergash makes a melee Strike against one of the totems and destroys it with a smash from his _bec de corbin_ or claw, funneling the residual magic from his recently cast spell into the totem and causing it to burst in an explosion of distracting color. Any non-[troll](/Traits.aspx?ID=777) within a 20-foot emanation of the totem must attempt a DC 39 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Awaken Totem for 24 hours.
**Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of Gavergash's next turn.
**Critical Failure** The creature is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute.
name: Gavergash

**Occult Spontaneous Spells** DC 30, attack +22
- **Cantrips (6th)**
[Daze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482), [Read Aura](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646), [Shield](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671), [Telekinetic Projectile](/Spells.aspx?ID=1718), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745)
- **1st**
[Enfeeble](/Spells.aspx?ID=1513), [Force Barrage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [Ill Omen](/Spells.aspx?ID=1566), [Sure Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709) (4 slots)
- **2nd**
[Blur](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455), [Invisibility](/Spells.aspx?ID=1577), [Resist Energy](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651), [Telekinetic Maneuver](/Spells.aspx?ID=1717) (4 slots)
- **3rd**
[Hypnotize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [Paralyze](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622), [Slow](/Spells.aspx?ID=1677), [Vampiric Feast](/Spells.aspx?ID=1736) (4 slots)
- **4th**
[Confusion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1471), [Fly](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548), [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) (4 slots)
- **5th**
[Blood Vendetta](/Spells.aspx?ID=1454), [Dispel Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1493), [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724), [Synaptic Pulse](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710) (4 slots)

- **4th**
[Illusory Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) (at will), [Illusory Object](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569) (at will), [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) (at will)
- **5th**

**[Change Shape](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=55)** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [Polymorph](/Traits.aspx?ID=670)) The lamia matriarch can take on the appearance of a Medium [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628). This doesn't change their Speed or their attack and damage modifiers with their Strikes, but it does prevent them from using their cursed touch. Each lamia matriarch has a fixed humanoid form that resembles their upper torso when in their true form. This is the only humanoid form they can adopt using this ability.

**Falchion Swipe** <actions string="Three Actions" />  Vesinra makes a falchion attack against each enemy within reach. Each attack counts toward her multiple attack penalty, but the penalty does not increase until after all the attacks. The first enemy she damages is subject to Vesinra's Caress.

**Vesinra's Caress** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Curse](/Traits.aspx?ID=566), [Mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [Occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) Vesinra touches a creature, who must succeed at a DC 30 Will save or become [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2. If the target fails additional saves against this ability, the condition value increases by 2 (to a maximum of enfeebled 4). This condition value decreases by 1 every 24 hours.

**[Recall Knowledge - Dream](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 33 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 31 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 28 [Risen Runelord](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4275) **Source** [Pathfinder Adventure Path #219: Lord of the Trinity Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=283) pg. 90 **Perception** +22 **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=13), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=1), [Thassilonian](/Languages.aspx?ID=52) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +26, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +22, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +22, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +24, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +22 **Str** +3 **Dex** +5 **Con** +3 **Int** +7 **Wis** +5 **Cha** +5 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +18 **Ref** +22 **Will** +24 [mythic resilience](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) (Will) **HP** 195 **Immunities** [sleep](/Traits.aspx?ID=696) **Resistances** [mythic resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=3341) 6 **Beyond Life** A risen runelord may drink or eat for pleasure or entertainment, but they need not do so for nourishment and never suffer the effects of [thirst or starvation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2603).
**Vanishing Death** ([arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) When a risen runelord is slain, their body and (if manifested) polearm vanishes as the trapped legacy that was drawn upon to create them is set free, exploding outward in a riot of swirling color. This release of arcane energy hammers the minds of nearby creatures. All creatures adjacent to a risen runelord during their vanishing death must succeed at a DC 27 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 24 hours (stupefied 2 on a critical failure) as fragments of their own memories are temporarily scrambled or hidden. Other objects carried by the risen runelord fall to the ground. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** _runelord's polearm_ +22 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+9 mental **Arcane Prepared Spells** DC 30, attack +22; see Risen Runelord Spells below. **Manifest Runelord's Polearm** ([Arcane](/Traits.aspx?ID=534)) **Requirements** The risen runelord isn't holding a runelord's polearm; **Effect** The risen runelord causes their runelord's polearm to appear in one of their hands. The exact appearance of the polearm depends on the type wielded by the runelord in life, but these weapons are purely spiritual constructs that inflict mental damage on a Strike and vanish as soon as the risen runelord releases the weapon or dies. **Mythic Power** 3 [Mythic Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=3323)
  • **Recharge** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)); **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The risen runelord regains an expended spell slot or can use Quickened Casting again.
  • **Remove a Condition** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)); **Cost** 1 Mythic Point; **Effect** The risen runelord ends one condition affecting them.
**Quickened Casting** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [Spellshape](/Traits.aspx?ID=513)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** If the risen runelord's next action is to cast a cantrip or spell that's 4th-rank or lower, reduce the number of actions to cast it by 1 (minimum 1 action). **Steady Spellcasting** If a reaction would disrupt the risen runelord's spellcasting action, the risen runelord attempts a DC 15 flat check. On a success, the action isn't disrupted.
name: Risen Runelord

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Giant Badger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4277)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

The violent, territorial giant badger is a relentless predator. An obligate carnivore, it can consume prey that ranges from rabbits to deer, livestock, and even the occasional adventurer. A giant badger's claws are sharp and strong enough to carve tunnels from solid rock. They typically stand 4 feet tall at the shoulder and weigh 500 pounds.

Giant badgers are fiercely territorial and are known to defend their burrows with fury, even if a creature isn't necessarily threatening. These burrows tend to be larger than a giant badger typically needs, which sometimes draws the attention of other creatures seeking homes. This leads to many encounters between giant badgers and larger animals such as [bears](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=370).

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 16

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 11
</column>

</column>

<column gap="medium">
<image src="/images/Monsters/Giant_Badger.webp" />
</column>

</row>

**Damage** 1d8+4 piercing

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
claw +11 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526)),
**Damage** 1d6+4 slashing

**Badger Rage** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) The giant badger enters a state of pure rage that lasts for 1 minute, until there are no enemies it can perceive, or until it falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95), whichever comes first. While raging, the giant badger has AC 17, its jaws Strike deals 1d8+8 damage, and its claw Strike deals 1d6+6 damage. While raging, the giant badger also can't use actions that have the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait except for [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301). After it has stopped raging, a giant badger can't use Badger Rage again for 1 minute.

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_2_AdditionalLore.png">Trained Diggers</title>

[Kobolds](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=435) and others who dwell in underground warrens know the value of having a few trained giant badgers in their lair, since they can excavate new tunnels and chambers with ease when their digging habits are directed. Of course, the fact that giant badgers find kobolds and others who dwell underground delicious means that relying on such trained diggers can be a risky endeavor. A giant badger trained for use as a digger or guardian tends to obey the commands of only one master, and even then, they can be prone to unpredictable outbursts for no reason other than pique.
</aside>

<document level="2" id="creature-family-573" />
name: Giant Badger
