Few sea monsters are as feared as the two-headed baomal. These massive predators typically dwell in the deepest waters, competing with [krakens](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3075) and other behemoths for food. They subsist on whales and other large sea creatures, often following them to the water's surface during a hunt. Near the surface, baomals that encounter ships quickly learn that they contain a variety of tasty morsels. The huge beasts use their devastating spikes to rip open the ships' hulls, then leisurely feed on the helpless sailors. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 40 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 38 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 35 [Baomal](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4278) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 55 **Perception** +34; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 80 feet **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +34, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +31, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +37 **Str** +10 **Dex** +2 **Con** +8 **Int** -3 **Wis** +6 **Cha** +1 --- **AC** 46 **Fort** +36 **Ref** +30 **Will** +34 **HP** 315 **Resistances** physical 10 **[All-Around Vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=49)**
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Doubled Reaction** A baomal gains an extra reaction each round that it can use only to make a Reactive Strike. It must use a different head for each one it attempts, and it can't make more than one Reactive Strike for the same triggering action.
**Psychic Static Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 120 feet. All creatures, except [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523), that begin their turn in the area take 5d6 mental damage.
**Two Heads** Any ability that would sever a baomal's head (such as a critical hit with a [_vorpal_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2853) weapon) severs one head at random. Losing one head doesn't kill a baomal, but it does prevent the baomal from making Strikes with the lost head and from using Doubled Reaction or Two-Headed Strike. --- **Speed** 50 feet, swim 80 feet **Melee** jaws +38 ([reach 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 4d12+18 piercing plus [Improved Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=27) **Ranged** tsunami jet +38 ([Brutal](/Traits.aspx?ID=249), [range 500 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d10+18 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=47) 40 feet **Breath of the Sea** ([Attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540)) A baomal can inhale tremendous amounts of water, drawing everything in the sea nearby closer. All creatures and objects in the water within 60 feet of the baomal (including ships) are pulled toward it. Creatures must succeed at a DC 42 [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) check or be pulled up to 20 feet toward the baomal (40 feet on a critical failure). For ships, use the captain's [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) in place of Athletics. Unattended objects are automatically pulled. **Shell Rake** The baomal Swims or Strides alongside a creature or the hull of a vessel, dealing damage with the strong spikes on its shell. Each creature or ship the baomal is adjacent to at any point during its movement takes 6d6+10 slashing and piercing damage (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). Against vessels, Shell Rake ignores the first 5 [Hardness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2183) and creates an explosion of splinters that deals 3d6+5 damage to every creature within 10 feet of the deck's edge (DC 42 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). **Two-Headed Strike** The baomal makes a Strike with each set of jaws, each against a different creature. These Strikes count as one attack for the baomal's multiple attack penalty, and the penalty doesn't increase until after both attacks.
Mysterious Origins All too often, those who study strange monsters assume they were created by powerful but ill-advised [wizards](/Classes.aspx?ID=39) or that they're the result of the [alghollthu](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=358) empire's ancient experiments. In the case of the baomal, extensive research suggests that neither of these explanations are accurate. No ancient texts have been recovered in which a wizard claims to have created the first baomal, and while the alghollthu use them as warbeasts, ancient carvings suggest that, in the earliest days, baomals ravenously hunted alghollthus.
name: Baomal

markdown:
Berberokas are giant humanoids who live in forests and swamps, where they use their ability to swallow massive amounts of water to drain small ponds and lakes. Creatures that visit their favorite watering hole and find only an empty mud basin are the favorite prey of the berberoka, who hides in the brush and overwhelms their victims with a massive torrent of regurgitated water. A berberoka's mottled backside—which resembles a bundle of local plants, small trees, and large rocks—grants them natural camouflage that allows them to hide in plain sight. Berberokas tend to disguise themselves as rock formations in the center of a dried-up waterbed while they lie in wait for passersby. In the tropical regions where berberokas are most common, locals know to give empty ponds a wide berth, regardless of the enticing fish flopping about. Hungry travelers, on the other hand, might see such bounty as a blessing from the gods, only to be swept up in the berberoka's deadly deluge. Berberokas rarely parley with other creatures, but they tend to become more pacified after a meal. They are known to listen to requests if it can lead them to discover new sources of water, especially if these feature an interesting taste or are frequented by novel prey. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 34 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 32 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 29 [Berberoka](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4281) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 57 **Perception** +26; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Jotun](/Languages.aspx?ID=162) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +27, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +27, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +25, [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) +26, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +25, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +26 **Str** +8 **Dex** +4 **Con** +6 **Int** -1 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +4 **Deep Breath** A berberoka can hold their breath for 2 hours. --- **AC** 36 **Fort** +29 **Ref** +23 **Will** +24 **HP** 310 **Weaknesses** fear of crabs **[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)**
**Fear of Crabs** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) If a berberoka sees a crab or crab-like creature, they must attempt a DC 33 Will save. They then become immune to the sight of that creature for 10 minutes.
**Critical Success** The berberoka is unaffected.
**Success** The berberoka becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2.
**Failure** The berberoka gains the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) condition for 1 round and is frightened 4. --- **Speed** 30 feet, swim 30 feet; 15 feet while waterlogged **Melee** fist +31 ([reach 15 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d12+16 bludgeoning plus [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) **[Greater Constrict](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=65)** 2d12+12 bludgeoning, DC 33 **Consume Lake** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) The berberoka drinks a prolific amount from an adjacent water source. If the water source is equal to or greater in volume than themself, the berberoka consumes up to 1,500 gallons of water per minute and becomes waterlogged. They can release water at the same rate. While waterlogged, the berberoka can use Spray Water, their size grows to Gargantuan, and their Speed is reduced to 15 feet. **Spray Water** **Requirements** The berberoka is waterlogged; **Effect** The berberoka sprays a blast of water in a 60-foot [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and is no longer waterlogged. All creatures in the line take 12d8 bludgeoning damage (DC 35 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). On a failed save, a creature is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88) and pushed back 5 feet (10 feet on a critically failed save).
Kabourophobia Berberokas share an unusually intense fear of crabs, so many folktales suggest that fishers bring a crab with them should they suspect a berberoka is lurking nearby. If no crab is available, clicking one's tongue or snapping one's fingers might suffice.
name: Berberoka

A betobeto-san wanders the highways and byways of [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26), searching for unwilling traveling companions to sustain their appetite for fear. Though this shadow spirit is invisible in darkness or shade, in light they appear as a formless, shadowy mass with two sandaled feet. These sandals are made of wood or bone that cause the creature's footsteps to make the distinct “beto beto” sound from which they receive their name. A wide, toothy mouth smiles in the middle of a betobeto-san's otherwise featureless mass, and they can manifest long, clawed limbs. Betobeto-san trail behind nighttime travelers, compelled to follow until the creatures verbally offer to let the betobeto-san pass. This compulsion isn't usually malicious by nature and occurs more from a betobeto-san's misguided desire for company and courtesy; unfortunately, they lack an understanding how this behavior can frighten others. Betobeto-san don't attack those they follow, but they often end up in combat because their unwelcome behavior causes those they follow to attack first. **[Recall Knowledge - Spirit](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 32 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 30 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 27 [Betobeto-San](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4282) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 58 **Perception** +22; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), fearsense (precise) 60 feet **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Shadowtongue](/Languages.aspx?ID=166) **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +23, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +23, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +27 **Str** +4 **Dex** +7 **Con** +5 **Int** +4 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +5 **Fearsense** ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The betobeto-san is aware of all [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) creatures within the listed range. **Items** sandals --- **AC** 33 **Fort** +19 **Ref** +25 **Will** +22 **HP** 170 **Immunities** [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=85), [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=669), precision **Resistances** all damage 10 (except [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=610), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2840), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), or [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509)) double resistance vs. non-magical)
**Ominous Footsteps** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) 60 feet. The betobeto-san's footsteps seem to draw ever closer, yet the source remains difficult to pinpoint. Each creature that starts its turn within 60 feet of the betobeto-san must attempt a DC 29 Will save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 1 minute.
**Success** The creature becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature becomes frightened 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature becomes frightened 4.
**Shadow Invisibility** The betobeto-san is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) unless within an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402).
**Shadow Step** ([occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=693), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Requirements** The betobeto-san isn't already within an area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402); **Trigger** A bright light source reveals the betobeto-san; **Effect** The betobeto-san Steps briefly into [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and then back again, appearing up to 30 feet away from where they began. --- **Speed** 40 feet **Melee** claw +25 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644)), **Damage** 3d12+10 void **Stepping Decoy** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) The betobeto-san Steps. They then create two illusory decoys of sound within 15 feet of them that mimic the sounds of their ominous footsteps. These decoys act independently on the betobeto-san's initiative with 2 actions apiece. They can only [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=2405) or [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305), and they have a Speed of 35 feet. Use the betobeto-san's Stealth DC (typically 37) against attempts to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or disbelieve a decoy. Each decoy lasts for 1 minute. Any existing decoys vanish if the betobeto-san uses this ability again.
Shadowy Afterimages Betobeto-san are the afterimages of travelers who have passed between [the Netherworld](/Planes.aspx?ID=36) and [the Universe](/Planes.aspx?ID=26). While few such journeys create betobeto-san, sages posit that certain emotions or intents from those who travel between these planes can create these apparitions during the transit.
name: Betobeto-San

Bison are large bovines with short faces and two horns; they weigh upward of 2,000 pounds and stand up to 6 feet at the withers. Bison herds thunder across the grassy plains of Golarion, shaking the earth. They're a common sight along the Whistling Plains east of Taldor and the wide grasslands of the nation of Karazh in Casmaron; they also frequently appear in the cooler northeastern reaches of Avistan, in the River Kingdoms through Numeria, and in the Realm of the Mammoth Lords and western Sarkoris. Communal by nature, bison gather in large numbers for the summer mating season before the bulls split off to wander the prairie grasses. Bison have adapted well to harsh prairie winters, as their shaggy fur, which grows thicker in winter, insulates them; in blizzards, they survive by facing steadfastly into the howling winds and hunkering down to reduce their exposure. The way bison wallow in dirt or rub against large stones might make it easy to mistake their docile nature for passivity. However, this presumption has been the ruin of many hunting parties. Their plentiful meat and thick furs make bison an appealing bounty, but with the ground thundering beneath them, bison can quickly overpower inexperienced hunters unprepared for a stampeding herd. Many hunting parties hire particularly daring adventurers who can contribute their spells, steel, and expertise to ensure a successful hunt. While the bison of the plains are the best known and most numerous, they have cousins both in thick boreal forests and along wide, open steppes. These bison variants stand taller but can't match the speed and aggression of their smaller plains relatives. **[Recall Knowledge - Animal](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 19 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 14 [Bison](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4283) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 59 **Perception** +8; [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=74) (imprecise) 60 feet **Languages** **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +12, [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) +10 **Str** +6 **Dex** +3 **Con** +5 **Int** -5 **Wis** +2 **Cha** -1 --- **AC** 20 **Fort** +13 **Ref** +11 **Will** +8 **HP** 70 **Cold Adaptation** The bison reduces the effects it suffers from [cold environments](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) by one step. --- **Speed** 30 feet **Melee** hoof +14, **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning **Melee** horn +12, **Damage** 2d8+6 piercing plus [Knockdown](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=46) **Pointed Charge** The bison surges forward at its foe, horns lowered. It [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice. If the bison ends its movement within melee range of an enemy, it makes a horn Strike against that enemy. This Strike gains the [fatal d12](/Traits.aspx?ID=597) trait. **Rolling Thunder** The bison kicks up dust and shakes the ground as it charges. The stampeding bison [Strides](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to twice its Speed in a straight [line](/Rules.aspx?ID=2388) and can move through the spaces of Medium or smaller creatures. Each creature whose space it moves through takes 4d6+6 bludgeoning damage (DC 21 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save).
Multiple bison can participate in Rolling Thunder by spending this ability's actions and waiting to charge until the herd is ready. Before the beginning of their next turn, they can then charge as a reaction triggered by an adjacent bison beginning its Rolling Thunder charge. All bison in the combined charge must charge in parallel lines, so the areas can't overlap. The combined charge deals an additional 3d6 bludgeoning damage to creatures in the area, and a creature that fails the Reflex saving throw is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
Bison Drives Hunting bison is a communal endeavor. [Centaurs](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=379) from the plains organize into hunting brigades and run alongside members of a stampeding herd, lancing and shooting them from close range. Less mobile peoples use disguises to shepherd bison into position before scaring them to rush over sheer drops or into corrals where they can be slaughtered. A single bison herd can provide enough meat, hides, and furs for an entire community.
name: Bison

Simultaneously enamored with the natural world yet almost too alien to survive in it, blood painters are eight-limbed artists who stalk, kill, and dismember in search of pigment and sustenance alike. The physiology of a blood painter doesn't allow them to digest typical food, and the creatures feed by harvesting blood and using it to paint and animate something they're then able to consume. When not hunting, blood painters seek out beautiful vistas, which they placidly admire through the eyes in the hands of their uppermost limbs before reproducing the view on canvas. Exceptional art endlessly fascinates these creatures, and skilled artisans occasionally escape a gruesome fate by creating a new work for the blood painter to admire in exchange for their lives. Blood painters jealously guard these works, and much of their treasure consists of art. They are often the masterminds behind the thefts of noteworthy masterpieces. **[Recall Knowledge - Aberration](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44)): DC 26 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 24 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 21 [Blood Painter](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4284) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 60 **Perception** +19; bloodsense (imprecise) 60 feet, [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [Aklo](/Languages.aspx?ID=164), [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155) **Skills** [Art Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) +21, [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +20, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +17, [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) +19, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +17 **Str** +5 **Dex** +4 **Con** +3 **Int** +6 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Bloodsense** A blood painter can detect exposed blood as an imprecise sense at the listed range, including from creatures taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86). --- **AC** 27 **Fort** +16 **Ref** +19 **Will** +17 **HP** 155 **Easily Fascinated** When subject to a visual illusion with the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait, the blood painter doesn't adjust their degree of success due to the incapacitation trait. --- **Speed** 30 feet, climb 15 feet **Melee** claw +20 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684)), **Damage** 3d8+8 slashing plus 1d8 [persistent bleed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86) **Dab** ([Manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The blood painter is within reach of an enemy taking [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86); **Effect** The blood painter touches the creature and applies blood to one of their four claws; the blood remains fresh for 1 minute. The target must succeed at a DC 28 Will save or become [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=72) with the blood painter. **Paint** ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) **Requirements** The blood painter has fresh blood applied to a claw using Dab; **Effect** The blood painter expends the blood on one claw to paint an illusion with the effects of one of the following spells: [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568), or [_illusory object_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1569). The Paint action gains the traits of the spell it's reproducing, and the blood painter can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) these effects. They use a spell attack modifier of +20 and DC 28 for these effects, which are heightened to 5th rank.
If they have fresh blood applied to two or more claws, the blood painter can expend the blood on all of them to instead produce the effects of [_cloak of colors_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1977) or [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741).
Any effects produced by this ability have a +2 status bonus to attack rolls, damage rolls, saving throws, skill checks, and AC against the creature whose blood was used to Paint. That creature also takes a –2 status penalty to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2298) checks and saves against them.
Apocryphal Origins Despite blood painters' mysterious origins, the prevailing theory insists the first one arose from overzealous [Shelynites](/Deities.aspx?ID=293) so devoted to their art they ceased eating and sleeping, eventually transforming into [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) that could feed only on their own art. Some believe it possible to “cure” a blood painter, restoring the accursed creature's original memories and form.
name: Blood Painter

<title level="1" pfs="Standard">[Bodach](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4285)</title>

<row gap="medium">

<column gap="medium" flex="1 1 400px">

A bodach, also known in legends as The Old Gray Man, is the fey embodiment of age and decrepitude, a malicious thief whose greed is depthless and unburdened by morality. They're likewise associated with the gray, ceaseless drizzle of winter and the steady weathering of cliffs and castles alike. 

In appearance, a bodach resembles an old man, and many can pass for mortal at first glance. A closer look betrays them, however—everything a bodach carries is old and decaying, every piece of fabric frayed and every scrap of metal tarnished and rusted. There's an odd grayness to them, as if the light never illuminates them properly. Bodachs are most often spotted at twilight or on overcast days; they aren't harmed by sunlight, but they don't much like it either. 

Despite their association with age, bodachs are quite spry. A bodach is a thief and a trickster by preference, accosting travelers and trying to wheedle away their coins with a plausible lie. More sinisterly, some bodachs steal people as well, crawling down chimneys to spirit away a household's children. 

Many non-[fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) believe catching even a glimpse of bodachs is an ill omen that foretells of grave dangers or even death, beliefs that might be tied to the dread these creatures inspire. Local folk tales note that circles of salt are enough to keep a bodach at bay, though in truth this has little effect. Regardless, many homes found near where bodach sightings are common are protected by a line of salt meant to “seal” the hearth or fireplace.

<column gap="tiny">
**[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**
([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 24

**[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 22

**[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 19
</column>

**Perception** +14; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69)

**Languages**

**Skills**

<row gap="medium">

---

**Ref** +12 

**Will** +17 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**HP** 110

</row>

**Weaknesses**
[cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 5

**Gray Aura** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 15 feet. The bodach is always surrounded by a pall of gloomy gray, like the air before it rains. A non-[fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) or non-[hag](/Traits.aspx?ID=620) creature entering the aura or beginning their turn in the aura must succeed at a DC 21 Will save or become [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 round (slowed 2 on a critical failure). A creature who succeeds is temporarily immune to the aura for 1 minute.

</column>

---

<column gap="tiny">

**Speed** 25 feet, climb 10 feet

**Melee**
<actions string="Single Action" />
_staff_ +16 ([Magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [two-hand d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=718)),
**Damage** 2d4+6 bludgeoning

**Primal Innate Spells** DC 24
- **2nd**
[Mist](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606) (at will)

**Implacable Curse** <actions string="Two Actions" />  The bodach makes a staff [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306). If it hits, it deals an additional 2d8 [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737) damage, and the target must attempt a save against either Mumbletongue or Stumblefoot (the bodach's choice).

**Mumbletongue** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The bodach mumbles a string of half-comprehensible nonsense, cursing creatures within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387). Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 24 Will save or become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 for 1 minute (stupefied 2 on a critical failure).

**Stumblefoot** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([Concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676), [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) The bodach stares unblinkingly at its foes, cursing creatures within a 15-foot [cone](/Rules.aspx?ID=2386). Each creature in the area must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or become [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 and [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute (clumsy 2 and enfeebled 2 on a critical failure).

**Unstoppable**   A bodach reduces any penalty it takes to its Speed by 5 feet (to a minimum of 0 feet). A bodach can fit into tight spaces as if it were a Small creature. It can move at its full Speed while [Squeezing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2372).

</column>

<aside>
<title level="2" noclass="true" icon="/images/Icons/Sidebar_4_RelatedCreatures.png">Gray Men And Red Caps</title>

Legend holds that the bodach is the [redcap's](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=348) cousin, and it's certainly true that these two types of fey get along better than most. Malicious redcaps enjoy having their prey softened up, while the bodach appreciates having someone around to do the dirty work of actual killing.
</aside>
name: Bodach

markdown:
A rumor can become so vivid and persistent that it comes to life, creating a brainchild—a living illusion that hatches from an intense belief in a remorseless and implacable killer. Often, these rumors are borne from victims of a [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) spell. A brainchild's capabilities grow when they pursue a believer but deflate against skeptics, making them only as dangerous as one believes them to be. A simple drive to stalk, terrify, and kill propels a brainchild, but the creature might also exhibit other behaviors ascribed to them through gossip. [Brainchild](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4286) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 62 **Perception** +18; [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59) **Languages** [telepathy](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=79) 100 feet, universal language **Skills** [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +22, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +24, [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) +22, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) +21, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +20 **Str** +4 **Dex** +5 **Con** +4 **Int** +2 **Wis** +3 **Cha** +7 **Universal Language** Anything spoken by the brainchild is perceived by the listener in its native language
**Urban Legend** A brainchild is sustained by only the reputation that precedes them. [Mindless](/Traits.aspx?ID=652) creatures are immune to a brainchild and can't perceive them. The brainchild's size, features, and items, as well as the appearance of their attacks, match what the foes perceiving them expect. If multiple foes expect to see different things, the brainchild chooses which to manifest. If any creature that can perceive the brainchild believes the brainchild has one of the abilities below, the brainchild has that ability. A creature can [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) (against the brainchild's Deception DC) to attempt to disbelieve an individual ability. If at any point no creature perceiving the brainchild believes in the ability, the brainchild loses that ability immediately. If foes expect different particulars, such as one believing the brainchild is immune to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) and another believing they're immune to [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [revelation](/Traits.aspx?ID=686), or [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689) effects, the brainchild chooses one to have.
  • [Tremorsense](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=82) (imprecise) 100 feet
  • [Immunity](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to one damage type, trait, or condition
  • [Weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 10 to one damage type other than mental
  • [Resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to physical damage, with an exception for either [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) or [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920)
  • [Frightful Presence](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=64) ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=542), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) 100 feet, DC 28
  • 1d6 extra damage on Strikes, of a type one foe believes in
  • Additional spells of [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632) and [_shadow blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1666) at 6th rank
 --- **AC** 30 **Fort** +21 **Ref** +22 **Will** +18 **HP** 200 **Immunities** [death effects](/Rules.aspx?ID=2331), [detection](/Traits.aspx?ID=574), [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=578), [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), [scrying](/Traits.aspx?ID=689), [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=737), [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) **Weaknesses** [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) 10 **Persistence of Memory** ([illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)) When a brainchild is destroyed, it returns if anyone still fully believes it exists, re-forming within 100 feet of any believer after 2d4 days. --- **Speed** 30 feet, fly 30 feet **Melee** illusory weapon +24 ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662)), **Damage** 4d6+10 mental plus urban legend **Ranged** illusory weapon +24 ([Illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662), [range 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d6+6 mental plus urban legend **Occult Innate Spells** DC 30 - **Cantrips (6th)** [Figment](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528), [Message](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) (×2) - **5th** [Vision of Death](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742) (×3; image resembles the brainchild)
Spreading Rumors Brainchildren require setup. Create some rumors the PCs might hear around town, adding more if they [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=2391). Take note of what rumors they share and who believes which ones. Don’t include too many—if the PCs believe them all, the brainchild will be too powerful! Examples include “I hear the Beast of Chimney Hill can walk through a fire unburnt” and “Woke with a pounding headache. Old Selby’s Dog must be lurking about again.” Depending on the nature of the legends surrounding a brainchild, attempts to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) about it can use almost any skill, but [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) and [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47) are likely the most relevant.
name: Brainchild

markdown:
Brownies make their homes in the trunks of hollow trees, small earthy burrows, and even under porches or within the crawl spaces of farmhouses. Often attired in clothes that appear to be made of plants or leaves, brownies also wear belts lined with pouches and tools. Whatever language they choose to speak is often is riddled with odd pronunciations and colloquialisms. Their manner of speaking might call upon turns of phrase that are decades or even centuries out of vogue, for example, or they might mix up their metaphors in strange ways. It's almost as if brownies adopt these quirky modes of speech intentionally—certainly, they do not react favorably to corrections to their chatter. There's often no swifter way to annoy a brownie than to correct their grammar. Brownies stand barely 2 feet tall and weigh about 20 pounds. When facing danger, brownies rarely engage in combat, preferring instead to confound or confuse their attackers and buy themselves enough time to escape. Content with honest toil and the love of their kin, brownies maintain a pacifist nature, harassing creatures only to run them off or punish them for an insult. Despite this preference, all brownies carry a blade. They refer to their swords with a hint of disgust and jokingly call them their “final trick,” reserving their use for the direst of circumstances. Honest to a fault, brownies take freely but always repay their debts through work or leave something behind as an offering. They might eat an apple from a farmer's orchard but harvest the entire tree as repayment, or they might consume an entire pie left on a windowsill, only to straighten up the kitchen or wash the dishes. A brownie can share a home with a family for years while avoiding detection. A family that's aware of a brownie in their midst usually finds the relationship beneficial and leaves out dishes of milk, pieces of fruit, trinkets, and sometimes even wine as gifts. In exchange, the resident brownie keeps the home clean, mends clothes, repairs tools, and shoos away vermin and small predators. Bragging about having a brownie in the house is the best way to lose one. Brownies distrust foxes and fear wolves, and they avoid farms with dogs. **[Recall Knowledge - Fey](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43)): DC 15 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 13 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 10 [Brownie](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4287) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 63 **Perception** +7; [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=69) **Languages** [Common](/Languages.aspx?ID=155), [Elven](/Languages.aspx?ID=158), [Fey](/Languages.aspx?ID=119), [Gnomish](/Languages.aspx?ID=159) **Skills** [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) +7, [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) +5, [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) +6, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +9 **Str** -2 **Dex** +4 **Con** +1 **Int** +2 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +3 **Items** [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) --- **AC** 16 **Fort** +4 **Ref** +9 **Will** +9 **HP** 21 **Weaknesses** [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2916) 3 --- **Speed** 20 feet **Melee** shortsword +7 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6 piercing **Primal Innate Spells** DC 17 - **Cantrips (4th)** [Light](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585), [Prestidigitation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1639) - **1st** [Ventriloquism](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740) - **3rd** [Mending](/Spells.aspx?ID=1597) - **4th** [Translocate](/Spells.aspx?ID=1724) **Baffling Bluff** ([Emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [primal](/Traits.aspx?ID=676)) The brownie's antics can confuse and disorient a creature. The brownie targets a single creature within 30 feet; that creature must attempt a DC 17 Will save. The target is temporarily immune to Baffling Bluff for 1 minute.
**Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is fooled momentarily and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) against the next melee Strike the brownie makes against it before the end of the brownie's next turn.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round.
**Critical Failure** The target is confused for 1 minute. It can attempt a new save at the end of each of its turns to end the confused condition.
Brownie Bargains As they're not a monolithic group of [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=599) and often have their own proclivities, it's sometimes hard to predict what might attract a brownie or ensure their aid. One of the more commonplace gifts is a saucer of milk or cream, though brownies seem to enjoy other edible treats. Some farmers have reported success in leaving shiny but inconsequential knickknacks like buttons, painted stones, smoothed glass, thimbles, or silver flatware. The whims of brownies shift, so someone who wants to retain the services of one should vary the gifts they leave for these elusive fey creatures.
name: Brownie

markdown:
Caligni children born without eyes—a rare occurrence—are considered sacred to their communities. From a young age, they're trained to become caligni vanguards, dedicated to a rigorous martial regimen and mental strictures. Other calignis revere vanguards' discipline and combat skill, but this respect is tinged with wary caution, as caligni vanguards often have inscrutable motives. Vanguards almost never rebel against their duties or their established role in the community; some claim that those who do are taken by the owbs. A vanguard's armor is fused to their body in pieces, and they rarely remove the elements that aren't. This armor plating and vanguards' keen sense of hearing render them particularly sensitive to sonic vibrations, which serves as both an asset and vulnerability. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Caligni Vanguard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4288) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 64 **Perception** +13; echolocation 60 feet, no vision **Languages** [Caligni](/Languages.aspx?ID=28), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) +14, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +8 **Str** +5 **Dex** -1 **Con** +3 **Int** +1 **Wis** +4 **Cha** +1 **Echolocation** A caligni vanguard can use their hearing as a precise sense at the listed range. **Items** [Composite Longbow](/Weapons.aspx?ID=434) (40 arrows), [Full Plate](/Armor.aspx?ID=50) (see death blaze), [Greatsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=379) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +14 **Ref** +8 **Will** +11 **HP** 50 **Immunities** [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) **Weaknesses** [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=697) 5 Resistances slashing 5
**Death Blaze** When the vanguard dies, their body combusts in a blaze of fire and armor shrapnel. All creatures within a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) take 3d6 fire damage and 3d6 piercing damage (DC 19 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Reflex save). The vanguard's armor is destroyed in the blaze, but their other gear is unaffected and left in a pile where they died.
**[Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48)** --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** greatsword +16 ([versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d12+8 slashing **Ranged** composite longbow +10 ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [range increment 100 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [volley 30 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 1d8+5 piercing **Call to Arms** ([Auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=541), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) Each [caligni](/Traits.aspx?ID=745) within 30 feet of the vanguard gains the [Reactive Strike](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=48) reaction until the end of the vanguard's next turn. Once a caligni has used this Reactive Strike, that caligni is temporarily immune to the same vanguard's Call to Arms for 10 minutes. **Shadowed Blade** ([Darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569)) The vanguard makes a melee Strike, channeling shadowy essence into their weapon or unarmed attack to envelop the target. If the Strike hits, the target must succeed at a DC 19 Fortitude save or become [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn.
Vanguard Training As soon as a caligni vanguard is old enough to hold a weapon, they're immersed in an intensive training regimen that emphasizes austerity, asceticism, and heavy armor prowess. Though initially painful, the fusion of their armor to their bodies serves as a reminder of their responsibilities, and they eventually grow accustomed to the sensation.
name: Caligni Vanguard

Although the mysterious demigods known as the Forsaken disappeared eons ago, many calignis continue to follow their ancient traditions of worship. Owbs, once the Forsaken's servants, still respond to caligni prayers. Caligni callers serve as the priests of their communities, calling upon these shadowy owb patrons for guidance, favors, and power. Their most important ceremony, the blanching, determines the potential of most newborn calignis and shapes their eventual forms. Due to their close ties to the malevolent owbs, most callers exhibit cruel and inscrutable natures. They often lead caligni enclaves alongside caligni stalkers as spiritual advisors. Callers tend to be highly superstitious, seeing omens everywhere, and they avoid revealing details of their rituals even to other calignis. **[Recall Knowledge - Humanoid](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)** ([Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=47)): DC 22 **[Unspecific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 20 **[Specific Lore](/Rules.aspx?ID=563)**: DC 17 [Caligni Caller](/Monsters.aspx?ID=4289) **Source** [Monster Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=276) pg. 65 **Perception** +11; [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=59), [light blindness](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=68) **Languages** [Caligni](/Languages.aspx?ID=28), [Sakvroth](/Languages.aspx?ID=118) **Skills** [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) +9, [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) +14, [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) +13, [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) +15 **Str** +2 **Dex** +5 **Con** +1 **Int** +1 **Wis** +1 **Cha** +4 **Items** [Dagger](/Weapons.aspx?ID=358) --- **AC** 24 **Fort** +9 **Ref** +15 **Will** +11 **HP** 80 **Death Umbra** ([darkness](/Traits.aspx?ID=569)) When the caller dies, an explosion of shadow devours their body. Each creature in a 10-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) must attempt a DC 22 Fortitude save.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 1 for 1 minute.
**Failure** The creature is enfeebled 2 and [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 for 1 minute. --- **Speed** 25 feet **Melee** dagger +15 ([Agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=602), [versatile S](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d4+4 piercing plus 1d6 void **Occult Innate Spells** DC 24, attack +16 - **Cantrips (3rd)** [Detect Magic](/Spells.aspx?ID=1485), [Void Warp](/Spells.aspx?ID=1745) - **2nd** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) (at will) - **3rd** [Chilling Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1464) (×2), [Grim Tendrils](/Spells.aspx?ID=1548) (×3) - **4th** [Darkness](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) - **5th** [Umbral Journey](/Spells.aspx?ID=1729) **Rituals** DC 24 - **3rd** [Owb Pact](/Rituals.aspx?ID=222) **Sneak Attack** The caller's Strikes deal an additional 2d6 precision damage to [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) creatures.
